en.21:47: We will set up the scales of justice on the Day of Judgment, so no soul will be wronged in the least. And ˹even˺ if a deed is the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as a ˹vigilant˺ Reckoner
en.21:48: Indeed, We granted Moses and Aaron the standard ˹to distinguish between right and wrong˺—a light and a reminder for the righteous
en.21:49: who are in awe of their Lord without seeing Him, and are fearful of the Hour
en.21:50: And this ˹Quran˺ is a blessed reminder which We have revealed. Will you ˹pagans˺ then deny it
en.21:51: And indeed, We had granted Abraham sound judgment early on, for We knew him well ˹to be worthy of it˺
en.21:52: ˹Remember˺ when he questioned his father and his people, “What are these statues to which you are so devoted?”
en.21:53: They replied, “We found our forefathers worshipping them.”
en.21:54: He responded, “Indeed, you and your forefathers have been clearly astray.”
en.21:55: They asked, “Have you come to us with the truth, or is this a joke?”
en.21:56: He replied, “In fact, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them ˹both˺. And to that I bear witness.”
en.21:57: ˹Then he said to himself,˺ “By Allah! I will surely plot against your idols after you have turned your backs and gone away.”
en.21:58: So he smashed them into pieces, except the biggest of them, so they might turn to it ˹for answers˺
en.21:59: They protested, “Who dared do this to our gods? It must be an evildoer!”
en.21:60: Some said, “We heard a young man, called Abraham, speaking ˹ill˺ of them.”
en.21:61: They demanded, “Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness ˹his trial˺.”
en.21:62: They asked, “Was it you who did this to our gods, O Abraham?”
en.21:63: He replied ˹sarcastically˺, “No, this one—the biggest of them—did it! So ask them, if they can talk!”
en.21:64: So they came back to their senses, saying ˹to one another˺, “You yourselves are truly the wrongdoers!”
en.21:65: Then they ˹quickly˺ regressed to their ˹original˺ mind-set, ˹arguing,˺ “You already know that those ˹idols˺ cannot talk.”
en.21:66: He rebuked ˹them˺, “Do you then worship—instead of Allah—what can neither benefit nor harm you in any way
en.21:67: Shame on you and whatever you worship instead of Allah! Do you not have any sense?”
en.21:68: They concluded, “Burn him up to avenge your gods, if you must act.”
en.21:69: We ordered, “O fire! Be cool and safe for Abraham!”
en.21:70: They had sought to harm him, but We made them the worst losers
en.21:71: Then We delivered him, along with Lot, to the land We had showered with blessings for all people
en.21:72: And We blessed him with Isaac ˹as a son˺ and Jacob ˹as a grandson˺, as an additional favour—making all of them righteous
en.21:73: We ˹also˺ made them leaders, guiding by Our command, and inspired them to do good deeds, establish prayer, and pay alms-tax. And they were devoted to Our worship
en.21:74: And to Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge, and delivered him from the society engrossed in shameful practices. They were certainly an evil, rebellious people
en.21:75: And We admitted him into Our mercy, ˹for˺ he was truly one of the righteous
en.21:76: And ˹remember˺ when Noah had cried out to Us earlier, so We responded to him and delivered him and his family from the great distress
en.21:77: And We made him prevail over those who had rejected Our signs. They were truly an evil people, so We drowned them all
en.21:78: And ˹remember˺ when David and Solomon passed judgment regarding the crops ruined ˹at night˺ by someone’s sheep, and We were witness to their judgments
en.21:79: We guided ˹young˺ Solomon to a fairer settlement, and granted each of them wisdom and knowledge. We subjected the mountains as well as the birds to hymn ˹Our praises˺ along with David. It is We Who did ˹it all˺
en.21:80: We taught him the art of making body armour to protect you in battle. Will you then be grateful
en.21:81: And to Solomon We subjected the raging winds, blowing by his command to the land We had showered with blessings. It is We Who know everything
en.21:82: And ˹We subjected˺ some jinn that dived for him, and performed other duties. It is We Who kept them in check
en.21:83: And ˹remember˺ when Job cried out to his Lord, “I have been touched with adversity, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.”
en.21:84: So We answered his prayer and removed his adversity, and gave him back his family, twice as many, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for the ˹devoted˺ worshippers
en.21:85: And ˹remember˺ Ishmael, Enoch, and Ⱬul-Kifl. They were all steadfast
en.21:86: We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were truly of the righteous
en.21:87: And ˹remember˺ when the Man of the Whale stormed off ˹from his city˺ in a rage, thinking We would not restrain him. Then in the ˹veils of˺ darkness he cried out, “There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except You. Glory be to You! I have certainly done wrong.”
en.21:88: So We answered his prayer and rescued him from anguish. And so do We save the ˹true˺ believers
en.21:89: And ˹remember˺ when Zachariah cried out to his Lord, “My Lord! Do not leave me childless, though You are the Best of Successors.”
en.21:90: So We answered his prayer, granted him John, and made his wife fertile. Indeed, they used to race in doing good, and call upon Us with hope and fear, totally humbling themselves before Us
en.21:91: And ˹remember˺ the one who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her through Our angel, ˹Gabriel,˺ making her and her son a sign for all peoples
en.21:92: ˹O prophets!˺ Indeed, this religion of yours is ˹only˺ one, and I am your Lord, so worship Me ˹alone˺
en.21:93: Yet the people have divided it into sects. But to Us they will all return
en.21:94: So whoever does good and is a believer will never be denied ˹the reward for˺ their striving, for We are recording it all
en.21:95: It is impossible for a society which We have destroyed to ever rise again
en.21:96: until ˹after˺ Gog and Magog have broken loose ˹from the barrier˺, swarming down from every hill
en.21:97: ushering in the True Promise. Then—behold!—the disbelievers will stare ˹in horror, crying,˺ “Oh, woe to us! We have truly been heedless of this. In fact, we have been wrongdoers.”
en.21:98: Certainly you ˹disbelievers˺ and whatever you worship instead of Allah will be the fuel of Hell. You are ˹all˺ bound to enter it
en.21:99: Had those idols been ˹true˺ gods, they would not have entered it. And they will be there forever
en.21:100: In it they will groan, and will not be able to hear
en.21:101: Surely those for whom We have destined the finest reward will be kept far away from Hell
en.21:102: not even hearing the slightest hissing from it. And they will delight forever in what their souls desire
en.21:103: The Supreme Horror ˹of that Day˺ will not disturb them, and the angels will greet them, ˹saying,˺ “This is your Day, which you have been promised.”
en.21:104: On that Day We will roll up the heavens like a scroll of writings. Just as We produced the first creation, ˹so˺ shall We reproduce it. That is a promise binding on Us. We truly uphold ˹Our promises˺
en.21:105: Surely, following the ˹heavenly˺ Record, We decreed in the Scriptures: “My righteous servants shall inherit the land.”
en.21:106: Surely this ˹Quran˺ is sufficient ˹as a reminder˺ for those devoted to worship
en.21:107: We have sent you ˹O Prophet˺ only as a mercy for the whole world
en.21:108: Say, “What has been revealed to me is this: ‘Your God is only One God.’ Will you then submit?”
en.21:109: If they turn away, then say, “I have warned you all equally. I do not know if what you are threatened with is near or far
en.21:110: Allah surely knows what you say openly and whatever you hide
en.21:111: I do not know if this ˹delay˺ is possibly a test for you and an enjoyment for a while.”
en.21:112: ˹In the end,˺ the Prophet said, “My Lord! Judge ˹between us˺ in truth. And our Lord is the Most Compassionate, Whose help is sought against what you claim.”
en.22:1: O humanity! Fear your Lord, for the ˹violent˺ quaking at the Hour is surely a dreadful thing
en.22:2: The Day you see it, every nursing mother will abandon what she is nursing, and every pregnant woman will deliver her burden ˹prematurely˺. And you will see people ˹as if they were˺ drunk, though they will not be drunk; but the torment of Allah is ˹terribly˺ severe
en.22:3: ˹Still˺ there are some who dispute about Allah without knowledge, and follow every rebellious devil
en.22:4: It has been decreed for such devils that whoever takes them as a guide will be misguided and led by them into the torment of the Blaze
en.22:5: O humanity! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, then ˹know that˺ We did create you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then ˹developed you into˺ a clinging clot ˹of blood˺, then a lump of flesh—fully formed or unformed—in order to demonstrate ˹Our power˺ to you. ˹Then˺ We settle whatever ˹embryo˺ We will in the womb for an appointed term, then bring you forth as infants, so that you may reach your prime. Some of you ˹may˺ die ˹young˺, while others are left to reach the most feeble stage of life so that they may know nothing after having known much. And you see the earth lifeless, but as soon as We send down rain upon it, it begins to stir ˹to life˺ and swell, producing every type of pleasant plant
en.22:6: That is because Allah ˹alone˺ is the Truth, He ˹alone˺ gives life to the dead, and He ˹alone˺ is Most Capable of everything
en.22:7: And certainly the Hour is coming, there is no doubt about it. And Allah will surely resurrect those in the graves
en.22:8: ˹Still˺ there are some who dispute about Allah without knowledge, guidance, or an enlightening scripture
en.22:9: turning away ˹in pride˺ to lead ˹others˺ astray from Allah’s Way. They will suffer disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Judgment We will make them taste the torment of burning
en.22:10: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is ˹the reward˺ for what your hands have done. And Allah is never unjust to ˹His˺ creation.”
en.22:11: And there are some who worship Allah on the verge ˹of faith˺: if they are blessed with something good, they are content with it; but if they are afflicted with a trial, they relapse ˹into disbelief˺, losing this world and the Hereafter. That is ˹truly˺ the clearest loss
en.22:12: They call besides Allah what can neither harm nor benefit them. That is ˹truly˺ the farthest one can stray
en.22:13: They invoke those whose worship leads to harm, not benefit. What an evil patron and what an evil associate
en.22:14: Indeed, Allah will admit those who believe and do good into Gardens, under which rivers flow. Surely Allah does what He wills
en.22:15: Whoever thinks that Allah will not help His Prophet in this world and the Hereafter, let them stretch out a rope to the ceiling and strangle themselves, then let them see if this plan will do away with ˹the cause of˺ their rage
en.22:16: And so We revealed this ˹Quran˺ as clear verses. And Allah certainly guides whoever He wills
en.22:17: Indeed, the believers, Jews, Sabians, Christians, Magi, and the polytheists—Allah will judge between them ˹all˺ on Judgment Day. Surely Allah is a Witness over all things
en.22:18: Do you not see that to Allah bow down ˹in submission˺ all those in the heavens and all those on the earth, as well as the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, and ˹all˺ living beings, as well as many humans, while many are deserving of punishment. And whoever Allah disgraces, none can honour. Surely Allah does what He wills
en.22:19: These are two opposing groups that disagree about their Lord: as for the disbelievers, garments of Fire will be cut out for them and boiling water will be poured over their heads
en.22:20: melting whatever is in their bellies, along with their skin
en.22:21: And awaiting them are maces of iron
en.22:22: Whenever they try to escape from Hell—out of anguish—they will be forced back into it, ˹and will be told,˺ “Taste the torment of burning!”
en.22:23: ˹But˺ Allah will surely admit those who believe and do good into Gardens, under which rivers flow, where they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing will be silk
en.22:24: for they have been guided to the best of speech, and they have been guided to the Commendable Path
en.22:25: Indeed, those who persist in disbelief and hinder ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah and from the Sacred Mosque—which We have appointed for all people, residents and visitors alike—along with whoever intends to deviate by doing wrong in it, We will cause them to taste a painful punishment
en.22:26: And ˹remember˺ when We assigned to Abraham the site of the House, ˹saying,˺ “Do not associate anything with Me ˹in worship˺ and purify My House for those who circle ˹the Ka’bah˺, stand ˹in prayer˺, and bow and prostrate themselves
en.22:27: Call ˹all˺ people to the pilgrimage. They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel from every distant path
en.22:28: so they may obtain the benefits ˹in store˺ for them, and pronounce the Name of Allah on appointed days over the sacrificial animals He has provided for them. So eat from their meat and feed the desperately poor
en.22:29: Then let them groom themselves, fulfil their vows, and circle the Ancient House.”
en.22:30: That is so. And whoever honours the rituals of Allah, it is best for them in the sight of their Lord. The ˹meat of˺ cattle has been made lawful for you, except what has ˹already˺ been recited to you. So shun the impurity of idolatry, and shun words of falsehood
en.22:31: Be upright ˹in devotion˺ to Allah, associating none with Him ˹in worship˺. For whoever associates ˹others˺ with Allah is like someone who has fallen from the sky and is either snatched away by birds or swept by the wind to a remote place
en.22:32: That is so. And whoever honours the symbols of Allah, it is certainly out of the piety of the heart
en.22:33: You may benefit from sacrificial animals for an appointed term, then their place of sacrifice is at the Ancient House
en.22:34: For every community We appointed a rite of sacrifice so that they may pronounce the Name of Allah over the sacrificial animals He has provided for them. For your God is only One God, so submit yourselves to Him ˹alone˺. And give good news ˹O Prophet˺ to the humble
en.22:35: those whose hearts tremble at the remembrance of Allah, who patiently endure whatever may befall them, and who establish prayer and donate from what We have provided for them
en.22:36: We have made sacrificial camels ˹and cattle˺ among the symbols of Allah, in which there is ˹much˺ good for you. So pronounce the Name of Allah over them when they are lined up ˹for sacrifice˺. Once they have fallen ˹lifeless˺ on their sides, you may eat from their meat, and feed the needy—those who do not beg, and those who do. In this way We have subjected these ˹animals˺ to you so that you may be grateful
en.22:37: Neither their meat nor blood reaches Allah. Rather, it is your piety that reaches Him. This is how He has subjected them to you so that you may proclaim the greatness of Allah for what He has guided you to, and give good news to the good-doers
en.22:38: Indeed, Allah defends those who believe. Surely Allah does not like whoever is deceitful, ungrateful
en.22:39: Permission ˹to fight back˺ is ˹hereby˺ granted to those being fought, for they have been wronged. And Allah is truly Most Capable of helping them ˹prevail˺
en.22:40: ˹They are˺ those who have been expelled from their homes for no reason other than proclaiming: “Our Lord is Allah.” Had Allah not repelled ˹the aggression of˺ some people by means of others, destruction would have surely claimed monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques in which Allah’s Name is often mentioned. Allah will certainly help those who stand up for Him. Allah is truly All-Powerful, Almighty
en.22:41: ˹They are˺ those who, if established in the land by Us, would perform prayer, pay alms-tax, encourage what is good, and forbid what is evil. And with Allah rests the outcome of all affairs
en.22:42: If they deny you ˹O Prophet˺, so did the people of Noah before them, as well as ˹the tribes of˺ ’Ȃd and Thamûd
en.22:43: the people of Abraham, the people of Lot
en.22:44: and the residents of Midian. And Moses was denied ˹too˺. But I delayed ˹the fate of˺ the disbelievers ˹until their appointed time˺ then seized them. And how severe was My response
en.22:45: Many are the societies We have destroyed for persisting in wrongdoing, leaving them in total ruin. ˹Many are˺ also the abandoned wells and lofty palaces
en.22:46: Have they not travelled throughout the land so their hearts may reason, and their ears may listen? Indeed, it is not the eyes that are blind, but it is the hearts in the chests that grow blind
en.22:47: They challenge you ˹O Prophet˺ to hasten the torment. And Allah will never fail in His promise. But a day with your Lord is indeed like a thousand years by your counting
en.22:48: Many are the societies whose end We delayed while they did wrong, then seized them. And to Me is the final return
en.22:49: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “O humanity! I am only sent to you with a clear warning
en.22:50: So those who believe and do good will have forgiveness and an honourable provision
en.22:51: But those who strive to discredit Our revelations, they will be the residents of the Hellfire.”
en.22:52: Whenever We sent a messenger or a prophet before you ˹O Prophet˺ and he recited ˹Our revelations˺, Satan would influence ˹people’s understanding of˺ his recitation. But ˹eventually˺ Allah would eliminate Satan’s influence. Then Allah would ˹firmly˺ establish His revelations. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.22:53: All that so He may make Satan’s influence a trial for those ˹hypocrites˺ whose hearts are sick and those ˹disbelievers˺ whose hearts are hardened. Surely the wrongdoers are totally engrossed in opposition
en.22:54: ˹This is˺ also so that those gifted with knowledge would know that this ˹revelation˺ is the truth from your Lord, so they have faith in it, and so their hearts would submit humbly to it. And Allah surely guides the believers to the Straight Path
en.22:55: Yet the disbelievers will persist in doubt about this ˹revelation˺ until the Hour takes them by surprise, or the torment of a terminating Day comes to them
en.22:56: All authority on that Day is for Allah ˹alone˺. He will judge between them. So those who believe and do good will be in the Gardens of Bliss
en.22:57: But those who disbelieve and deny Our revelations, it is they who will suffer a humiliating punishment
en.22:58: As for those who emigrate in the cause of Allah and then are martyred or die, Allah will indeed grant them a good provision. Surely Allah is the Best Provider
en.22:59: He will certainly admit them into a place they will be pleased with. For Allah is truly All-Knowing, Most Forbearing
en.22:60: That is so. And whoever retaliates in equivalence to the injury they have received, and then are wronged ˹again˺, Allah will certainly help them. Surely Allah is Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving
en.22:61: That is because Allah causes the night to merge into the day, and the day into the night. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing
en.22:62: That is because Allah ˹alone˺ is the Truth and what they invoke besides Him is falsehood, and Allah ˹alone˺ is truly the Most High, All-Great
en.22:63: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky, then the earth becomes green? Surely Allah is Most Subtle, All-Aware
en.22:64: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Allah ˹alone˺ is truly the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy
en.22:65: Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you whatever is in the earth as well as the ships ˹that˺ sail through the sea by His command? He keeps the sky from falling down on the earth except by His permission. Surely Allah is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to humanity
en.22:66: And He is the One Who gave you life, then will cause you to die, and then will bring you back to life. ˹But˺ surely humankind is ever ungrateful
en.22:67: For every community We appointed a code of life to follow. So do not let them dispute with you ˹O Prophet˺ in this matter. And invite ˹all˺ to your Lord, for you are truly on the Right Guidance
en.22:68: But if they argue with you, then say, “Allah knows best what you do.”
en.22:69: Allah will judge between you ˹all˺ on Judgment Day regarding your differences
en.22:70: Do you not know that Allah ˹fully˺ knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth? Surely it is all ˹written˺ in a Record. That is certainly easy for Allah
en.22:71: Yet they worship besides Allah that for which He has sent down no authority, and of which they have no knowledge. The wrongdoers will have no helper
en.22:72: Whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, you ˹O Prophet˺ recognize rage on the faces of the disbelievers, as if they are going to snap at those who recite Our revelations to them. Say, “Shall I inform you of something far more enraging than that? ˹It is˺ the Fire with which Allah has threatened those who disbelieve. What an evil destination!”
en.22:73: O humanity! A lesson is set forth, so listen to it ˹carefully˺: those ˹idols˺ you invoke besides Allah can never create ˹so much as˺ a fly, even if they ˹all˺ were to come together for that. And if a fly were to snatch anything away from them, they cannot ˹even˺ retrieve it from the fly. How powerless are those who invoke and those invoked
en.22:74: They have not shown Allah the reverence He deserves. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty
en.22:75: Allah selects messengers from both angels and people, for Allah is truly All-Hearing, All-Seeing
en.22:76: He knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them. And to Allah ˹all˺ matters will be returned ˹for judgment˺
en.22:77: O believers! Bow down, prostrate yourselves, worship your Lord, and do ˹what is˺ good so that you may be successful
en.22:78: Strive for ˹the cause of˺ Allah in the way He deserves, for ˹it is˺ He ˹Who˺ has chosen you, and laid upon you no hardship in the religion—the way of your forefather Abraham. ˹It is Allah˺ Who named you ‘the ones who submit’ ˹in the˺ earlier ˹Scriptures˺ and in this ˹Quran˺, so that the Messenger may be a witness over you, and that you may be witnesses over humanity. So establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and hold fast to Allah. He ˹alone˺ is your Guardian. What an excellent Guardian, and what an excellent Helper
en.23:2: those who humble themselves in prayer
en.23:6: except with their wives or those ˹bondwomen˺ in their possession, for then they are free from blame
en.23:7: but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors
en.23:8: ˹the believers are also˺ those who are true to their trusts and covenants
en.23:9: and those who are ˹properly˺ observant of their prayers
en.23:10: These are the ones who will be awarded
en.23:11: Paradise as their own. They will be there forever
en.23:12: And indeed, We created humankind from an extract of clay
en.23:13: then placed each ˹human˺ as a sperm-drop in a secure place
en.23:14: then We developed the drop into a clinging clot ˹of blood˺, then developed the clot into a lump ˹of flesh˺, then developed the lump into bones, then clothed the bones with flesh, then We brought it into being as a new creation. So Blessed is Allah, the Best of Creators
en.23:15: After that you will surely die
en.23:16: then on the Day of Judgment you will be resurrected
en.23:17: And indeed, We created above you seven levels ˹of heaven˺. We are never unmindful of ˹Our˺ creation
en.23:18: We send down rain from the sky in perfect measure, causing it to soak into the earth. And We are surely able to take it away
en.23:19: With it We produce for you gardens of palm trees and grapevines, in which there are abundant fruits, and from which you may eat
en.23:20: as well as ˹olive˺ trees which grow at Mount Sinai, providing oil and a condiment to eat
en.23:21: And there is certainly a lesson for you in cattle, from whose bellies We give you ˹milk˺ to drink, and in them are many other benefits for you, and from them you may eat
en.23:22: And you are carried upon ˹some of˺ them and upon ships
en.23:23: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people. He declared, “O my people! Worship Allah ˹alone˺. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear ˹Him˺?”
en.23:24: But the disbelieving chiefs of his people said ˹to the masses˺, “This is only a human like you, who wants to be superior to you. Had Allah willed, He could have easily sent down angels ˹instead˺. We have never heard of this in ˹the history of˺ our forefathers
en.23:25: He is simply insane, so bear with him for a while.”
en.23:26: Noah prayed, “My Lord! Help me, because they have denied ˹me˺.”
en.23:27: So We inspired him: “Build the Ark under Our ˹watchful˺ Eyes and directions. Then when Our command comes and the oven bursts ˹with water˺, take on board a pair from every species along with your family—except those against whom the decree ˹to drown˺ has already been passed. And do not plead with Me for those who have done wrong, for they will surely be drowned.”
en.23:28: Then when you and those with you have settled in the Ark, say, “All praise is for Allah, Who saved us from the wrongdoing people.”
en.23:29: And pray, “My Lord! Allow me a blessed landing, for You are the best accommodator.”
en.23:30: Surely in this are lessons. And We ˹always˺ put ˹people˺ to the test
en.23:31: Then We raised another generation after them
en.23:32: and sent to them a messenger from among themselves, ˹declaring,˺ “Worship Allah ˹alone˺. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear ˹Him˺?”
en.23:33: But the chiefs of his people—who disbelieved, denied the meeting ˹with Allah˺ in the Hereafter, and were spoiled by the worldly luxuries We had provided for them—said ˹to the masses˺, “This is only a human like you. He eats what you eat, and drinks what you drink
en.23:34: And if you ˹ever˺ obey a human like yourselves, then you would certainly be losers
en.23:35: Does he promise you that once you are dead and reduced to dust and bones, you will be brought forth ˹alive˺
en.23:36: Impossible, simply impossible is what you are promised
en.23:37: There is nothing beyond our worldly life. We die, others are born, and none will be resurrected
en.23:38: He is no more than a man who has fabricated a lie about Allah, and we will never believe in him.”
en.23:39: The messenger prayed, “My Lord! Help me, because they have denied ˹me˺.”
en.23:40: Allah responded, “Soon they will be truly regretful.”
en.23:41: Then the ˹mighty˺ blast overtook them with justice, and We reduced them to rubble. So away with the wrongdoing people
en.23:42: Then We raised other generations after them
en.23:43: No people can advance their doom, nor can they delay it
en.23:44: Then We sent Our messengers in succession: whenever a messenger came to his people, they denied him. So We destroyed them, one after the other, reducing them to ˹cautionary˺ tales. So away with the people who refuse to believe
en.23:45: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs and compelling proof
en.23:46: to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they behaved arrogantly and were a tyrannical people
en.23:47: They argued, “Will we believe in two humans, like ourselves, whose people are slaves to us?”
en.23:48: So they rejected them both, and ˹so˺ were among those destroyed
en.23:49: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture, so perhaps his people would be ˹rightly˺ guided
en.23:50: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, and gave them refuge on high ground—a ˹suitable˺ place for rest with flowing water
en.23:51: O messengers! Eat from what is good and lawful, and act righteously. Indeed, I fully know what you do
en.23:52: Surely this religion of yours is ˹only˺ one, and I am your Lord, so fear Me ˹alone˺
en.23:53: Yet the people have divided it into different sects, each rejoicing in what they have
en.23:54: So leave them ˹O Prophet˺ in their heedlessness for a while
en.23:55: Do they think, since We provide them with wealth and children
en.23:56: that We hasten to ˹honour˺ them ˹with˺ all kinds of good? No! They are not aware
en.23:57: Surely those who tremble in awe of their Lord
en.23:58: and who believe in the revelations of their Lord
en.23:59: and who associate none with their Lord
en.23:60: and who do whatever ˹good˺ they do with their hearts fearful, ˹knowing˺ that they will return to their Lord—
en.23:61: it is they who race to do good deeds, always taking the lead
en.23:62: We never require of any soul more than what it can afford. And with Us is a record which speaks the truth. None will be wronged
en.23:63: But the hearts of those ˹who disbelieve˺ are oblivious to ˹all of˺ this, and they have other ˹evil˺ deeds, opposite to this, in which they are engrossed
en.23:64: But as soon as We seize their elite with torment, they start to cry for help
en.23:65: ˹They will be told,˺ “Do not cry for help today. Surely you will never be saved from Us
en.23:66: Indeed, My revelations were recited to you, but you used to back away ˹in disgust˺
en.23:67: boasting of the Sacred House, and babbling ˹nonsense about the Quran˺ by night.”
en.23:68: Is it because they have never contemplated the Word ˹of Allah˺? Or ˹because˺ there has come to them something that did not come to their forefathers
en.23:69: Or ˹because˺ they failed to recognize their Messenger, and so they denied him
en.23:70: Or ˹because˺ they say, “He is insane?” In fact, he has come to them with the truth, but most of them are resentful of the truth
en.23:71: Had the truth followed their desires, the heavens, the earth, and all those in them would have certainly been corrupted. In fact, We have brought them ˹the means to˺ their glory, but they turn away from it
en.23:72: Or ˹is it because˺ you ˹O Prophet˺ are asking them for tribute? But the reward of your Lord is best, for He is the Best Provider
en.23:73: And surely you are calling them to the Straight Path
en.23:74: but those who disbelieve in the Hereafter are certainly deviating from that Path
en.23:75: ˹Even˺ if We had mercy on them and removed their affliction, they would still persist in their transgression, wandering blindly
en.23:76: And We have already seized them with torment, but they never humbled themselves to their Lord, nor did they ˹submissively˺ appeal ˹to Him˺
en.23:77: But as soon as We open for them a gate of severe punishment, they will be utterly desperate
en.23:78: He is the One Who created for you hearing, sight, and intellect. ˹Yet˺ you hardly give any thanks
en.23:79: And He is the One Who has dispersed you ˹all˺ over the earth, and to Him you will ˹all˺ be gathered
en.23:80: And He is the One Who gives life and causes death, and to Him belongs the alternation of the day and night. Will you not then understand
en.23:81: But they ˹just˺ say what their predecessors said
en.23:82: They said, “Once we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected
en.23:83: We have already been promised this, as well as our forefathers earlier. This is nothing but ancient fables!”
en.23:84: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “To whom belong the earth and all those on it, if you ˹really˺ know?”
en.23:85: They will reply, “To Allah!” Say, “Why are you not then mindful?”
en.23:86: ˹And˺ ask ˹them˺, “Who is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the Mighty Throne?”
en.23:87: They will reply, “Allah.” Say, “Will you not then fear ˹Him˺?”
en.23:88: Ask ˹them also,˺ “In Whose Hands is the authority over all things, protecting ˹all˺ while none can protect against Him, if you ˹really˺ know?”
en.23:89: They will reply, “Allah.” Say, “How are you then so deluded?”
en.23:90: In fact, We have brought them the truth, and they are certainly liars
en.23:91: Allah has never had ˹any˺ offspring, nor is there any god besides Him. Otherwise, each god would have taken away what he created, and they would have tried to dominate one another. Glorified is Allah above what they claim
en.23:92: ˹He is the˺ Knower of the seen and unseen. Exalted is He above what they associate ˹with Him˺
en.23:93: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “My Lord! Should You show me what they are threatened with
en.23:94: then, my Lord, do not count me among the wrongdoing people.”
en.23:95: We are indeed able to show you what We have threatened them with
en.23:96: Respond to evil with what is best. We know well what they claim
en.23:97: And say, “My Lord! I seek refuge in You from the temptations of the devils
en.23:98: And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, that they ˹even˺ come near me.”
en.23:99: When death approaches any of them, they cry, “My Lord! Let me go back
en.23:100: so I may do good in what I left behind.” Never! It is only a ˹useless˺ appeal they make. And there is a barrier behind them until the Day they are resurrected
en.23:101: Then, when the Trumpet will be blown, there will be no kinship between them on that Day, nor will they ˹even care to˺ ask about one another
en.23:102: As for those whose scale is heavy ˹with good deeds˺, it is they who will be successful
en.23:103: But those whose scale is light, they will have doomed themselves, staying in Hell forever
en.23:104: The Fire will burn their faces, leaving them deformed
en.23:105: ˹It will be said,˺ “Were My revelations not recited to you, but you used to deny them?”
en.23:106: They will cry, “Our Lord! Our ill-fate took hold of us, so we became a misguided people
en.23:107: Our Lord! Take us out of this ˹Fire˺. Then if we ever return ˹to denial˺, we will truly be wrongdoers.”
en.23:108: Allah will respond, “Be despised in there! Do not ˹ever˺ plead with Me ˹again˺
en.23:109: Indeed, there was a group of My servants who used to pray, ‘Our Lord! We have believed, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of those who show mercy,’
en.23:110: but you were ˹so busy˺ making fun of them that it made you forget My remembrance. And you used to laugh at them
en.23:111: Today I have indeed rewarded them for their perseverance: they are certainly the triumphant.”
en.23:112: He will ask ˹them˺, “How many years did you remain on earth?”
en.23:113: They will reply, “We remained ˹only˺ a day or part of a day. But ask those who kept count.”
en.23:114: He will say, “You only remained for a little while, if only you knew
en.23:115: Did you then think that We had created you without purpose, and that you would never be returned to Us?”
en.23:116: Exalted is Allah, the True King! There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him, the Lord of the Honourable Throne
en.23:117: Whoever invokes, besides Allah, another god—for which they can have no proof—they will surely find their penalty with their Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will never succeed
en.23:118: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the best of those who show mercy.”
en.24:1: ˹This is˺ a sûrah which We have revealed and made ˹its rulings˺ obligatory, and revealed in it clear commandments so that you may be mindful
en.24:2: As for female and male fornicators, give each of them one hundred lashes, and do not let pity for them make you lenient in ˹enforcing˺ the law of Allah, if you ˹truly˺ believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a number of believers witness their punishment
en.24:3: A male fornicator would only marry a female fornicator or idolatress. And a female fornicator would only be married to a fornicator or idolater. This is ˹all˺ forbidden to the believers
en.24:4: Those who accuse chaste women ˹of adultery˺ and fail to produce four witnesses, give them eighty lashes ˹each˺. And do not ever accept any testimony from them—for they are indeed the rebellious—
en.24:5: except those who repent afterwards and mend their ways, then surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.24:6: And those who accuse their wives ˹of adultery˺ but have no witness except themselves, the accuser must testify, swearing four times by Allah that he is telling the truth
en.24:7: and a fifth oath that Allah may condemn him if he is lying
en.24:8: For her to be spared the punishment, she must swear four times by Allah that he is telling a lie
en.24:9: and a fifth oath that Allah may be displeased with her if he is telling the truth
en.24:10: ˹You would have suffered,˺ had it not been for Allah’s grace and mercy upon you, and had Allah not been Accepting of Repentance, All-Wise
en.24:11: Indeed, those who came up with that ˹outrageous˺ slander are a group of you. Do not think this is bad for you. Rather, it is good for you. They will be punished, each according to their share of the sin. As for their mastermind, he will suffer a tremendous punishment
en.24:12: If only the believing men and women had thought well of one another, when you heard this ˹rumour˺, and said, “This is clearly ˹an outrageous˺ slander!”
en.24:13: Why did they not produce four witnesses? Now, since they have failed to produce witnesses, they are ˹truly˺ liars in the sight of Allah
en.24:14: Had it not been for Allah’s grace and mercy upon you in this world and the Hereafter, you would have certainly been touched with a tremendous punishment for what you plunged into—
en.24:15: when you passed it from one tongue to the other, and said with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, taking it lightly while it is ˹extremely˺ serious in the sight of Allah
en.24:16: If only you had said upon hearing it, “How can we speak about such a thing! Glory be to You ˹O Lord˺! This is a heinous slander!”
en.24:17: Allah forbids you from ever doing something like this again, if you are ˹true˺ believers
en.24:18: And Allah makes ˹His˺ commandments clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.24:19: Indeed, those who love to see indecency spread among the believers will suffer a painful punishment in this life and the Hereafter. Allah knows and you do not know
en.24:20: ˹You would have suffered,˺ had it not been for Allah’s grace and mercy upon you, and had Allah not been Ever Gracious, Most Merciful
en.24:21: O believers! Do not follow the footsteps of Satan. Whoever follows Satan’s footsteps, then ˹let them know that˺ he surely bids ˹all to˺ immorality and wickedness. Had it not been for Allah’s grace and mercy upon you, none of you would have ever been purified. But Allah purifies whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing
en.24:22: Do not let the people of virtue and affluence among you swear to suspend donations to their relatives, the needy, and the emigrants in the cause of Allah. Let them pardon and forgive. Do you not love to be forgiven by Allah? And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.24:23: Surely those who accuse chaste, unsuspecting, believing women are cursed in this life and the Hereafter. And they will suffer a tremendous punishment
en.24:24: on the Day their tongues, hands, and feet will testify against them for what they used to do
en.24:25: On that Day, Allah will give them their just penalty in full, and they will ˹come to˺ know that Allah ˹alone˺ is the Ultimate Truth
en.24:26: Wicked women are for wicked men, and wicked men are for wicked women. And virtuous women are for virtuous men, and virtuous men are for virtuous women. The virtuous are innocent of what the wicked say. They will have forgiveness and an honourable provision
en.24:27: O believers! Do not enter any house other than your own until you have asked for permission and greeted its occupants. This is best for you, so perhaps you will be mindful
en.24:28: If you find no one at home, do not enter it until you have been given permission. And if you are asked to leave, then leave. That is purer for you. And Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of what you do
en.24:29: There is no blame on you if you enter public places where there is something of benefit for you. And Allah knows what you reveal and what you conceal
en.24:30: ˹O Prophet!˺ Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their chastity. That is purer for them. Surely Allah is All-Aware of what they do
en.24:31: And tell the believing women to lower their gaze and guard their chastity, and not to reveal their adornments except what normally appears. Let them draw their veils over their chests, and not reveal their ˹hidden˺ adornments except to their husbands, their fathers, their fathers-in-law, their sons, their stepsons, their brothers, their brothers’ sons or sisters’ sons, their fellow women, those ˹bondwomen˺ in their possession, male attendants with no desire, or children who are still unaware of women’s nakedness. Let them not stomp their feet, drawing attention to their hidden adornments. Turn to Allah in repentance all together, O believers, so that you may be successful
en.24:32: Marry off the ˹free˺ singles among you, as well as the righteous of your bondmen and bondwomen. If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His bounty. For Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing
en.24:33: And let those who do not have the means to marry keep themselves chaste until Allah enriches them out of His bounty. And if any of those ˹bondspeople˺ in your possession desires a deed of emancipation, make it possible for them, if you find goodness in them. And give them some of Allah’s wealth which He has granted you. Do not force your ˹slave˺ girls into prostitution for your own worldly gains while they wish to remain chaste. And if someone coerces them, then after such a coercion Allah is certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful ˹to them˺
en.24:34: Indeed, We have sent down to you clear revelations, along with examples of those who had gone before you, and a lesson to the God-fearing
en.24:35: Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His light is like a niche in which there is a lamp, the lamp is in a crystal, the crystal is like a shining star, lit from ˹the oil of˺ a blessed olive tree, ˹located˺ neither to the east nor the west, whose oil would almost glow, even without being touched by fire. Light upon light! Allah guides whoever He wills to His light. And Allah sets forth parables for humanity. For Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
en.24:36: ˹That light shines˺ through houses ˹of worship˺ which Allah has ordered to be raised, and where His Name is mentioned. He is glorified there morning and evening
en.24:37: by men who are not distracted—either by buying or selling—from Allah’s remembrance, or performing prayer, or paying alms-tax. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will tremble
en.24:38: ˹hoping˺ that Allah may reward them according to the best of their deeds, and increase them out of His grace. And Allah provides for whoever He wills without limit
en.24:39: As for the disbelievers, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert, which the thirsty perceive as water, but when they approach it, they find it to be nothing. Instead, they find Allah there ˹in the Hereafter, ready˺ to settle their account. And Allah is swift in reckoning
en.24:40: Or ˹their deeds are˺ like the darkness in a deep sea, covered by waves upon waves, topped by ˹dark˺ clouds. Darkness upon darkness! If one stretches out their hand, they can hardly see it. And whoever Allah does not bless with light will have no light
en.24:41: Do you not see that Allah is glorified by all those in the heavens and the earth, even the birds as they soar? Each ˹instinctively˺ knows their manner of prayer and glorification. And Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all they do
en.24:42: To Allah ˹alone˺ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allah is the final return
en.24:43: Do you not see that Allah gently drives the clouds, then joins them together, piling them up into masses, from which you see raindrops come forth? And He sends down from the sky mountains ˹of clouds˺ loaded with hail, pouring it on whoever He wills and averting it from whoever He wills. The flash of the clouds’ lightning nearly takes away eyesight
en.24:44: Allah alternates the day and night. Surely in this is a lesson for people of insight
en.24:45: And Allah has created from water every living creature. Some of them crawl on their bellies, some walk on two legs, and some walk on four. Allah creates whatever He wills. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything
en.24:46: We have indeed sent down revelations clarifying ˹the truth˺. But Allah ˹only˺ guides whoever He wills to the Straight Path
en.24:47: And the hypocrites say, “We believe in Allah and the Messenger, and we obey.” Then a group of them turns away soon after that. These are not ˹true˺ believers
en.24:48: And as soon as they are called to Allah and His Messenger so he may judge between them, a group of them turns away
en.24:49: But if the truth is in their favour, they come to him, fully submitting
en.24:50: Is there a sickness in their hearts? Or are they in doubt? Or do they fear that Allah and His Messenger will be unjust to them? In fact, it is they who are the ˹true˺ wrongdoers
en.24:51: The only response of the ˹true˺ believers, when they are called to Allah and His Messenger so he may judge between them, is to say, “We hear and obey.” It is they who will ˹truly˺ succeed
en.24:52: For whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, and fears Allah and is mindful of Him, then it is they who will ˹truly˺ triumph
en.24:53: They swear by Allah their most solemn oaths that if you ˹O Prophet˺ were to command them, they would certainly march forth ˹in Allah’s cause˺. Say, “˹You˺ do not ˹have to˺ swear; your obedience is well known!” Surely Allah is All-Aware of what you do.”
en.24:54: Say, “Obey Allah and obey the Messenger. But if you turn away, then he is only responsible for his duty and you are responsible for yours. And if you obey him, you will be ˹rightly˺ guided. The Messenger’s duty is only to deliver ˹the message˺ clearly.”
en.24:55: Allah has promised those of you who believe and do good that He will certainly make them successors in the land, as He did with those before them; and will surely establish for them their faith which He has chosen for them; and will indeed change their fear into security—˹provided that˺ they worship Me, associating nothing with Me. But whoever disbelieves after this ˹promise˺, it is they who will be the rebellious
en.24:56: Moreover, establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and obey the Messenger, so you may be shown mercy
en.24:57: Do not think ˹O Prophet˺ that the disbelievers can escape in the land. The Fire will be their home. Indeed, what an evil destination
en.24:58: O believers! Let those ˹bondspeople˺ in your possession and those of you who are still under age ask for your permission ˹to come in˺ at three times: before dawn prayer, when you take off your ˹outer˺ clothes at noon, and after the late evening prayer. ˹These are˺ three times of privacy for you. Other than these times, there is no blame on you or them to move freely, attending to one another. This is how Allah makes the revelations clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.24:59: And when your children reach the age of puberty, let them seek permission ˹to come in˺, as their seniors do. This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.24:60: As for elderly women past the age of marriage, there is no blame on them if they take off their ˹outer˺ garments, without revealing their adornments. But it is better for them if they avoid this ˹altogether˺. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing
en.24:61: There is no restriction on the blind, or the disabled, or the sick. Nor on yourselves if you eat from your homes, or the homes of your fathers, or your mothers, or your brothers, or your sisters, or your paternal uncles, or your paternal aunts, or your maternal uncles, or your maternal aunts, or from the homes in your trust, or ˹the homes of˺ your friends. There is no blame on you eating together or separately. However, when you enter houses, greet one another with a greeting ˹of peace˺ from Allah, blessed and good. This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so perhaps you will understand
en.24:62: The ˹true˺ believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, and when they are with him on a public matter, they do not leave without his permission. Indeed, those who ask your permission ˹O Prophet˺ are the ones who ˹truly˺ believe in Allah and His Messenger. So when they ask your permission for a private matter, grant permission to whoever you wish and ask Allah’s forgiveness for them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.24:63: Do not treat the Messenger’s summons to you ˹as lightly˺ as your summons to one another. Allah certainly knows those of you who slip away, hiding behind others. So let those who disobey his orders beware, for an affliction may befall them, or a painful torment may overtake them
en.24:64: Surely to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. He knows well what you stand for. And ˹on˺ the Day all will be returned to Him, He will inform them of what they did. For Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
en.25:1: Blessed is the One Who sent down the Standard to His servant, so that he may be a warner to the whole world
en.25:2: ˹Allah is˺ the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, Who has never had ˹any˺ offspring, nor does He have a partner in ˹governing˺ the kingdom. He has created everything, ordaining it precisely
en.25:3: Yet they have taken besides Him gods who cannot create anything but are themselves created. Nor can they protect or benefit themselves. Nor can they control life, death, or resurrection
en.25:4: The disbelievers say, “This ˹Quran˺ is nothing but a fabrication which he made up with the help of others.” Their claim is totally unjustified and untrue
en.25:5: And they say, “˹These revelations are only˺ ancient fables which he has had written down, and they are rehearsed to him morning and evening.”
en.25:6: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “This ˹Quran˺ has been revealed by the One Who knows the secrets of the heavens and the earth. Surely He is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”
en.25:7: And they say ˹mockingly˺, “What kind of messenger is this who eats food and goes about in market-places ˹for a living˺? If only an angel had been sent down with him to be his co-warner
en.25:8: or a treasure had been cast down to him, or he had had a garden from which he may eat!” And the wrongdoers say ˹to the believers˺, “You are only following a bewitched man.”
en.25:9: See ˹O Prophet˺ how they call you names! So they have gone so ˹far˺ astray that they cannot find the ˹Right˺ Way
en.25:10: Blessed is the One Who—if He wills—can give you far better than ˹all˺ that: gardens under which rivers flow, and palaces as well
en.25:11: In fact, they deny the Hour. And for the deniers of the Hour, We have prepared a blazing Fire
en.25:12: Once it sees them from a distance, they will hear it fuming and growling
en.25:13: And when they are tossed into a narrow place inside ˹Hell˺, chained together, then and there they will cry out for ˹instant˺ destruction
en.25:14: ˹They will be told,˺ “Do not cry only once for destruction, but cry many times over!”
en.25:15: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Is this better or the Garden of Eternity which the righteous have been promised, as a reward and ˹an ultimate˺ destination
en.25:16: There they will have whatever they wish for, forever. That is a promise ˹to be sought after˺, binding on your Lord.”
en.25:17: ˹Watch for˺ the Day He will gather them along with what they used to worship besides Allah, and ask ˹the objects of worship˺, “Was it you who misled these servants of Mine, or did they stray from the Way ˹on their own˺?”
en.25:18: They will say, “Glory be to You! It was not right for ˹others like˺ us to take any lords besides You, but You allowed enjoyment for them and their forefathers ˹for so long˺ that they forgot ˹Your˺ remembrance and became a doomed people.”
en.25:19: ˹The doomed will be told˺, “Your gods have clearly denied your claims. So now you can neither ward off ˹the punishment˺ nor get any help.” And whoever of you does wrong, We will make them taste a horrible punishment
en.25:20: We never sent any messenger before you ˹O Prophet˺, who did not eat food and go about in market-places. We have made some of you a trial for others. Will you ˹not then˺ be patient? And your Lord is All-Seeing
en.25:21: Those who do not expect to meet Us say, “If only the angels were sent down to us, or we could see our Lord!” They have certainly been carried away by their arrogance and have entirely exceeded all limits
en.25:22: ˹But˺ on the Day they will see the angels, there will be no good news for the wicked, who will cry, “Keep away! Away ˹from us˺!”
en.25:23: Then We will turn to whatever ˹good˺ deeds they did, reducing them to scattered dust
en.25:24: ˹But˺ on that Day the residents of Paradise will have the best settlement and the finest place to rest
en.25:25: ˹Watch for˺ the Day the heavens will burst with clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive ranks
en.25:26: True authority on that Day will belong ˹only˺ to the Most Compassionate. And it will be a hard day for the disbelievers
en.25:27: And ˹beware of˺ the Day the wrongdoer will bite his nails ˹in regret˺ and say, “Oh! I wish I had followed the Way along with the Messenger
en.25:28: Woe to me! I wish I had never taken so-and-so as a close friend
en.25:29: It was he who truly made me stray from the Reminder after it had reached me.” And Satan has always betrayed humanity
en.25:30: The Messenger has cried, “O my Lord! My people have indeed received this Quran with neglect.”
en.25:31: Similarly, We made enemies for every prophet from among the wicked, but sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper
en.25:32: The disbelievers say, “If only the Quran had been sent down to him all at once!” ˹We have sent it˺ as such ˹in stages˺ so We may reassure your heart with it. And We have revealed it at a deliberate pace
en.25:33: Whenever they bring you an argument, We come to you with the right refutation and the best explanation
en.25:34: Those who will be dragged into Hell on their faces will be in the worst place, and are ˹now˺ farthest from the ˹Right˺ Way
en.25:35: We certainly gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron as his helper
en.25:36: We had ordered ˹them˺, “Go to the people who would deny Our signs.” Then We annihilated the deniers entirely
en.25:37: And when the people of Noah rejected the messengers, We drowned them, making them an example to humanity. And We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers
en.25:38: Also ˹We destroyed˺ ’Ȃd, Thamûd, and the people of the Water-pit, as well as many peoples in between
en.25:39: For each We set forth ˹various˺ lessons, and We ultimately destroyed each
en.25:40: They have certainly passed by the city ˹of Sodom˺, which had been showered with a dreadful rain ˹of stones˺. Have they not seen its ruins? But they do not expect to be resurrected
en.25:41: When they see you ˹O Prophet˺, they only make fun of you, ˹saying,˺ “Is this the one that Allah has sent as a messenger
en.25:42: He would have almost tricked us away from our gods, had we not been so devoted to them.” ˹But˺ soon they will know, when they face the punishment, who is far astray from the ˹Right˺ Way
en.25:43: Have you seen ˹O Prophet˺ the one who has taken their own desires as their god? Will you then be a keeper over them
en.25:44: Or do you think that most of them listen or understand? They are only like cattle—no, more than that, they are astray from the ˹Right˺ Way
en.25:45: Have you not seen how your Lord extends the shade—He could have simply made it ˹remain˺ still if He so willed—then We make the sun its guide
en.25:46: causing the shade to retreat gradually
en.25:47: He is the One Who has made the night for you as a cover, and ˹made˺ sleep for resting, and the day for rising
en.25:48: And He is the One Who sends the winds ushering in His mercy, and We send down pure rain from the sky
en.25:49: giving life to a lifeless land, and providing water for countless animals and humans of Our Own creation
en.25:50: We certainly disperse it among them so they may be mindful, but most people persist in ungratefulness
en.25:51: Had We willed, We could have easily sent a warner to every society
en.25:52: So do not yield to the disbelievers, but strive diligently against them with this ˹Quran˺
en.25:53: And He is the One Who merges the two bodies of water: one fresh and palatable and the other salty and bitter, placing between them a barrier they cannot cross
en.25:54: And He is the One Who creates human beings from a ˹humble˺ liquid, then establishes for them bonds of kinship and marriage. For your Lord is Most Capable
en.25:55: Yet they worship besides Allah what can neither benefit nor harm them. And the disbeliever always collaborates against their Lord
en.25:56: And We have sent you ˹O Prophet˺ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner
en.25:57: Say, “I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺, but whoever wishes, let them pursue the Way to their Lord.”
en.25:58: Put your trust in the Ever-Living, Who never dies, and glorify His praises. Sufficient is He as All-Aware of the sins of His servants
en.25:59: ˹He is˺ the One Who created the heavens and the earth and everything in between in six Days, then established Himself on the Throne. ˹He is˺ the Most Compassionate! Ask ˹none other than˺ the All-Knowledgeable about Himself
en.25:60: When it is said to them, “Prostrate to the Most Compassionate,” they ask ˹in disgust˺, “What is ‘the Most Compassionate’? Will we prostrate to whatever you order us to?” And it only drives them farther away
en.25:61: Blessed is the One Who has placed constellations in the sky, as well as a ˹radiant˺ lamp and a luminous moon
en.25:62: And He is the One Who causes the day and the night to alternate, ˹as a sign˺ for whoever desires to be mindful or to be grateful
en.25:63: The ˹true˺ servants of the Most Compassionate are those who walk on the earth humbly, and when the foolish address them ˹improperly˺, they only respond with peace
en.25:64: ˹They are˺ those who spend ˹a good portion of˺ the night, prostrating themselves and standing before their Lord
en.25:65: ˹They are˺ those who pray, “Our Lord! Keep the punishment of Hell away from us, for its punishment is indeed unrelenting
en.25:66: It is certainly an evil place to settle and reside.”
en.25:67: ˹They are˺ those who spend neither wastefully nor stingily, but moderately in between
en.25:68: ˹They are˺ those who do not invoke any other god besides Allah, nor take a ˹human˺ life—made sacred by Allah—except with ˹legal˺ right, nor commit fornication. And whoever does ˹any of˺ this will face the penalty
en.25:69: Their punishment will be multiplied on the Day of Judgment, and they will remain in it forever, in disgrace
en.25:70: As for those who repent, believe, and do good deeds, they are the ones whose evil deeds Allah will change into good deeds. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.25:71: And whoever repents and does good has truly turned to Allah properly
en.25:72: ˹They are˺ those who do not bear false witness, and when they come across falsehood, they pass ˹it˺ by with dignity
en.25:73: ˹They are˺ those who, when reminded of the revelation of their Lord, do not turn a blind eye or a deaf ear to it
en.25:74: ˹They are˺ those who pray, “Our Lord! Bless us with ˹pious˺ spouses and offspring who will be the joy of our hearts, and make us models for the righteous.”
en.25:75: It is they who will be rewarded with ˹elevated˺ mansions ˹in Paradise˺ for their perseverance, and will be received with salutations and ˹greetings of˺ peace
en.25:76: staying there forever. What an excellent place to settle and reside
en.25:77: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “You ˹all˺ would not ˹even˺ matter to my Lord were it not for your faith ˹in Him˺. But now you ˹disbelievers˺ have denied ˹the truth˺, so the torment is bound to come.”
en.26:2: These are the verses of the clear Book
en.26:3: Perhaps you ˹O Prophet˺ will grieve yourself to death over their disbelief
en.26:4: If We willed, We could send down upon them a ˹compelling˺ sign from the heavens, leaving their necks bent in ˹utter˺ submission to it
en.26:5: Whatever new reminder comes to them from the Most Compassionate, they always turn away from it
en.26:6: They have certainly denied ˹the truth˺, so they will soon face the consequences of their ridicule
en.26:7: Have they failed to look at the earth, ˹to see˺ how many types of fine plants We have caused to grow in it
en.26:8: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:9: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:10: ˹Remember˺ when your Lord called out to Moses, “Go to the wrongdoing people—
en.26:11: the people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear ˹Allah˺?”
en.26:12: He replied, “My Lord! I fear that they will reject me
en.26:13: And ˹so˺ my heart will be broken and my tongue will be tied. So send Aaron along ˹as a messenger˺
en.26:14: Also, they have a charge against me, so I fear they may kill me.”
en.26:15: Allah responded, “Certainly not! So go, both of you, with Our signs. We will be with you, listening
en.26:16: Go to Pharaoh and say, ‘We are messengers from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:17: ˹commanded to say:˺ ‘Let the Children of Israel go with us.’’”
en.26:18: Pharaoh protested, “Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you stayed several years of your life in our care
en.26:19: Then you did what you did, being ˹utterly˺ ungrateful!”
en.26:20: Moses replied, “I did it then, lacking guidance
en.26:21: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and made me one of the messengers
en.26:22: How can that be a ‘favour,’ of which you remind me, when ˹it was only because˺ you ˹have˺ enslaved the Children of Israel?”
en.26:23: Pharaoh asked, “And what is ‘the Lord of all worlds’?”
en.26:24: Moses replied, “˹He is˺ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith.”
en.26:25: Pharaoh said to those around him, “Did you hear ˹what he said˺?”
en.26:26: Moses added, “˹He is˺ your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.”
en.26:27: Pharaoh said ˹mockingly˺, “Your messenger, who has been sent to you, must be insane.”
en.26:28: Moses responded: “˹He is˺ the Lord of the east and west, and everything in between, if only you had any sense.”
en.26:29: Pharaoh threatened, “If you take any other god besides me, I will certainly have you imprisoned.”
en.26:30: Moses responded, “Even if I bring you a clear proof?”
en.26:31: Pharaoh demanded, “Bring it then, if what you say is true.”
en.26:32: So he threw down his staff and—behold!—it became a real snake
en.26:33: Then he drew his hand ˹out of his collar˺ and it was ˹shining˺ white for all to see
en.26:34: Pharaoh said to the chiefs around him, “He is indeed a skilled magician
en.26:35: who seeks to drive you from your land by his magic. So what do you propose?”
en.26:36: They replied, “Let him and his brother wait and dispatch mobilizers to all cities
en.26:37: to bring you every skilled magician.”
en.26:38: So the magicians were assembled at the set time on the appointed day
en.26:39: And the people were asked, “Will you join the gathering
en.26:40: so that we may follow the magicians if they prevail?”
en.26:41: When the magicians came, they asked Pharaoh, “Shall we have a ˹suitable˺ reward if we prevail?”
en.26:42: He replied, “Yes, and you will then certainly be among those closest to me.”
en.26:43: Moses said to them, “Cast whatever you wish to cast.”
en.26:44: So they cast down their ropes and staffs, saying, “By Pharaoh’s might, it is we who will surely prevail.”
en.26:45: Then Moses threw down his staff, and—behold!—it devoured the objects of their illusion
en.26:46: So the magicians fell down, prostrating
en.26:47: They declared, “We ˹now˺ believe in the Lord of all worlds—
en.26:48: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.”
en.26:49: Pharaoh threatened, “How dare you believe in him before I give you permission? He must be your master who taught you magic, but soon you will see. I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then crucify you all.”
en.26:50: They responded, “˹That would be˺ no harm! Surely to our Lord we will ˹all˺ return
en.26:51: We really hope that our Lord will forgive our sins, as we are the first to believe.”
en.26:52: And We inspired Moses, ˹saying,˺ “Leave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued.”
en.26:53: Then Pharaoh sent mobilizers to all cities
en.26:54: ˹and said,˺ “These ˹outcasts˺ are just a handful of people
en.26:56: but we are all on the alert.”
en.26:57: So We lured the tyrants out of ˹their˺ gardens, springs
en.26:59: So it was. And We awarded it ˹all˺ to the Children of Israel
en.26:60: And so they pursued them at sunrise
en.26:61: When the two groups came face to face, the companions of Moses cried out, “We are overtaken for sure.”
en.26:62: Moses reassured ˹them˺, “Absolutely not! My Lord is certainly with me—He will guide me.”
en.26:63: So We inspired Moses: “Strike the sea with your staff,” and the sea was split, each part was like a huge mountain
en.26:64: We drew the pursuers to that place
en.26:65: and delivered Moses and those with him all together
en.26:67: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:68: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:69: Relate to them ˹O Prophet˺ the story of Abraham
en.26:70: when he questioned his father and his people, “What is that you worship ˹besides Allah˺?”
en.26:71: They replied, “We worship idols, to which we are fully devoted.”
en.26:72: Abraham asked, “Can they hear you when you call upon them
en.26:73: Or can they benefit or harm you?”
en.26:74: They replied, “No! But we found our forefathers doing the same.”
en.26:75: Abraham responded, “Have you ˹really˺ considered what you have been worshipping—
en.26:77: They are ˹all˺ enemies to me, except the Lord of all worlds
en.26:78: ˹He is˺ the One Who created me, and He ˹alone˺ guides me
en.26:79: ˹He is˺ the One Who provides me with food and drink
en.26:80: And He ˹alone˺ heals me when I am sick
en.26:81: And He ˹is the One Who˺ will cause me to die, and then bring me back to life
en.26:82: And He is ˹the One˺ Who, I hope, will forgive my flaws on Judgment Day.”
en.26:83: “My Lord! Grant me wisdom, and join me with the righteous
en.26:84: Bless me with honourable mention among later generations
en.26:85: Make me one of those awarded the Garden of Bliss
en.26:86: Forgive my father, for he is certainly one of the misguided
en.26:87: And do not disgrace me on the Day all will be resurrected—
en.26:88: the Day when neither wealth nor children will be of any benefit
en.26:89: Only those who come before Allah with a pure heart ˹will be saved˺.”
en.26:90: ˹On that Day˺ Paradise will be brought near to the God-fearing
en.26:91: and the Hellfire will be displayed to the deviant
en.26:92: And it will be said to them, “Where are those you used to worship
en.26:93: besides Allah? Can they help you or even help themselves?”
en.26:94: Then the idols will be hurled headlong into Hell, along with the deviant
en.26:95: and the soldiers of Iblîs, all together
en.26:96: There the deviant will cry while disputing with their idols
en.26:98: when we made you equal to the Lord of all worlds
en.26:99: And none led us astray other than the wicked
en.26:100: Now we have none to intercede for us
en.26:102: If only we could have a second chance, then we would be believers.”
en.26:103: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:104: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:105: The people of Noah rejected the messengers
en.26:106: when their brother Noah said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺
en.26:107: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you
en.26:108: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:109: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:110: So fear Allah, and obey me.”
en.26:111: They argued, “How can we believe in you, when you are followed ˹only˺ by the lowest of the low?”
en.26:112: He responded, “And what knowledge do I have of what they do
en.26:113: Their judgment is with my Lord, if you had any sense
en.26:114: I am not going to expel the believers
en.26:115: I am only sent with a clear warning.”
en.26:116: They threatened, “If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be stoned ˹to death˺.”
en.26:117: Noah prayed, “My Lord! My people have truly rejected me
en.26:118: So judge between me and them decisively, and save me and the believers with me.”
en.26:119: So We saved him and those with him in the fully loaded Ark
en.26:120: Then afterwards We drowned the rest
en.26:121: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:122: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:123: The people of ’Âd rejected the messengers
en.26:124: when their brother Hûd said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺
en.26:125: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you
en.26:126: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:127: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:128: ˹Why˺ do you build a landmark on every high place in vanity
en.26:129: and construct castles, as if you are going to live forever
en.26:130: and act so viciously when you attack ˹others˺
en.26:131: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:132: Fear the One Who has provided you with ˹the good˺ things you know
en.26:133: He provided you with cattle, and children
en.26:135: I truly fear for you the torment of a tremendous day.”
en.26:136: They responded, “It is all the same to us whether you warn ˹us˺ or not
en.26:137: This is simply the tradition of our predecessors
en.26:138: And we will never be punished.”
en.26:139: So they rejected him, and ˹so˺ We destroyed them. Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:140: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:141: The people of Thamûd rejected the messengers
en.26:142: when their brother Ṣâliḥ said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺
en.26:143: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you
en.26:144: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:145: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:146: Do you think you will be ˹forever˺ left secure in what you have here
en.26:148: and ˹various˺ crops, and palm trees ˹loaded˺ with tender fruit
en.26:149: to carve homes in the mountains with great skill
en.26:150: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:151: And do not follow the command of the transgressors
en.26:152: who spread corruption throughout the land, never setting things right.”
en.26:154: You are only a human being like us, so bring forth a sign if what you say is true.”
en.26:155: Ṣâliḥ said, “Here is a camel. She will have her turn to drink as you have yours, each on an appointed day
en.26:156: And do not ever touch her with harm, or you will be overtaken by the torment of a tremendous day.”
en.26:157: But they killed her, becoming regretful
en.26:158: So the punishment overtook them. Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:159: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:160: The people of Lot rejected the messengers
en.26:161: when their brother Lot said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺
en.26:162: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you
en.26:163: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:164: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:165: Why do you ˹men˺ lust after fellow men
en.26:166: leaving the wives that your Lord has created for you? In fact, you are a transgressing people.”
en.26:167: They threatened, “If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be expelled.”
en.26:168: Lot responded, “I am truly one of those who despise your ˹shameful˺ practice
en.26:169: My Lord! Save me and my family from ˹the consequences of˺ what they do.”
en.26:170: So We saved him and all of his family
en.26:171: except an old woman, who was one of the doomed
en.26:172: Then We utterly destroyed the rest
en.26:173: pouring upon them a rain ˹of brimstone˺. How evil was the rain of those who had been warned
en.26:174: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:175: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:176: The residents of the Forest rejected the messengers
en.26:177: when Shu’aib said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺
en.26:178: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you
en.26:179: So fear Allah, and obey me
en.26:180: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:181: Give full measure, and cause no loss ˹to others˺
en.26:183: and do not defraud people of their property. Nor go about spreading corruption in the land
en.26:184: And fear the One Who created you and ˹all˺ earlier peoples.”
en.26:186: Also, you are only a human being like us, and we think you are indeed a liar
en.26:187: So cause ˹deadly˺ pieces of the sky to fall upon us, if what you say is true.”
en.26:188: Shu’aib responded, “My Lord knows best whatever you do.”
en.26:189: So they rejected him, and ˹so˺ were overtaken by the torment of the day of the ˹deadly˺ cloud. That was really a torment of a tremendous day
en.26:190: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe
en.26:191: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:192: This is certainly a revelation from the Lord of all worlds
en.26:193: which the trustworthy spirit ˹Gabriel˺ brought down
en.26:194: into your heart ˹O Prophet˺—so that you may be one of the warners—
en.26:196: And it has indeed been ˹foretold˺ in the Scriptures of those before
en.26:197: Was it not sufficient proof for the deniers that it has been recognized by the knowledgeable among the Children of Israel
en.26:198: Had We revealed it to a non-Arab
en.26:199: who would then recite it to the deniers ˹in fluent Arabic˺, still they would not have believed in it
en.26:200: This is how We allow denial ˹to steep˺ into the hearts of the wicked
en.26:201: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment
en.26:202: which will take them by surprise when they least expect ˹it˺
en.26:203: Then they will cry, “Can we be allowed more time?”
en.26:204: Do they ˹really˺ seek to hasten Our torment
en.26:205: Imagine ˹O Prophet˺ if We allowed them enjoyment for years
en.26:206: then there came to them what they had been threatened with
en.26:207: would that enjoyment be of any benefit to them ˹at all˺
en.26:208: We have never destroyed a society without warners
en.26:209: to remind ˹them˺, for We would never wrong ˹anyone˺
en.26:210: It was not the devils who brought this ˹Quran˺ down
en.26:211: it is not for them ˹to do so˺, nor can they
en.26:212: for they are strictly barred from ˹even˺ overhearing ˹it˺
en.26:213: So do not ever call upon any other god besides Allah, or you will be one of the punished
en.26:214: And warn ˹all, starting with˺ your closest relatives
en.26:215: and be gracious to the believers who follow you
en.26:216: But if they disobey you, say, “I am certainly free of what you do.”
en.26:217: Put your trust in the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.26:218: Who sees you when you rise ˹for prayer at night˺
en.26:219: as well as your movements ˹in prayer˺ along with ˹fellow˺ worshippers
en.26:220: He ˹alone˺ is indeed the All-Hearing, All-Knowing
en.26:221: Shall I inform you of whom the devils ˹actually˺ descend upon
en.26:222: They descend upon every sinful liar
en.26:223: who gives an ˹attentive˺ ear ˹to half-truths˺, mostly passing on sheer lies
en.26:224: As for poets, they are followed ˹merely˺ by deviants
en.26:225: Do you not see how they rant in every field
en.26:226: only saying what they never do
en.26:227: Except those who believe, do good, remember Allah often, and ˹poetically˺ avenge ˹the believers˺ after being wrongfully slandered. The wrongdoers will come to know what ˹evil˺ end they will meet
en.27:1: Ṭâ-Sĩn. These are the verses of the Quran; the clear Book
en.27:2: ˹It is˺ a guide and good news for the believers
en.27:3: ˹those˺ who establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and have sure faith in the Hereafter
en.27:4: As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have certainly made their ˹evil˺ deeds appealing to them, so they wander blindly
en.27:5: It is they who will suffer a dreadful torment, and in the Hereafter they will ˹truly˺ be the greatest losers
en.27:6: And indeed, you ˹O Prophet˺ are receiving the Quran from the One ˹Who is˺ All-Wise, All-Knowing
en.27:7: ˹Remember˺ when Moses said to his family, “I have spotted a fire. I will either bring you some directions from there, or a burning torch so you may warm yourselves.”
en.27:8: But when he came to it, he was called ˹by Allah˺, “Blessed is the one at the fire, and whoever is around it! Glory be to Allah, the Lord of all worlds
en.27:9: O Moses! It is truly I. I am Allah—the Almighty, All-Wise
en.27:10: Now, throw down your staff!” But when he saw it slithering like a snake, he ran away without looking back. ˹Allah reassured him,˺ “O Moses! Do not be afraid! Messengers should have no fear in My presence
en.27:11: ˹Fear is˺ only for those who do wrong. But if they later mend ˹their˺ evil ˹ways˺ with good, then I am certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.27:12: Now put your hand through ˹the opening of˺ your collar, it will come out ˹shining˺ white, unblemished. ˹These are two˺ of nine signs for Pharaoh and his people. They have truly been a rebellious people.”
en.27:13: But when Our enlightening signs came to them, they said, “This is pure magic.”
en.27:14: And, although their hearts were convinced the signs were true, they still denied them wrongfully and arrogantly. See then what was the end of the corruptors
en.27:15: Indeed, We granted knowledge to David and Solomon. And they said ˹in acknowledgment˺, “All praise is for Allah Who has privileged us over many of His faithful servants.”
en.27:16: And David was succeeded by Solomon, who said, “O people! We have been taught the language of birds, and been given everything ˹we need˺. This is indeed a great privilege.”
en.27:17: Solomon’s forces of jinn, humans, and birds were rallied for him, perfectly organized
en.27:18: And when they came across a valley of ants, an ant warned, “O ants! Go quickly into your homes so Solomon and his armies do not crush you, unknowingly.”
en.27:19: So Solomon smiled in amusement at her words, and prayed, “My Lord! Inspire me to ˹always˺ be thankful for Your favours which You have blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please you. Admit me, by Your mercy, into ˹the company of˺ Your righteous servants.”
en.27:20: ˹One day˺ he inspected the birds, and wondered, “Why is it that I cannot see the hoopoe? Or could he be absent
en.27:21: I will surely subject him to a severe punishment, or ˹even˺ slaughter him, unless he brings me a compelling excuse.”
en.27:22: It was not long before the bird came and said, “I have found out something you do not know. I have just come to you from Sheba with sure news
en.27:23: Indeed, I found a woman ruling over them, who has been given everything ˹she needs˺, and who has a magnificent throne
en.27:24: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah. For Satan has made their deeds appealing to them—hindering them from the ˹Right˺ Way and leaving them unguided—
en.27:25: so they do not prostrate to Allah, Who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you ˹all˺ conceal and what you reveal
en.27:26: ˹He is˺ Allah! There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him, the Lord of the Mighty Throne.”
