en.34:39: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Surely ˹it is˺ my Lord ˹Who˺ gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills of His servants. And whatever you spend in charity, He will compensate ˹you˺ for it. For He is the Best Provider.”
en.34:40: And ˹consider˺ the Day He will gather them all together, and then ask the angels, “Was it you that these ˹polytheists˺ used to worship?”
en.34:41: They will say, “Glory be to You! Our loyalty is to You, not them. In fact, they ˹only˺ followed the ˹temptations of evil˺ jinn, in whom most of them had faith.”
en.34:42: So Today neither of you can benefit or protect each other. And We will say to the wrongdoers, “Taste the torment of the Fire, which you used to deny.”
en.34:43: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, they say, “This is only a man who wishes to hinder you from what your forefathers used to worship.” They also say, “This ˹Quran˺ is no more than a fabricated lie.” And the disbelievers say of the truth when it has come to them, “This is nothing but pure magic.”
en.34:44: ˹They say so even though˺ We had never given them any scriptures to study, nor did We ever send them a warner before you ˹O Prophet˺
en.34:45: Those ˹destroyed˺ before them denied as well—and these ˹Meccans˺ have not attained even one-tenth of what We had given their predecessors. Yet ˹when˺ they denied My messengers, how severe was My response
en.34:46: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I advise you to do ˹only˺ one thing: stand up for ˹the sake of˺ Allah—individually or in pairs—then reflect. Your fellow man is not insane. He is only a warner to you before ˹the coming of˺ a severe punishment.”
en.34:47: Say, “If I had ever asked you for a reward, you could keep it. My reward is only from Allah. And He is a Witness over all things.”
en.34:48: Say, “Surely my Lord hurls the truth ˹against falsehood˺. ˹He is˺ the Knower of all unseen.”
en.34:49: Say, “The truth has come, and falsehood will vanish, never to return.”
en.34:50: Say, “If I am astray, the loss is only mine. And if I am guided, it is ˹only˺ because of what my Lord reveals to me. He is indeed All-Hearing, Ever Near.”
en.34:51: If only you could see when they will be horrified with no escape ˹on Judgment Day˺! And they will be seized from a nearby place
en.34:52: They will ˹then˺ cry, “We do ˹now˺ believe in it ˹all˺.” But how could they ˹possibly˺ attain faith from a place so far-off ˹from the world˺
en.34:53: while they had already rejected it before, guessing blindly from a place ˹equally˺ far-away ˹from the Hereafter˺
en.34:54: They will be sealed off from whatever they desire, as was done to their counterparts before. Indeed, they were ˹all˺ in alarming doubt
en.35:1: All praise is for Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, Who made angels ˹as His˺ messengers with wings—two, three, or four. He increases in creation whatever He wills. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything
en.35:2: Whatever mercy Allah opens up for people, none can withhold it. And whatever He withholds, none but Him can release it. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.35:3: O humanity! Remember Allah’s favours upon you. Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the heavens and the earth? There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. How can you then be deluded ˹from the truth˺
en.35:4: If you are rejected by them, so too were messengers before you. And to Allah ˹all˺ matters will be returned ˹for judgment˺
en.35:5: O humanity! Indeed, Allah’s promise is true. So do not let the life of this world deceive you, nor let the Chief Deceiver deceive you about Allah
en.35:6: Surely Satan is an enemy to you, so take him as an enemy. He only invites his followers to become inmates of the Blaze
en.35:7: Those who disbelieve will have a severe punishment. But those who believe and do good will have forgiveness and a great reward
en.35:8: Are those whose evil-doing is made so appealing to them that they deem it good ˹like those who are rightly guided˺? ˹It is˺ certainly Allah ˹Who˺ leaves to stray whoever He wills, and guides whoever He wills. So do not grieve yourself to death over them ˹O Prophet˺. Surely Allah is All-Knowing of what they do
en.35:9: And it is Allah Who sends the winds, which then stir up ˹vapour, forming˺ clouds, and then We drive them to a lifeless land, giving life to the earth after its death. Similar is the Resurrection
en.35:10: Whoever seeks honour and power, then ˹let them know that˺ all honour and power belongs to Allah. To Him ˹alone˺ good words ascend, and righteous deeds are raised up by Him. As for those who plot evil, they will suffer a severe punishment. And the plotting of such ˹people˺ is doomed ˹to fail˺
en.35:11: And ˹it is˺ Allah ˹Who˺ created you from dust, then ˹developed you˺ from a sperm-drop, then made you into pairs. No female ever conceives or delivers without His knowledge. And no one’s life is made long or cut short but is ˹written˺ in a Record. That is certainly easy for Allah
en.35:12: The two bodies of water are not alike: one is fresh, palatable, and pleasant to drink and the other is salty and bitter. Yet from them both you eat tender seafood and extract ornaments to wear. And you see the ships ploughing their way through both, so you may seek His bounty and give thanks ˹to Him˺
en.35:13: He merges the night into the day and the day into the night, and has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term. That is Allah—your Lord! All authority belongs to Him. But those ˹idols˺ you invoke besides Him do not possess even the skin of a date stone
en.35:14: If you call upon them, they cannot hear your calls. And if they were to hear, they could not respond to you. On the Day of Judgment they will disown your worship ˹of them˺. And no one can inform you ˹O Prophet˺ like the All-Knowledgeable
en.35:15: O humanity! It is you who stand in need of Allah, but Allah ˹alone˺ is the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy
en.35:16: If He willed, He could eliminate you and produce a new creation
en.35:17: And that is not difficult for Allah ˹at all˺
en.35:18: No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. And if a sin-burdened soul cries for help with its burden, none of it will be carried—even by a close relative. You ˹O Prophet˺ can only warn those who stand in awe of their Lord without seeing Him and establish prayer. Whoever purifies themselves, they only do so for their own good. And to Allah is the final return
en.35:19: Those blind ˹to the truth˺ and those who can see are not equal
en.35:20: nor are the darkness and the light
en.35:21: nor the ˹scorching˺ heat and the ˹cool˺ shade
en.35:22: Nor are the dead and the living equal. Indeed, Allah ˹alone˺ makes whoever He wills hear, but you ˹O Prophet˺ can never make those in the graves hear ˹your call˺
en.35:24: We have surely sent you with the truth as a deliverer of good news and a warner. There is no community that has not had a warner
en.35:25: If they deny you, so did those before them. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, divine Books, and enlightening Scriptures
en.35:26: Then I seized those who persisted in disbelief. How severe was My response
en.35:27: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky with which We bring forth fruits of different colours? And in the mountains are streaks of varying shades of white, red, and raven black
en.35:28: just as people, living beings, and cattle are of various colours as well. Of all of Allah’s servants, only the knowledgeable ˹of His might˺ are ˹truly˺ in awe of Him. Allah is indeed Almighty, All-Forgiving
en.35:29: Surely those who recite the Book of Allah, establish prayer, and donate from what We have provided for them—secretly and openly—˹can˺ hope for an exchange that will never fail
en.35:30: so that He will reward them in full and increase them out of His grace. He is truly All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative
en.35:31: The Book We have revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺ is the truth, confirming what came before it. Surely Allah is All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants
en.35:32: Then We granted the Book to those We have chosen from Our servants. Some of them wrong themselves, some follow a middle course, and some are foremost in good deeds by Allah’s Will. That is ˹truly˺ the greatest bounty
en.35:33: They will enter the Gardens of Eternity, where they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing will be silk
en.35:34: And they will say, “Praise be to Allah, Who has kept away from us all ˹causes of˺ sorrow. Our Lord is indeed All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative
en.35:35: ˹He is the One˺ Who—out of His grace—has settled us in the Home of Everlasting Stay, where we will be touched by neither fatigue nor weariness.”
en.35:36: As for the disbelievers, they will have the Fire of Hell, where they will not be ˹allowed to be˺ finished by death, nor will its torment be lightened for them. This is how We reward every ˹stubborn˺ disbeliever
en.35:37: There they will be ˹fervently˺ screaming, “Our Lord! Take us out ˹and send us back˺. We will do good, unlike what we used to do.” ˹They will be told,˺ “Did We not give you lives long enough so that whoever wanted to be mindful could have done so? And the warner came to you. So taste ˹the punishment˺, for the wrongdoers have no helper.”
en.35:38: Indeed, Allah is the Knower of the unseen of the heavens and the earth. He surely knows best what is ˹hidden˺ in the heart
en.35:39: He is the One Who has placed you as successors on earth. So whoever disbelieves will bear ˹the burden of˺ their own disbelief. The disbelievers’ denial only increases them in contempt in the sight of their Lord, and it will only contribute to their loss
en.35:40: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “Have you considered your associate-gods which you invoke besides Allah? Show me what they have created on earth! Or do they have a share in ˹the creation of˺ the heavens? Or have We given the polytheists a Book, which serves as a clear proof for them? In fact, the wrongdoers promise each other nothing but delusion.”
en.35:41: Indeed, Allah ˹alone˺ keeps the heavens and the earth from falling apart. If they were to fall apart, none but Him could hold them up. He is truly Most Forbearing, All-Forgiving
en.35:42: They swore by Allah their most solemn oaths that if a warner were to come to them, they would certainly be better guided than any other community. Yet when a warner did come to them, it only drove them farther away—
en.35:43: behaving arrogantly in the land and plotting evil. But evil plotting only backfires on those who plot. Are they awaiting anything but the fate of those ˹destroyed˺ before? You will find no change in the way of Allah, nor will you find it diverted ˹to someone else˺
en.35:44: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those ˹destroyed˺ before them? They were far superior in might. But there is nothing that can escape Allah in the heavens or the earth. He is certainly All-Knowing, Most Capable
en.35:45: If Allah were to punish people ˹immediately˺ for what they have committed, He would not have left a single living being on earth. But He delays them for an appointed term. And when their time arrives, then surely Allah is All-Seeing of His servants
en.36:2: By the Quran, rich in wisdom
en.36:3: You ˹O Prophet˺ are truly one of the messengers
en.36:5: ˹This is˺ a revelation from the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.36:6: so that you may warn a people whose forefathers were not warned, and so are heedless
en.36:7: The decree ˹of torment˺ has already been justified against most of them, for they will never believe
en.36:8: ˹It is as if˺ We have put shackles around their necks up to their chins, so their heads are forced up
en.36:9: and have placed a barrier before them and a barrier behind them and covered them ˹all˺ up, so they fail to see ˹the truth˺
en.36:10: It is the same whether you warn them or not—they will never believe
en.36:11: You can only warn those who follow the Reminder and are in awe of the Most Compassionate without seeing Him. So give them good news of forgiveness and an honourable reward
en.36:12: It is certainly We Who resurrect the dead, and write what they send forth and what they leave behind. Everything is listed by Us in a perfect Record
en.36:13: Give them an example ˹O Prophet˺ of the residents of a town, when the messengers came to them
en.36:14: We sent them two messengers, but they rejected both. So We reinforced ˹the two˺ with a third, and they declared, “We have indeed been sent to you ˹as messengers˺.”
en.36:15: The people replied, “You are only humans like us, and the Most Compassionate has not revealed anything. You are simply lying!”
en.36:16: The messengers responded, “Our Lord knows that we have truly been sent to you
en.36:17: And our duty is only to deliver ˹the message˺ clearly.”
en.36:18: The people replied, “We definitely see you as a bad omen for us. If you do not desist, we will certainly stone you ˹to death˺ and you will be touched with a painful punishment from us.”
en.36:19: The messengers said, “Your bad omen lies within yourselves. Are you saying this because you are reminded ˹of the truth˺? In fact, you are a transgressing people.”
en.36:20: Then from the farthest end of the city a man came, rushing. He advised, “O my people! Follow the messengers
en.36:21: Follow those who ask no reward of you, and are ˹rightly˺ guided
en.36:22: And why should I not worship the One Who has originated me, and to Whom you will be returned
en.36:23: How could I take besides Him other gods whose intercession would not be of any benefit to me, nor could they save me if the Most Compassionate intended to harm me
en.36:24: Indeed, I would then be clearly astray
en.36:25: I do believe in your Lord, so listen to me.”
en.36:26: ˹But they killed him, then˺ he was told ˹by the angels˺, “Enter Paradise!” He said, “If only my people knew
en.36:27: of how my Lord has forgiven me, and made me one of the honourable.”
en.36:28: We did not send any soldiers from the heavens against his people after his death, nor did We need to
en.36:29: All it took was one ˹mighty˺ blast, and they were extinguished at once
en.36:30: Oh pity, such beings! No messenger ever came to them without being mocked
en.36:31: Have the deniers not considered how many peoples We destroyed before them who never came back to life again
en.36:32: Yet they will all be brought before Us
en.36:33: There is a sign for them in the dead earth: We give it life, producing grain from it for them to eat
en.36:34: And We have placed in it gardens of palm trees and grapevines, and caused springs to gush forth in it
en.36:35: so that they may eat from its fruit, which they had no hand in making. Will they not then give thanks
en.36:36: Glory be to the One Who created all ˹things in˺ pairs—˹be it˺ what the earth produces, their genders, or what they do not know
en.36:37: There is also a sign for them in the night: We strip from it daylight, then—behold!—they are in darkness
en.36:38: The sun travels for its fixed term. That is the design of the Almighty, All-Knowing
en.36:39: As for the moon, We have ordained ˹precise˺ phases for it, until it ends up like an old, curved palm stalk
en.36:40: It is not for the sun to catch up with the moon, nor does the night outrun the day. Each is travelling in an orbit of their own
en.36:41: Another sign for them is that We carried their ancestors ˹with Noah˺ in the fully loaded Ark
en.36:42: and created for them similar things to ride in
en.36:43: If We willed, We could drown them: then no one would respond to their cries, nor would they be rescued—
en.36:44: except by mercy from Us, allowing them enjoyment for a ˹little˺ while
en.36:45: ˹Still they turn away˺ when it is said to them, “Beware of what is ahead of you ˹in the Hereafter˺ and what is behind you ˹of destroyed nations˺ so you may be shown mercy.”
en.36:46: Whenever a sign comes to them from their Lord, they turn away from it
en.36:47: And when it is said to them, “Donate from what Allah has provided for you,” the disbelievers say to the believers, “Why should we feed those whom Allah could have fed if He wanted to? You are clearly astray!”
en.36:48: And they ask ˹the believers˺, “When will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?”
en.36:49: They must be awaiting a single Blast, which will seize them while they are ˹entrenched˺ in ˹worldly˺ disputes
en.36:50: Then they will not be able to make a ˹last˺ will, nor can they return to their own people
en.36:51: The Trumpet will be blown ˹a second time˺, then—behold!—they will rush from the graves to their Lord
en.36:52: They will cry, “Woe to us! Who has raised us up from our place of rest? This must be what the Most Compassionate warned us of; the messengers told the truth!”
en.36:53: It will only take one Blast, then at once they will all be brought before Us
en.36:54: On that Day no soul will be wronged in the least, nor will you be rewarded except for what you used to do
en.36:55: Indeed, on that Day the residents of Paradise will be busy enjoying themselves
en.36:56: They and their spouses will be in ˹cool˺ shade, reclining on ˹canopied˺ couches
en.36:57: There they will have fruits and whatever they desire
en.36:58: And “Peace!” will be ˹their˺ greeting from the Merciful Lord
en.36:59: ˹Then the disbelievers will be told,˺ “Step away ˹from the believers˺ this Day, O wicked ones
en.36:60: Did I not command you, O Children of Adam, not to follow Satan, for he is truly your sworn enemy
en.36:61: but to worship Me ˹alone˺? This is the Straight Path
en.36:62: Yet he already misled great multitudes of you. Did you not have any sense
en.36:63: This is the Hell you were warned of
en.36:64: Burn in it Today for your disbelief.”
en.36:65: On this Day We will seal their mouths, their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify to what they used to commit
en.36:66: Had We willed, We could have easily blinded their eyes, so they would struggle to find their way. How then could they see
en.36:67: And had We willed, We could have transfigured them on the spot, so they could neither progress forward nor turn back
en.36:68: And whoever We grant a long life, We reverse them in development. Will they not then understand
en.36:69: We have not taught him poetry, nor is it fitting for him. This ˹Book˺ is only a Reminder and a clear Quran
en.36:70: to warn whoever is ˹truly˺ alive and fulfil the decree ˹of torment˺ against the disbelievers
en.36:71: Do they not see that We singlehandedly created for them, among other things, cattle which are under their control
en.36:72: And We have subjected these ˹animals˺ to them, so they may ride some and eat others
en.36:73: And they derive from them other benefits and drinks. Will they not then give thanks
en.36:74: Still they have taken other gods besides Allah, hoping to be helped ˹by them˺
en.36:75: They cannot help the pagans, even though they serve the idols as dedicated guards
en.36:76: So do not let their words grieve you ˹O Prophet˺. Indeed, We ˹fully˺ know what they conceal and what they reveal
en.36:77: Do people not see that We have created them from a sperm-drop, then—behold!—they openly challenge ˹Us˺
en.36:78: And they argue with Us—forgetting they were created—saying, “Who will give life to decayed bones?”
en.36:79: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “They will be revived by the One Who produced them the first time, for He has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of every created being
en.36:80: ˹He is the One˺ Who gives you fire from green trees, and—behold!—you kindle ˹fire˺ from them
en.36:81: Can the One Who created the heavens and the earth not ˹easily˺ resurrect these ˹deniers˺?” Yes ˹He can˺! For He is the Master Creator, All-Knowing
en.36:82: All it takes, when He wills something ˹to be˺, is simply to say to it: “Be!” And it is
en.36:83: So glory be to the One in Whose Hands is the authority over all things, and to Whom ˹alone˺ you will ˹all˺ be returned
en.37:1: By those ˹angels˺ lined up in ranks
en.37:2: and those who diligently drive ˹the clouds˺
en.37:3: and those who recite the Reminder
en.37:5: ˹He is˺ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, and the Lord of all points of sunrise
en.37:6: Indeed, We have adorned the lowest heaven with the stars for decoration
en.37:7: and ˹for˺ protection from every rebellious devil
en.37:8: They cannot listen to the highest assembly ˹of angels˺ for they are pelted from every side
en.37:9: ˹fiercely˺ driven away. And they will suffer an everlasting torment
en.37:10: But whoever manages to stealthily eavesdrop is ˹instantly˺ pursued by a piercing flare
en.37:11: So ask them ˹O Prophet˺, which is harder to create: them or other marvels of Our creation? Indeed, We created them from a sticky clay
en.37:12: In fact, you are astonished ˹by their denial˺, while they ridicule ˹you˺
en.37:13: When they are reminded, they are never mindful
en.37:14: And whenever they see a sign, they make fun of it
en.37:15: saying, “This is nothing but pure magic
en.37:16: When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected
en.37:18: Say, “Yes! And you will be fully humbled.”
en.37:19: It will only take one Blast, then at once they will see ˹it all˺
en.37:20: They will cry, “Oh, woe to us! This is the Day of Judgment!”
en.37:21: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is the Day of ˹Final˺ Decision which you used to deny.”
en.37:22: ˹Allah will say to the angels,˺ “Gather ˹all˺ the wrongdoers along with their peers, and whatever they used to worship
en.37:23: instead of Allah, then lead them ˹all˺ to the path of Hell
en.37:24: And detain them, for they must be questioned.”
en.37:25: ˹Then they will be asked,˺ “What is the matter with you that you can no longer help each other?”
en.37:26: In fact, on that Day they will be ˹fully˺ submissive
en.37:27: They will turn on each other, throwing blame
en.37:28: The misled will say, “It was you who deluded us away from what is right.”
en.37:29: The misleaders will reply, “No! You disbelieved on your own
en.37:30: We had no authority over you. In fact, you yourselves were a transgressing people
en.37:31: The decree of our Lord has come to pass against us ˹all˺: we will certainly taste ˹the punishment˺
en.37:32: We caused you to deviate, for we ourselves were deviant.”
en.37:33: Surely on that Day they will ˹all˺ share in the punishment
en.37:34: That is certainly how We deal with the wicked
en.37:35: For whenever it was said to them ˹in the world˺, “There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Allah,” they acted arrogantly
en.37:36: and argued, “Should we really abandon our gods for a mad poet?”
en.37:37: In fact, he came with the truth, confirming ˹earlier˺ messengers
en.37:38: You will certainly taste the painful torment
en.37:39: and will only be rewarded for what you used to do
en.37:40: But not the chosen servants of Allah
en.37:41: They will have a known provision
en.37:42: fruits ˹of every type˺. And they will be honoured
en.37:45: A drink ˹of pure wine˺ will be passed around to them from a flowing stream
en.37:47: It will neither harm ˹them˺, nor will they be intoxicated by it
en.37:48: And with them will be maidens of modest gaze and gorgeous eyes
en.37:49: as if they were pristine pearls
en.37:50: Then they will turn to one another inquisitively
en.37:51: One of them will say, “I once had a companion ˹in the world˺
en.37:52: who used to ask ˹me˺, ‘Do you actually believe ˹in resurrection˺
en.37:53: When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be brought to judgment?’”
en.37:54: He will ˹then˺ ask, “Would you care to see ˹his fate˺?”
en.37:55: Then he ˹and the others˺ will look and spot him in the midst of the Hellfire
en.37:56: He will ˹then˺ say, “By Allah! You nearly ruined me
en.37:57: Had it not been for the grace of my Lord, I ˹too˺ would have certainly been among those brought ˹to Hell˺.”
en.37:58: ˹Then he will ask his fellow believers,˺ “Can you imagine that we will never die
en.37:59: except our first death, nor be punished ˹like the others˺?”
en.37:60: This is truly the ultimate triumph
en.37:62: Is this ˹bliss˺ a better accommodation or the tree of Zaqqûm
en.37:63: We have surely made it a test for the wrongdoers
en.37:64: Indeed, it is a tree that grows in the depths of Hell
en.37:66: The evildoers will certainly ˹be left to˺ eat from it, filling up their bellies with it
en.37:67: Then on top of that they will be given a blend of boiling drink
en.37:68: Then they will ultimately return to ˹their place in˺ Hell
en.37:69: Indeed, they found their forefathers astray
en.37:70: so they rushed in their footsteps
en.37:71: And surely most of the earlier generations had strayed before them
en.37:72: although We had certainly sent warners among them
en.37:73: See then what was the end of those who had been warned
en.37:74: But not the chosen servants of Allah
en.37:75: Indeed, Noah cried out to Us, and how excellent are We in responding
en.37:76: We delivered him and his family from the great distress
en.37:77: and made his descendants the sole survivors
en.37:78: And We blessed him ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations
en.37:79: “Peace be upon Noah among all peoples.”
en.37:80: Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers
en.37:81: ˹For˺ he was truly one of Our faithful servants
en.37:83: And indeed, one of those who followed his way was Abraham
en.37:84: ˹Remember˺ when he came to his Lord with a pure heart
en.37:85: and said to his father and his people, “What are you worshipping
en.37:86: Is it false gods that you desire instead of Allah
en.37:87: What then do you expect from the Lord of all worlds?”
en.37:88: He later looked up to the stars ˹in contemplation˺
en.37:90: So they turned their backs on him and went away
en.37:91: Then he ˹stealthily˺ advanced towards their gods, and said ˹mockingly˺, “Will you not eat ˹your offerings˺
en.37:92: What is wrong with you that you cannot speak?”
en.37:93: Then he swiftly turned on them, striking ˹them˺ with his right hand
en.37:94: Later, his people came rushing towards him ˹furiously˺
en.37:95: He argued, “How can you worship what you carve ˹with your own hands˺
en.37:96: when it is Allah Who created you and whatever you do?”
en.37:97: They said ˹to one another˺, “Build him a furnace and cast him into the blazing fire.”
en.37:98: And so they sought to harm him, but We made them inferior
en.37:99: He later said, “I am leaving ˹in obedience˺ to my Lord. He will guide me
en.37:100: My Lord! Bless me with righteous offspring.”
en.37:101: So We gave him good news of a forbearing son
en.37:102: Then when the boy reached the age to work with him, Abraham said, “O my dear son! I have seen in a dream that I ˹must˺ sacrifice you. So tell me what you think.” He replied, “O my dear father! Do as you are commanded. Allah willing, you will find me steadfast.”
en.37:103: Then when they submitted ˹to Allah’s Will˺, and Abraham laid him on the side of his forehead ˹for sacrifice˺
en.37:104: We called out to him, “O Abraham
en.37:105: You have already fulfilled the vision.” Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers
en.37:106: That was truly a revealing test
en.37:107: And We ransomed his son with a great sacrifice
en.37:108: and blessed Abraham ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations
en.37:110: This is how We reward the good-doers
en.37:111: He was truly one of Our faithful servants
en.37:112: We ˹later˺ gave him good news of Isaac—a prophet, and one of the righteous
en.37:113: We blessed him and Isaac as well. Some of their descendants did good, while others clearly wronged themselves
en.37:114: And We certainly showed favour to Moses and Aaron
en.37:115: and delivered them and their people from the great distress
en.37:116: We helped them so it was they who prevailed
en.37:117: We gave them the clear Scripture
en.37:118: and guided them to the Straight Path
en.37:119: And We blessed them ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations
en.37:121: Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers
en.37:122: They were truly ˹two˺ of Our faithful servants
en.37:123: And Elias was indeed one of the messengers
en.37:124: ˹Remember˺ when he said to his people, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺
en.37:125: Do you call upon ˹the idol of˺ Ba’l and abandon the Best of Creators—
en.37:126: Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?”
en.37:127: But they rejected him, so they will certainly be brought ˹for punishment˺
en.37:128: But not the chosen servants of Allah
en.37:129: We blessed him ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations
en.37:131: Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers
en.37:132: He was truly one of Our faithful servants
en.37:133: And Lot was indeed one of the messengers
en.37:134: ˹Remember˺ when We delivered him and all of his family
en.37:135: except an old woman, who was one of the doomed
en.37:137: You ˹Meccans˺ certainly pass by their ruins day
en.37:138: and night. Will you not then understand
en.37:139: And Jonah was indeed one of the messengers
en.37:140: ˹Remember˺ when he fled to the overloaded ship
en.37:141: Then ˹to save it from sinking,˺ he drew straws ˹with other passengers˺. He lost ˹and was thrown overboard˺
en.37:142: Then the whale engulfed him while he was blameworthy
en.37:144: he would have certainly remained in its belly until the Day of Resurrection
en.37:145: But We cast him onto the open ˹shore˺, ˹totally˺ worn out
en.37:146: and caused a squash plant to grow over him
en.37:147: We ˹later˺ sent him ˹back˺ to ˹his city of˺ at least one hundred thousand people
en.37:148: who then believed ˹in him˺, so We allowed them enjoyment for a while
en.37:149: Ask them ˹O Prophet˺ if your Lord has daughters, while the pagans ˹prefer to˺ have sons
en.37:150: Or ˹ask them˺ if We created the angels as females right before their eyes
en.37:151: Indeed, it is one of their ˹outrageous˺ fabrications to say
en.37:152: “Allah has children.” They are simply liars
en.37:153: Has He chosen daughters over sons
en.37:154: What is the matter with you? How do you judge
en.37:155: Will you not then be mindful
en.37:156: Or do you have ˹any˺ compelling proof
en.37:157: Then bring ˹us˺ your scripture, if what you say is true
en.37:158: They have also established a ˹marital˺ relationship between Him and the jinn. Yet the jinn ˹themselves˺ know well that such people will certainly be brought ˹for punishment˺
en.37:159: Glorified is Allah far above what they claim
en.37:160: But not the chosen servants of Allah
en.37:161: Surely you ˹pagans˺ and whatever ˹idols˺ you worship
en.37:162: can never lure ˹anyone˺ away from Him
en.37:163: except those ˹destined˺ to burn in Hell
en.37:164: ˹The angels respond,˺ “There is not one of us without an assigned station ˹of worship˺
en.37:165: We are indeed the ones lined up in ranks ˹for Allah˺
en.37:166: And we are indeed the ones ˹constantly˺ glorifying ˹His praise˺.”
en.37:168: “If only we had a Reminder like ˹those of˺ earlier peoples
en.37:169: we would have truly been Allah’s devoted servants.”
en.37:170: But ˹now˺ they reject it, so they will soon know
en.37:171: Our Word has already gone forth to Our servants, the messengers
en.37:172: that they would surely be helped
en.37:173: and that Our forces will certainly prevail
en.37:174: So turn away from the deniers for a while ˹O Prophet˺
en.37:175: You will see ˹what will happen to˺ them, and they too will see
en.37:176: Do they ˹really˺ wish to hasten Our punishment
en.37:177: Yet when it descends upon them: how evil will that morning be for those who had been warned
en.37:178: And turn away from them for a while
en.37:179: You will see, and they too will see
en.37:180: Glorified is your Lord—the Lord of Honour and Power—above what they claim
en.37:182: And praise be to Allah—Lord of all worlds
en.38:1: Ṣãd. By the Quran, full of reminders
en.38:2: ˹This is the truth,˺ yet the disbelievers are ˹entrenched˺ in arrogance and opposition
en.38:3: ˹Imagine˺ how many peoples We destroyed before them, and they cried out when it was too late to escape
en.38:4: Now, the pagans are astonished that a warner has come to them from among themselves. And the disbelievers say, “This is a magician, a total liar
en.38:5: Has he reduced ˹all˺ the gods to One God? Indeed, this is something totally astonishing.”
en.38:6: The chiefs among them went forth saying, “Carry on, and stand firm in devotion to your gods. Certainly this is just a scheme ˹for power˺
en.38:7: We have never heard of this in the previous faith. This is nothing but a fabrication
en.38:8: Has the Reminder been revealed ˹only˺ to him out of ˹all of˺ us?” In fact, they are ˹only˺ in doubt of My ˹revealed˺ Reminder. In fact, ˹they do so because˺ they have not yet tasted My punishment
en.38:9: Or ˹is it because˺ they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord—the Almighty, the Giver ˹of all bounties˺
en.38:10: Or ˹is it because˺ the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between belongs to them? Let them then climb their way ˹to heaven, if their claim is true˺
en.38:11: This is just another ˹enemy˺ force bound for defeat out there
en.38:12: Before them, the people of Noah denied ˹the truth˺, as did ’Âd, Pharaoh of the mighty structures
en.38:13: Thamûd, the people of Lot, and the residents of the Forest. These were ˹all˺ enemy forces
en.38:14: Each rejected their messenger, so My punishment was justified
en.38:15: These ˹pagans˺ are awaiting nothing but a single Blast that cannot be stopped
en.38:16: They say ˹mockingly˺, “Our Lord! Hasten for us our share ˹of the punishment˺ before the Day of Reckoning.”
en.38:17: Be patient ˹O Prophet˺ with what they say. And remember Our servant, David, the man of strength. Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺
en.38:18: We truly subjected the mountains to hymn ˹Our praises˺ along with him in the evening and after sunrise
en.38:19: And ˹We subjected˺ the birds, flocking together. All turned to him ˹echoing his hymns˺
en.38:20: We strengthened his kingship, and gave him wisdom and sound judgment
en.38:21: Has the story of the two plaintiffs, who scaled the ˹wall of David’s˺ sanctuary, reached you ˹O Prophet˺
en.38:22: When they came into David’s presence, he was startled by them. They said, “Have no fear. ˹We are merely˺ two in a dispute: one of us has wronged the other. So judge between us with truth—do not go beyond ˹it˺—and guide us to the right way
en.38:23: This is my brother. He has ninety-nine sheep while I have ˹only˺ one. ˹Still˺ he asked me to give it up to him, overwhelming me with ˹his˺ argument.”
en.38:24: David ˹eventually˺ ruled, “He has definitely wronged you in demanding ˹to add˺ your sheep to his. And certainly many partners wrong each other, except those who believe and do good—but how few are they!” Then David realized that We had tested him so he asked for his Lord’s forgiveness, fell down in prostration, and turned ˹to Him in repentance˺
en.38:25: So We forgave that for him. And he will indeed have ˹a status of˺ closeness to Us and an honourable destination
en.38:26: ˹We instructed him:˺ “O David! We have surely made you an authority in the land, so judge between people with truth. And do not follow ˹your˺ desires or they will lead you astray from Allah’s Way. Surely those who go astray from Allah’s Way will suffer a severe punishment for neglecting the Day of Reckoning.”
en.38:27: We have not created the heavens and earth and everything in between without purpose—as the disbelievers think. So woe to the disbelievers because of the Fire
en.38:28: Or should We treat those who believe and do good like those who make mischief throughout the land? Or should We treat the righteous like the wicked
en.38:29: ˹This is˺ a blessed Book which We have revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺ so that they may contemplate its verses, and people of reason may be mindful
en.38:30: And We blessed David with Solomon—what an excellent servant ˹he was˺! Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺
en.38:31: ˹Remember˺ when the well-trained, swift horses were paraded before him in the evening
en.38:32: He then proclaimed, “I am truly in love with ˹these˺ fine things out of remembrance for Allah,” until they went out of sight
en.38:33: ˹He ordered,˺ “Bring them back to me!” Then he began to rub down their legs and necks
en.38:34: And indeed, We tested Solomon, placing a ˹deformed˺ body on his throne, then he turned ˹to Allah in repentance˺
en.38:35: He prayed, “My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me an authority that will never be matched by anyone after me. You are indeed the Giver ˹of all bounties˺.”
en.38:36: So We subjected to him the wind, blowing gently at his command to wherever he pleased
en.38:37: And ˹We subjected to him˺ every builder and diver of the jinn
en.38:38: and others bound together in chains
en.38:39: ˹Allah said,˺ “This is Our gift, so give or withhold ˹as you wish˺, never to be called to account.”
en.38:40: And he will indeed have ˹a status of˺ closeness to Us and an honourable destination
en.38:41: And remember Our servant Job, when he cried out to his Lord, “Satan has afflicted me with distress and suffering.”
en.38:42: ˹We responded,˺ “Stomp your foot: ˹now˺ here is a cool ˹and refreshing˺ spring for washing and drinking.”
en.38:43: And We gave him back his family, twice as many, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for people of reason
en.38:44: ˹And We said to him,˺ “Take in your hand a bundle of grass, and strike ˹your wife˺ with it, and do not break your oath.” We truly found him patient. What an excellent servant ˹he was˺! Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺
en.38:45: And remember Our servants: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the men of strength and insight
en.38:46: We truly chose them for the honour of proclaiming the Hereafter
en.38:47: And in Our sight they are truly among the chosen and the finest
en.38:48: Also remember Ishmael, Elisha, and Ⱬul-Kifl. All are among the best
en.38:49: This is ˹all˺ a reminder. And the righteous will certainly have an honourable destination
en.38:50: the Gardens of Eternity, whose gates will be open for them
en.38:51: There they will recline, calling for abundant fruit and drink
en.38:52: And with them will be maidens of modest gaze and equal age
en.38:53: This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning
en.38:54: This is indeed Our provision that will never end
en.38:55: That is that. And the transgressors will certainly have the worst destination
en.38:56: Hell, where they will burn. What an evil place to rest
en.38:57: Let them then taste this: boiling water and ˹oozing˺ pus
en.38:58: and other torments of the same sort
en.38:59: ˹The misleaders will say to one another,˺ “Here is a crowd ˹of followers˺ being thrown in with us. They are not welcome, ˹for˺ they ˹too˺ will burn in the Fire.”
en.38:60: The followers will respond, “No! You are not welcome! You brought this upon us. What an evil place for settlement!”
en.38:61: Adding, “Our Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, double their punishment in the Fire.”
en.38:62: The tyrants will ask ˹one another˺, “But why do we not see those we considered to be lowly
en.38:63: Were we wrong in mocking them ˹in the world˺? Or do our eyes ˹just˺ fail to see them ˹in the Fire˺?”
en.38:64: This dispute between the residents of the Fire will certainly come to pass
en.38:65: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I am only a warner. And there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Allah—the One, the Supreme
en.38:66: ˹He is the˺ Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between—the Almighty, Most Forgiving.”
en.38:68: from which you ˹pagans˺ are turning away.”
en.38:69: ˹And say,˺ “I had no knowledge of the highest assembly ˹in heaven˺ when they differed ˹concerning Adam˺
en.38:70: What is revealed to me is that I am only sent with a clear warning.”
en.38:71: ˹Remember, O Prophet˺ when your Lord said to the angels, “I am going to create a human being from clay
en.38:72: So when I have fashioned him and had a spirit of My Own ˹creation˺ breathed into him, fall down in prostration to him.”
en.38:73: So the angels prostrated all together—
en.38:74: but not Iblîs, who acted arrogantly, becoming unfaithful
en.38:75: Allah asked, “O Iblîs! What prevented you from prostrating to what I created with My Own Hands? Did you ˹just˺ become proud? Or have you always been arrogant?”
en.38:76: He replied, “I am better than he is: You created me from fire and him from clay.”
en.38:77: Allah commanded, “Then get out of Paradise, for you are truly cursed
en.38:78: And surely upon you is My condemnation until the Day of Judgment.”
en.38:79: Satan appealed, “My Lord! Then delay my end until the Day of their resurrection.”
en.38:82: Satan said, “By Your Glory! I will certainly mislead them all
en.38:83: except Your chosen servants among them.”
en.38:84: Allah concluded, “The truth is—and I ˹only˺ say the truth—
en.38:85: I will surely fill up Hell with you and whoever follows you from among them, all together.”
en.38:86: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹Quran˺, nor do I pretend to be someone I am not
en.38:87: It is only a reminder to the whole world
en.38:88: And you will certainly know its truth before long.”
en.39:1: The revelation of this Book is from Allah—the Almighty, All-Wise
en.39:2: Indeed, We have sent down the Book to you ˹O Prophet˺ in truth, so worship Allah ˹alone˺, being sincerely devoted to Him
en.39:3: Indeed, sincere devotion is due ˹only˺ to Allah. As for those who take other lords besides Him, ˹saying,˺ “We worship them only so they may bring us closer to Allah,” surely Allah will judge between all regarding what they differed about. Allah certainly does not guide whoever persists in lying and disbelief
en.39:4: Had it been Allah’s Will to have offspring, He could have chosen whatever He willed of His creation. Glory be to Him! He is Allah—the One, the Supreme
en.39:5: He created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. He wraps the night around the day, and wraps the day around the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term. He is truly the Almighty, Most Forgiving
en.39:6: He created you ˹all˺ from a single soul, then from it He made its mate. And He produced for you four pairs of cattle. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers ˹in stages˺, one development after another, in three layers of darkness. That is Allah—your Lord! All authority belongs to Him. There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. How can you then be turned away
en.39:7: If you disbelieve, then ˹know that˺ Allah is truly not in need of you, nor does He approve of disbelief from His servants. But if you become grateful ˹through faith˺, He will appreciate that from you. No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of what you used to do. He certainly knows best what is ˹hidden˺ in the heart
en.39:8: When one is touched with hardship, they cry out to their Lord, turning to Him ˹alone˺. But as soon as He showers them with blessings from Him, they ˹totally˺ forget the One they had cried to earlier, and set up equals to Allah to mislead ˹others˺ from His Way. Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Enjoy your disbelief for a little while! You will certainly be one of the inmates of the Fire.”
en.39:9: ˹Are they better˺ or those who worship ˹their Lord˺ devoutly in the hours of the night, prostrating and standing, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of their Lord? Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Are those who know equal to those who do not know?” None will be mindful ˹of this˺ except people of reason
en.39:10: Say ˹O Prophet, that Allah says˺, “O My servants who believe! Be mindful of your Lord. Those who do good in this world will have a good reward. And Allah’s earth is spacious. Only those who endure patiently will be given their reward without limit.”
en.39:11: Say, “I am commanded to worship Allah, being sincerely devoted to Him ˹alone˺
en.39:12: And I am commanded to be the first of those who submit ˹to His Will˺.”
en.39:13: Say, “I truly fear—if I were to disobey my Lord—the torment of a tremendous Day.”
en.39:14: Say, “It is ˹only˺ Allah that I worship, being sincere in my devotion to Him
en.39:15: Worship then whatever ˹gods˺ you want instead of Him.” Say, “The ˹true˺ losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on Judgment Day. That is indeed the clearest loss.”
en.39:16: They will have layers of fire above and below them. That is what Allah warns His servants with. So fear Me, O My servants
en.39:17: And those who shun the worship of false gods, turning to Allah ˹alone˺, will have good news. So give good news to My servants ˹O Prophet˺—
en.39:18: those who listen to what is said and follow the best of it. These are the ones ˹rightly˺ guided by Allah, and these are ˹truly˺ the people of reason
en.39:19: What about those against whom the decree of torment has been justified? Is it you ˹O Prophet˺ who will then save those bound for the Fire
en.39:20: But those mindful of their Lord will have ˹elevated˺ mansions, built one above the other, under which rivers flow. ˹That is˺ the promise of Allah. ˹And˺ Allah never fails in ˹His˺ promise
en.39:21: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky—channelling it through streams in the earth—then produces with it crops of various colours, then they dry up and you see them wither, and then He reduces them to chaff? Surely in this is a reminder for people of reason
en.39:22: Can ˹the misguided be like˺ those whose hearts Allah has opened to Islam, so they are enlightened by their Lord? So woe to those whose hearts are hardened at the remembrance of Allah! It is they who are clearly astray
en.39:23: ˹It is˺ Allah ˹Who˺ has sent down the best message—a Book of perfect consistency and repeated lessons—which causes the skin ˹and hearts˺ of those who fear their Lord to tremble, then their skin and hearts soften at the mention of ˹the mercy of˺ Allah. That is the guidance of Allah, through which He guides whoever He wills. But whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide
en.39:24: Are those who will only have their ˹bare˺ faces to shield themselves from the awful torment on Judgment Day ˹better than those in Paradise˺? It will ˹then˺ be said to the wrongdoers: “Reap what you sowed!”
en.39:25: Those before them ˹also˺ rejected ˹the truth˺, then the torment came upon them from where they least expected
en.39:26: So Allah made them taste humiliation in this worldly life, but far worse is the punishment of the Hereafter, if only they knew
en.39:27: We have certainly set forth every ˹kind of˺ lesson for people in this Quran, so perhaps they will be mindful
en.39:28: ˹It is˺ a Quran ˹revealed˺ in Arabic without any crookedness, so perhaps they will be conscious ˹of Allah˺
en.39:29: Allah sets forth the parable of a slave owned by several quarrelsome masters, and a slave owned by only one master. Are they equal in condition? Praise be to Allah! In fact, most of them do not know
en.39:30: You ˹O Prophet˺ will certainly die, and they will die too
en.39:31: Then on the Day of Judgment you will ˹all settle your˺ dispute before your Lord
en.39:32: Who then does more wrong than those who lie about Allah and reject the truth after it has reached them? Is Hell not a ˹fitting˺ home for the disbelievers
en.39:33: And the one who has brought the truth and those who embrace it—it is they who are the righteous
en.39:34: They will have whatever they desire with their Lord. That is the reward of the good-doers
en.39:35: As such, Allah will absolve them of ˹even˺ the worst of what they did and reward them according to the best of what they used to do
en.39:36: Is Allah not sufficient for His servant? Yet they threaten you with other ˹powerless˺ gods besides Him! Whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide
en.39:37: And whoever Allah guides, none can lead astray. Is Allah not Almighty, capable of punishment
en.39:38: If you ask them ˹O Prophet˺ who created the heavens and the earth, they will certainly say, “Allah!” Ask ˹them˺, “Consider then whatever ˹idols˺ you invoke besides Allah: if it was Allah’s Will to harm me, could they undo that harm? Or if He willed ˹some˺ mercy for me, could they withhold His mercy?” Say, “Allah is sufficient for me. In Him ˹alone˺ the faithful put their trust.”
en.39:39: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “O my people! Persist in your ways, for I ˹too˺ will persist in mine. You will soon come to know
en.39:40: who will be visited by a humiliating torment ˹in this life˺ and overwhelmed by an everlasting punishment ˹in the next˺.”
en.39:41: Surely We have revealed to you the Book ˹O Prophet˺ with the truth for humanity. So whoever chooses to be guided, it is for their own good. And whoever chooses to stray, it is only to their own loss. You are not a keeper over them
en.39:42: ˹It is˺ Allah ˹Who˺ calls back the souls ˹of people˺ upon their death as well as ˹the souls˺ of the living during their sleep. Then He keeps those for whom He has ordained death, and releases the others until ˹their˺ appointed time. Surely in this are signs for people who reflect
en.39:43: Or have they taken others besides Allah as intercessors? Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “˹Would they do so,˺ even though those ˹idols˺ have neither authority nor intelligence?”
en.39:44: Say, “All intercession belongs to Allah ˹alone˺. To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. Then to Him you will ˹all˺ be returned.”
en.39:45: Yet when Allah alone is mentioned, the hearts of those who disbelieve in the Hereafter are filled with disgust. But as soon as those ˹gods˺ other than Him are mentioned, they are filled with joy
en.39:46: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “O Allah—Originator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the seen and unseen! You will judge between Your servants regarding their differences.”
en.39:47: Even if the wrongdoers were to possess everything in the world twice over, they would certainly offer it to ransom themselves from the horrible punishment on Judgment Day, for they will see from Allah what they had never expected
en.39:48: And the evil ˹consequences˺ of their deeds will unfold before them, and they will be overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule
en.39:49: When one is touched with hardship, they cry out to Us ˹alone˺. Then when We shower Our blessings upon them, they say, “I have been granted all this only because of ˹my˺ knowledge.” Not at all! It is ˹no more than˺ a test. But most of them do not know
en.39:50: The same had already been said by those ˹destroyed˺ before them, but their ˹worldly˺ gains were of no benefit to them
en.39:51: So the evil ˹consequences˺ of their deeds overtook them. And the wrongdoers among these ˹pagans˺ will be overtaken by the evil ˹consequences˺ of their deeds. And they will have no escape
en.39:52: Do they not know that Allah gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills? Surely in this are signs for people who believe
en.39:53: Say, ˹O Prophet, that Allah says,˺ “O My servants who have exceeded the limits against their souls! Do not lose hope in Allah’s mercy, for Allah certainly forgives all sins. He is indeed the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.39:54: Turn to your Lord ˹in repentance˺, and ˹fully˺ submit to Him before the punishment reaches you, ˹for˺ then you will not be helped
en.39:55: Follow ˹the Quran,˺ the best of what has been revealed to you from your Lord, before the punishment takes you by surprise while you are unaware
en.39:56: so that no ˹sinful˺ soul will say ˹on Judgment Day˺, ‘Woe to me for neglecting ˹my duties towards˺ Allah, while ridiculing ˹the truth˺.’
en.39:57: Or ˹a soul will˺ say, ‘If only Allah had guided me, I would have certainly been one of the righteous.’
en.39:58: Or say, upon seeing the torment, ‘If only I had a second chance, I would have been one of the good-doers.’
en.39:59: Not at all! My revelations had already come to you, but you rejected them, acted arrogantly, and were one of the disbelievers.”
en.39:60: On the Day of Judgment you will see those who lied about Allah with their faces gloomy. Is Hell not a ˹fitting˺ home for the arrogant
en.39:61: And Allah will deliver those who were mindful ˹of Him˺ to their place of ˹ultimate˺ triumph. No evil will touch them, nor will they grieve
en.39:62: Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is the Maintainer of everything
en.39:63: To Him belong the keys ˹of the treasuries˺ of the heavens and the earth. As for those who rejected the signs of Allah, it is they who will be the ˹true˺ losers
en.39:64: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Are you urging me to worship ˹anyone˺ other than Allah, O ignorant ones?”
en.39:65: It has already been revealed to you—and to those ˹prophets˺ before you—that if you associate others ˹with Allah˺, your deeds will certainly be void and you will truly be one of the losers
en.39:66: Rather, worship Allah ˹alone˺ and be one of the grateful
en.39:67: They have not shown Allah His proper reverence—when on the Day of Judgment the ˹whole˺ earth will be in His Grip, and the heavens will be rolled up in His Right Hand. Glorified and Exalted is He above what they associate ˹with Him˺
en.39:68: The Trumpet will be blown and all those in the heavens and all those on the earth will fall dead, except those Allah wills ˹to spare˺. Then it will be blown again and they will rise up at once, looking on ˹in anticipation˺
en.39:69: The earth will shine with the light of its Lord, the record ˹of deeds˺ will be laid ˹open˺, the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forward—and judgment will be passed on all with fairness. None will be wronged
en.39:70: Every soul will be paid in full for its deeds, for Allah knows best what they have done
en.39:71: Those who disbelieved will be driven to Hell in ˹successive˺ groups. When they arrive there, its gates will be opened and its keepers will ask them: “Did messengers not come to you from among yourselves, reciting to you the revelations of your Lord and warning you of the coming of this Day of yours?” The disbelievers will cry, “Yes ˹indeed˺! But the decree of torment has come to pass against the disbelievers.”
en.39:72: It will be said to them, “Enter the gates of Hell, to stay there forever.” What an evil home for the arrogant
en.39:73: And those who were mindful of their Lord will be led to Paradise in ˹successive˺ groups. When they arrive at its ˹already˺ open gates, its keepers will say, “Peace be upon you! You have done well, so come in, to stay forever.”
en.39:74: The righteous will say, “Praise be to Allah Who has fulfilled His promise to us, and made us inherit the ˹everlasting˺ land to settle in Paradise wherever we please.” How excellent is the reward of those who work ˹righteousness˺
en.39:75: You will see the angels all around the Throne, glorifying the praises of their Lord, for judgment will have been passed on all with fairness. And it will be said, “Praise be to Allah—Lord of all worlds!”
en.40:2: The revelation of this Book is from Allah—the Almighty, All-Knowing
en.40:3: the Forgiver of sin and Accepter of repentance, the Severe in punishment, and Infinite in bounty. There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. To Him ˹alone˺ is the final return
en.40:4: None disputes the signs of Allah except the disbelievers, so do not be deceived by their prosperity throughout the land
en.40:5: Before them, the people of Noah denied ˹the truth˺, as did ˹other˺ enemy forces afterwards. Every community plotted against its prophet to seize him, and argued in falsehood, ˹hoping˺ to discredit the truth with it. So I seized them. And how ˹horrible˺ was My punishment
en.40:6: And so your Lord’s decree has been proven true against the disbelievers—that they will be the inmates of the Fire
en.40:7: Those ˹angels˺ who carry the Throne and those around it glorify the praises of their Lord, have faith in Him, and seek forgiveness for the believers, ˹praying:˺ “Our Lord! You encompass everything in ˹Your˺ mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your Way, and protect them from the torment of the Hellfire
en.40:8: Our Lord! Admit them into the Gardens of Eternity which You have promised them, along with the righteous among their parents, spouses, and descendants. You ˹alone˺ are truly the Almighty, All-Wise
en.40:9: And protect them from ˹the consequences of their˺ evil deeds. For whoever You protect from the evil of their deeds on that Day will have been shown Your mercy. That is ˹truly˺ the ultimate triumph.”
en.40:10: Indeed, it will be announced to the disbelievers, “Allah’s contempt for you—as you disbelieved when invited to belief—was far worse than your contempt for one another ˹Today˺.”
en.40:11: They will plead, “Our Lord! You made us lifeless twice, and gave us life twice. Now we confess our sins. So is there any way out?”
en.40:12: ˹They will be told,˺ “˹No!˺ This is because when Allah alone was invoked, you ˹staunchly˺ disbelieved. But when others were associated with Him ˹in worship˺, you ˹readily˺ believed. So ˹Today˺ judgment belongs to Allah ˹alone˺—the Most High, All-Great.”
en.40:13: He is the One Who shows you His signs and sends down ˹rain as˺ a provision for you from the sky. ˹But˺ none will be mindful except those who turn ˹to Him˺
en.40:14: So call upon Allah with sincere devotion, even to the dismay of the disbelievers
en.40:15: ˹He is˺ Highly Exalted in rank, Lord of the Throne. He sends down the revelation by His command to whoever He wills of His servants to warn ˹all˺ of the Day of Meeting—
en.40:16: the Day all will appear ˹before Allah˺. Nothing about them will be hidden from Him. ˹He will ask,˺ “Who does all authority belong to this Day? To Allah—the One, the Supreme
en.40:17: Today every soul will be rewarded for what it has done. No injustice Today! Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.”
en.40:18: Warn them ˹O Prophet˺ of the approaching Day when the hearts will jump into the throats, suppressing distress. The wrongdoers will have neither a close friend nor intercessor to be heard
en.40:19: Allah ˹even˺ knows the sly glances of the eyes and whatever the hearts conceal
en.40:20: And Allah judges with the truth, while those ˹idols˺ they invoke besides Him cannot judge at all. Indeed, Allah ˹alone˺ is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing
en.40:21: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those ˹destroyed˺ before them? They were far superior in might and ˹richer in˺ monuments throughout the land. But Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no protector from Allah
en.40:22: That was because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs, but they persisted in disbelief. So Allah seized them. Surely He is All-Powerful, severe in punishment
en.40:23: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs and compelling proof
en.40:24: to Pharaoh, Hamân, and Korah. But they responded: “Magician! Total liar!”
en.40:25: Then, when he came to them with the truth from Us, they said, “Kill the sons of those who believe with him and keep their women.” But the plotting of the disbelievers was only in vain
en.40:26: And Pharaoh said, “Let me kill Moses, and let him call upon his Lord! I truly fear that he may change your traditions or cause mischief in the land.”
en.40:27: Moses replied, “I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant person who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.”
en.40:28: A believing man from Pharaoh’s people, who was hiding his faith, argued, “Will you kill a man ˹only˺ for saying: ‘My Lord is Allah,’ while he has in fact come to you with clear proofs from your Lord? If he is a liar, it will be to his own loss. But if he is truthful, then you will be afflicted with some of what he is threatening you with. Surely Allah does not guide whoever is a transgressor, a total liar
en.40:29: O my people! Authority belongs to you today, reigning supreme in the land. But who would help us against the torment of Allah, if it were to befall us?” Pharaoh assured ˹his people˺, “I am telling you only what I believe, and I am leading you only to the way of guidance.”
en.40:30: And the man who believed cautioned, “O my people! I truly fear for you the doom of ˹earlier˺ enemy forces—
en.40:31: like the fate of the people of Noah, ’Ȃd, Thamûd, and those after them. For Allah would never will to wrong ˹His˺ servants
en.40:32: O my people! I truly fear for you the Day all will be crying out ˹to each other˺—
en.40:33: the Day you will ˹try in vain to˺ turn your backs and run away, with no one to protect you from Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide
en.40:34: Joseph already came to you earlier with clear proofs, yet you never ceased to doubt what he came to you with. When he died you said, ‘Allah will never send a messenger after him.’ This is how Allah leaves every transgressor and doubter to stray—
en.40:35: those who dispute Allah’s signs with no proof given to them. How despicable is that for Allah and the believers! This is how Allah seals the heart of every arrogant tyrant.”
en.40:36: Pharaoh ordered, “O Hamân! Build me a high tower so I may reach the pathways
en.40:37: leading up to the heavens and look for the God of Moses, although I am sure he is a liar.” And so Pharaoh’s evil deeds were made so appealing to him that he was hindered from the ˹Right˺ Way. But the plotting of Pharaoh was only in vain
en.40:38: And the man who believed urged, “O my people! Follow me, ˹and˺ I will lead you to the Way of Guidance
en.40:39: O my people! This worldly life is only ˹a fleeting˺ enjoyment, whereas the Hereafter is truly the home of settlement
en.40:40: Whoever does an evil deed will only be paid back with its equivalent. And whoever does good, whether male or female, and is a believer, they will enter Paradise, where they will be provided for without limit
en.40:41: O my people! How is it that I invite you to salvation, while you invite me to the Fire
en.40:42: You invite me to disbelieve in Allah and associate with Him what I have no knowledge of, while I invite you to the Almighty, Most Forgiving
en.40:43: There is no doubt that whatever ˹idols˺ you invite me to ˹worship˺ are not worthy to be invoked either in this world or the Hereafter. ˹Undoubtedly,˺ our return is to Allah, and the transgressors will be the inmates of the Fire
en.40:44: You will remember what I say to you, and I entrust my affairs to Allah. Surely Allah is All-Seeing of all ˹His˺ servants.”
en.40:45: So Allah protected him from the evil of their schemes. And Pharaoh’s people were overwhelmed by an evil punishment
en.40:46: they are exposed to the Fire ˹in their graves˺ morning and evening. And on the Day the Hour will be established ˹it will be said˺, “Admit Pharaoh’s people into the harshest punishment ˹of Hell˺.”
en.40:47: ˹Consider the Day˺ when they will dispute in the Fire, and the lowly ˹followers˺ will appeal to the arrogant ˹leaders˺, “We were your ˹dedicated˺ followers, will you then shield us from a portion of the Fire?”
en.40:48: The arrogant will say, “We are all in it! ˹For˺ Allah has already passed judgment over ˹His˺ servants.”
en.40:49: And those in the Fire will cry out to the keepers of Hell, “Pray to your Lord to lighten the torment for us ˹even˺ for one day!”
en.40:50: The keepers will reply, “Did your messengers not ˹constantly˺ come to you with clear proofs?” They will say, “Yes ˹they did˺.” The keepers will say, “Then pray! Though the prayer of the disbelievers is only in vain.”
en.40:51: We certainly help Our messengers and the believers, ˹both˺ in this worldly life and on the Day the witnesses will stand forth ˹for testimony˺—
en.40:52: the Day the wrongdoers’ excuses will be of no benefit to them. They will be condemned, and will have the worst outcome
en.40:53: And indeed, We gave Moses ˹true˺ guidance, and made the Children of Israel inherit the Scripture—
en.40:54: a guide and a reminder to people of reason
en.40:55: So be patient ˹O Prophet˺, ˹for˺ Allah’s promise is certainly true. Seek forgiveness for your shortcomings. And glorify the praises of your Lord morning and evening
en.40:56: Surely those who dispute Allah’s signs—with no proof given to them—have nothing in their hearts but greed for dominance, which they will never attain. So seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He alone is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing
en.40:57: The creation of the heavens and the earth is certainly greater than the re-creation of humankind, but most people do not know
en.40:58: Those blind ˹to the truth˺ and those who can see are not equal, nor are those who believe and do good ˹equal˺ to those who do evil. Yet you are hardly mindful
en.40:59: The Hour is certainly coming, there is no doubt about it. But most people do not believe
en.40:60: Your Lord has proclaimed, “Call upon Me, I will respond to you. Surely those who are too proud to worship Me will enter Hell, fully humbled.”
en.40:61: It is Allah Who has made the night for you to rest in and the day bright. Surely Allah is ever Bountiful to humanity, but most people are ungrateful
en.40:62: That is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things. There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. How can you then be deluded ˹from the truth˺
en.40:63: This is how those who used to reject Allah’s signs were ˹also˺ deluded
en.40:64: It is Allah Who made the earth a place of settlement for you and the sky a canopy. He shaped you ˹in the womb˺, perfecting your form. And He has provided you with what is good and lawful. That is Allah—your Lord. So Blessed is Allah, Lord of all worlds
en.40:65: He is the Ever-Living. There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. So call upon Him with sincere devotion, ˹saying,˺ “All praise is for Allah—Lord of all worlds.”
en.40:66: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I have been forbidden to worship those ˹idols˺ you worship besides Allah, since clear proofs have come to me from my Lord. And I have been commanded to ˹fully˺ submit to the Lord of all worlds.”
en.40:67: He is the One Who created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then ˹developed you into˺ a clinging clot ˹of blood˺, then He brings you forth as infants, so that you may reach your prime, and become old—though some of you ˹may˺ die sooner—reaching an appointed time, so perhaps you may understand ˹Allah’s power˺
en.40:68: He is the One Who gives life and causes death. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, “Be!” And it is
en.40:69: Have you not seen how those who dispute Allah’s signs are turned away
en.40:70: ˹They are˺ the ones who reject this Book and all ˹scriptures˺ We sent Our messengers with. So they will know ˹the consequences˺
en.40:71: when shackles will be around their necks and chains ˹on their legs˺. They will be dragged
en.40:72: through boiling water, then burned in the Fire ˹as fuel˺
en.40:73: Then they will be asked, “Where are those ˹idols˺ you used to associate
en.40:74: with Allah?” They will cry, “They have ˹all˺ failed us. In fact, we did not invoke anything ˹real˺ before.” This is how Allah leaves the disbelievers to stray
en.40:75: ˹They will be told,˺ “This ˹punishment˺ is for being prideful on earth unjustly and for acting arrogantly
en.40:76: Enter the gates of Hell, to stay there forever. What an evil home for the arrogant!”
en.40:77: So be patient ˹O Prophet˺. Surely Allah’s promise is true. Whether We show you some of what We threaten them with, or cause you to die ˹before that˺, to Us they will ˹all˺ be returned
en.40:78: We already sent messengers before you. We have told you the stories of some of them, while others We have not. It was not for any messenger to bring a sign without Allah’s permission. But when Allah’s decree comes, judgment will be passed with fairness, and the people of falsehood will then be in ˹total˺ loss
en.40:79: It is Allah Who made cattle for you so that you may ride some and eat others
en.40:80: Also, you find in them ˹other˺ benefits. And by means of them you may reach destinations you desire. And you are carried upon ˹some of˺ them and upon ships
en.40:81: And He shows you His signs. Now which of Allah’s signs will you deny
en.40:82: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those who were ˹destroyed˺ before them? They were far superior in might and ˹richer in˺ monuments throughout the land, but their ˹worldly˺ gains were of no benefit to them
en.40:83: When their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they were prideful in whatever ˹worldly˺ knowledge they had, and were ˹ultimately˺ overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule
en.40:84: When they saw Our punishment, they cried, “˹Now˺ we believe in Allah alone and reject what we had been associating with Him!”
en.40:85: But their faith was of no benefit to them when they saw Our torment. This has ˹always˺ been Allah’s way ˹of dealing˺ with His ˹wicked˺ servants. Then and there the disbelievers were in ˹total˺ loss
en.41:2: ˹This is˺ a revelation from the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful
en.41:3: ˹It is˺ a Book whose verses are perfectly explained—a Quran in Arabic for people who know
en.41:4: delivering good news and warning. Yet most of them turn away, so they do not hear
en.41:5: They say, “Our hearts are veiled against what you are calling us to, there is deafness in our ears, and there is a barrier between us and you. So do ˹whatever you want˺ and so shall we!”
en.41:6: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I am only a man like you, ˹but˺ it has been revealed to me that your God is only One God. So take the Straight Way towards Him, and seek His forgiveness. And woe to the polytheists—
en.41:7: those who do not pay alms-tax and are in denial of the Hereafter
en.41:8: ˹But˺ those who believe and do good will certainly have a never-ending reward
en.41:9: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “How can you disbelieve in the One Who created the earth in two Days? And how can you set up equals with Him? That is the Lord of all worlds
en.41:10: He placed on the earth firm mountains, standing high, showered His blessings upon it, and ordained ˹all˺ its means of sustenance—totaling four Days exactly—for all who ask
en.41:11: Then He turned towards the heaven when it was ˹still like˺ smoke, saying to it and to the earth, ‘Submit, willingly or unwillingly.’ They both responded, ‘We submit willingly.’
en.41:12: So He formed the heaven into seven heavens in two Days, assigning to each its mandate. And We adorned the lowest heaven with ˹stars like˺ lamps ˹for beauty˺ and for protection. That is the design of the Almighty, All-Knowing.”
en.41:13: If they turn away, then say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I warn you of a ˹mighty˺ blast, like the one that befell ’Ȃd and Thamûd.”
en.41:14: The messengers had come to them from all angles, ˹proclaiming,˺ “Worship none but Allah.” They responded, “Had our Lord willed, He could have easily sent down angels ˹instead˺. So we totally reject what you have been sent with.”
en.41:15: As for ’Ȃd, they acted arrogantly throughout the land with no right, boasting, “Who is superior to us in might?” Did they not see that Allah ˹Himself˺, Who created them, was far superior to them in might? Still they persisted in denying Our signs
en.41:16: So We sent against them a furious wind, for ˹several˺ miserable days, to make them taste a humiliating punishment in this worldly life. But far more humiliating will be the punishment of the Hereafter. And they will not be helped
en.41:17: As for Thamûd, We showed them guidance, but they preferred blindness over guidance. So the blast of a disgracing punishment overtook them for what they used to commit
en.41:18: And We delivered those who were faithful and were mindful ˹of Allah˺
en.41:19: ˹Consider˺ the Day ˹when˺ the enemies of Allah will be gathered for the Fire, all driven in ranks
en.41:20: When they reach it, their ears, eyes, and skin will testify against what they used to do
en.41:21: They will ask their skin ˹furiously˺, “Why have you testified against us?” It will say, “We have been made to speak by Allah, Who causes all things to speak. He ˹is the One Who˺ created you the first time, and to Him you were bound to return
en.41:22: You did not ˹bother to˺ hide yourselves from your ears, eyes, and skin to prevent them from testifying against you. Rather, you assumed that Allah did not know much of what you used to do
en.41:23: It was that ˹false˺ assumption you entertained about your Lord that has brought about your doom, so you have become losers.”
en.41:24: Even if they endure patiently, the Fire will ˹always˺ be their home. And if they ˹beg to˺ appease ˹their Lord˺, they will never be allowed to
en.41:25: We placed at their disposal ˹evil˺ associates who made their past and future ˹misdeeds˺ appealing to them. ˹So˺ the fate of earlier communities of jinn and humans has been justified against them ˹as well˺, ˹for˺ they were truly losers
en.41:26: The disbelievers advised ˹one another˺, “Do not listen to this Quran but drown it out so that you may prevail.”
en.41:27: So We will certainly make the disbelievers taste a severe punishment, and We will surely repay them according to the worst of their deeds
en.41:28: That is the reward of Allah’s enemies: the Fire, which will be their eternal home—a ˹fitting˺ reward for their denial of Our revelations
en.41:29: The disbelievers will ˹then˺ cry, “Our Lord! Show us those jinn and humans who led us astray: we will put them under our feet so that they will be among the lowest ˹in Hell˺.”
en.41:30: Surely those who say, “Our Lord is Allah,” and then remain steadfast, the angels descend upon them, ˹saying,˺ “Do not fear, nor grieve. Rather, rejoice in the good news of Paradise, which you have been promised
en.41:31: We are your supporters in this worldly life and in the Hereafter. There you will have whatever your souls desire, and there you will have whatever you ask for
en.41:32: an accommodation from the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful ˹Lord˺.”
en.41:33: And whose words are better than someone who calls ˹others˺ to Allah, does good, and says, “I am truly one of those who submit.”
en.41:34: Good and evil cannot be equal. Respond ˹to evil˺ with what is best, then the one you are in a feud with will be like a close friend
en.41:35: But this cannot be attained except by those who are patient and who are truly fortunate
en.41:36: And if you are tempted by Satan, then seek refuge with Allah. Indeed, He ˹alone˺ is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing
en.41:37: Among His signs are the day and the night, the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun or the moon, but prostrate to Allah, Who created them ˹all˺, if you ˹truly˺ worship Him ˹alone˺
en.41:38: But if the pagans are too proud, then ˹let them know that˺ those ˹angels˺ nearest to your Lord glorify Him day and night, and never grow weary
en.41:39: And among His signs is that you see the earth devoid of life, but as soon as We send down rain upon it, it begins to stir ˹to life˺ and swell. Indeed, the One Who revives it can easily revive the dead. He is certainly Most Capable of everything
en.41:40: Indeed, those who abuse Our revelations are not hidden from Us. Who is better: the one who will be cast into the Fire or the one who will be secure on Judgment Day? Do whatever you want. He is certainly All-Seeing of what you do
en.41:41: Indeed, those who deny the Reminder after it has come to them ˹are doomed˺, for it is truly a mighty Book
en.41:42: It cannot be proven false from any angle. ˹It is˺ a revelation from the ˹One Who is˺ All-Wise, Praiseworthy
en.41:43: ˹O Prophet!˺ Nothing is said to you ˹by the deniers˺ except what was already said to the messengers before you. Surely your Lord is ˹the Lord˺ of forgiveness and painful punishment
en.41:44: Had We revealed it as a non-Arabic Quran, they would have certainly argued, “If only its verses were made clear ˹in our language˺. What! A non-Arabic revelation for an Arab audience!” Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “It is a guide and a healing to the believers. As for those who disbelieve, there is deafness in their ears and blindness to it ˹in their hearts˺. It is as if they are being called from a faraway place.”
en.41:45: Indeed, We had given Moses the Scripture, but differences arose regarding it. Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord, their differences would have been settled ˹at once˺. They are truly in alarming doubt about it
en.41:46: Whoever does good, it is to their own benefit. And whoever does evil, it is to their own loss. Your Lord is never unjust to ˹His˺ creation
en.41:47: With Him ˹alone˺ is the knowledge of the Hour. No fruit comes out of its husk, nor does a female conceive or deliver without His knowledge. And ˹consider˺ the Day He will call to them, “Where are My ˹so-called˺ associate-gods?” They will cry, “We declare before you that none of us testifies to that ˹any longer˺.”
en.41:48: Whatever ˹idols˺ they used to invoke besides Allah will fail them. And they will realize that they will have no escape
en.41:49: One never tires of praying for good. And if touched with evil, they become desperate and hopeless
en.41:50: And if We let them taste a mercy from Us after being touched with adversity, they will certainly say, “This is what I deserve. I do not think the Hour will ˹ever˺ come. And if in fact I am returned to my Lord, the finest reward with Him will definitely be mine.” But We will surely inform the disbelievers of what they used to do. And We will certainly make them taste a harsh torment
en.41:51: When We show favour to someone, they turn away, acting arrogantly. And when touched with evil, they make endless prayers ˹for good˺
en.41:52: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “Imagine if this ˹Quran˺ is ˹truly˺ from Allah and you deny it: who can be more astray than those who have gone too far in opposition ˹to the truth˺?”
en.41:53: We will show them Our signs in the universe and within themselves until it becomes clear to them that this ˹Quran˺ is the truth. Is it not enough that your Lord is a Witness over all things
en.41:54: They are truly in doubt of the meeting with their Lord! ˹But˺ He is indeed Fully Aware of everything
en.42:3: And so you ˹O Prophet˺ are sent revelation, just like those before you, by Allah—the Almighty, All-Wise
en.42:4: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And He is the Most High, the Greatest
en.42:5: The heavens nearly burst, one above the other, ˹in awe of Him˺. And the angels glorify the praises of their Lord, and seek forgiveness for those on earth. Surely Allah alone is the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.42:6: As for those who take other protectors besides Him, Allah is Watchful over them. And you ˹O Prophet˺ are not a keeper over them
en.42:7: And so We have revealed to you a Quran in Arabic, so you may warn the Mother of Cities and everyone around it, and warn of the Day of Gathering—about which there is no doubt—˹when˺ a group will be in Paradise and another in the Blaze
en.42:8: Had Allah willed, He could have easily made all ˹humanity˺ into a single community ˹of believers˺. But He admits into His mercy whoever He wills. And the wrongdoers will have no protector or helper
en.42:9: How can they take protectors besides Him? Allah alone is the Protector. He ˹alone˺ gives life to the dead. And He ˹alone˺ is Most Capable of everything
en.42:10: ˹Say to the believers, O Prophet,˺ “Whatever you may differ about, its judgment rests with Allah. That is Allah—my Lord. In Him I put my trust, and to Him I ˹always˺ turn.”
en.42:11: ˹He is˺ the Originator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you spouses from among yourselves, and ˹made˺ mates for cattle ˹as well˺—multiplying you ˹both˺. There is nothing like Him, for He ˹alone˺ is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing
en.42:12: To Him belong the keys ˹of the treasuries˺ of the heavens and the earth. He gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. Indeed, He has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
