en.42:13: He has ordained for you ˹believers˺ the Way which He decreed for Noah, and what We have revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺ and what We decreed for Abraham, Moses, and Jesus, ˹commanding:˺ “Uphold the faith, and make no divisions in it.” What you call the polytheists to is unbearable for them. Allah chooses for Himself whoever He wills, and guides to Himself whoever turns ˹to Him˺
en.42:14: They did not split ˹into sects˺ out of mutual envy until knowledge came to them. Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord for an appointed term, the matter would have certainly been settled between them ˹at once˺. And surely those who were made to inherit the Scripture after them are truly in alarming doubt about this ˹Quran˺
en.42:15: Because of that, you ˹O Prophet˺ will invite ˹all˺. Be steadfast as you are commanded, and do not follow their desires. And say, “I believe in every Scripture Allah has revealed. And I am commanded to judge fairly among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. We will be accountable for our deeds and you for yours. There is no ˹need for˺ contention between us. Allah will gather us together ˹for judgment˺. And to Him is the final return.”
en.42:16: As for those who dispute about Allah after He is ˹already˺ acknowledged ˹by many˺, their argument is futile in the sight of their Lord. Upon them is wrath, and they will suffer a severe punishment
en.42:17: It is Allah Who has revealed the Book with the truth and the balance ˹of justice˺. You never know, perhaps the Hour is near
en.42:18: Those who disbelieve in it ˹ask to˺ hasten it ˹mockingly˺. But the believers are fearful of it, knowing that it is the truth. Surely those who dispute about the Hour have gone far astray
en.42:19: Allah is Ever Kind to His servants. He provides ˹abundantly˺ to whoever He wills. And He is the All-Powerful, Almighty
en.42:20: Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We will increase their harvest. And whoever desires ˹only˺ the harvest of this world, We will give them some of it, but they will have no share in the Hereafter
en.42:21: Or do they have associate-gods who have ordained for them some ˹polytheistic˺ beliefs, which Allah has not authorized? Had it not been for ˹prior˺ decree on Judgment, the matter would have certainly been settled between them ˹at once˺. And surely the wrongdoers will suffer a painful punishment
en.42:22: You will see the wrongdoers fearful ˹of the punishment˺ for what they committed but it will be inevitable for them, whereas those who believe and do good will be in the lush Gardens of Paradise. They will have whatever they desire from their Lord. That is ˹truly˺ the greatest bounty
en.42:23: That ˹reward˺ is the good news which Allah gives to His servants who believe and do good. Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I do not ask you for a reward for this ˹message˺—only honour for ˹our˺ kinship.” Whoever earns a good deed, We will increase it in goodness for them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative
en.42:24: Or do they say, “He has fabricated a lie about Allah!”? ˹If you had,˺ Allah would have sealed your heart, if He willed. And Allah wipes out falsehood and establishes the truth by His Words. He certainly knows best what is ˹hidden˺ in the heart
en.42:25: He is the One Who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons ˹their˺ sins. And He knows whatever you do
en.42:26: He responds to those who believe and do good, and increases their reward out of His grace. As for the disbelievers, they will suffer a severe punishment
en.42:27: Had Allah given abundant provisions to ˹all˺ His servants, they would have certainly transgressed throughout the land. But He sends down whatever He wills in perfect measure. He is truly All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants
en.42:28: He is the One Who sends down rain after people have given up hope, spreading out His mercy. He is the Guardian, the Praiseworthy
en.42:29: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and all living beings He dispersed throughout both. And He is Most Capable of bringing all together whenever He wills
en.42:30: Whatever affliction befalls you is because of what your own hands have committed. And He pardons much
en.42:31: You can never escape ˹Him˺ on earth, nor do you have any protector or helper besides Allah
en.42:32: And among His signs are the ships like mountains ˹sailing˺ in the sea
en.42:33: If He wills, He can calm the wind, leaving the ships motionless on the water. Surely in this are signs for whoever is steadfast, grateful
en.42:34: Or He can wreck the ships for what the people have committed—though He forgives much—
en.42:35: so those who dispute about Our signs may know that they have no refuge
en.42:36: Whatever ˹pleasure˺ you have been given is ˹no more than a fleeting˺ enjoyment of this worldly life. But what is with Allah is far better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord
en.42:37: who avoid major sins and shameful deeds, and forgive when angered
en.42:38: who respond to their Lord, establish prayer, conduct their affairs by mutual consultation, and donate from what We have provided for them
en.42:39: and who enforce justice when wronged
en.42:40: The reward of an evil deed is its equivalent. But whoever pardons and seeks reconciliation, then their reward is with Allah. He certainly does not like the wrongdoers
en.42:41: There is no blame on those who enforce justice after being wronged
en.42:42: Blame is only on those who wrong people and transgress in the land unjustly. It is they who will suffer a painful punishment
en.42:43: And whoever endures patiently and forgives—surely this is a resolve to aspire to
en.42:44: And whoever Allah leaves to stray will have no guide after Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they face the torment, pleading, “Is there any way back ˹to the world˺?”
en.42:45: And you will see them exposed to the Fire, fully humbled out of disgrace, stealing glances ˹at it˺. And the believers will say, “The ˹true˺ losers are those who have lost themselves and their families on Judgment Day.” The wrongdoers will certainly be in everlasting torment
en.42:46: They will have no protectors to help them against Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray, for them there is no way
en.42:47: Respond to your Lord before the coming of a Day from Allah that cannot be averted. There will be no refuge for you then, nor ˹grounds for˺ denial ˹of sins˺
en.42:48: But if they turn away, We have not sent you ˹O Prophet˺ as a keeper over them. Your duty is only to deliver ˹the message˺. And indeed, when We let someone taste a mercy from Us, they become prideful ˹because˺ of it. But when afflicted with evil because of what their hands have done, then one becomes totally ungrateful
en.42:49: To Allah ˹alone˺ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He creates whatever He wills. He blesses whoever He wills with daughters, and blesses whoever He wills with sons
en.42:50: or grants both, sons and daughters, ˹to whoever He wills˺, and leaves whoever He wills infertile. He is indeed All-Knowing, Most Capable
en.42:51: It is not ˹possible˺ for a human being to have Allah communicate with them, except through inspiration, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger-angel to reveal whatever He wills by His permission. He is surely Most High, All-Wise
en.42:52: And so We have sent to you ˹O Prophet˺ a revelation by Our command. You did not know of ˹this˺ Book and faith ˹before˺. But We have made it a light, by which We guide whoever We will of Our servants. And you are truly leading ˹all˺ to the Straight Path—
en.42:53: the Path of Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Surely to Allah all matters will return ˹for judgment˺
en.43:3: Certainly, We have made it a Quran in Arabic so perhaps you will understand
en.43:4: And indeed, it is—in the Master Record with Us—highly esteemed, rich in wisdom
en.43:5: Should We then turn the ˹Quranic˺ Reminder away from you ˹simply˺ because you have been a transgressing people
en.43:6: ˹Imagine˺ how many prophets We sent to those ˹destroyed˺ before
en.43:7: But no prophet ever came to them without being mocked
en.43:8: So We destroyed those who were far mightier than these ˹Meccans˺. The examples of ˹their˺ predecessors have ˹already˺ been related
en.43:9: If you ask them ˹O Prophet˺ who created the heavens and the earth, they will certainly say, “The Almighty, All-Knowing did.”
en.43:10: ˹He is the One˺ Who has laid out the earth for you, and set in it pathways for you so that you may find your way
en.43:11: And ˹He is the One˺ Who sends down rain from the sky in perfect measure, with which We give life to a lifeless land. And so will you be brought forth ˹from the grave˺
en.43:12: And ˹He is the One˺ Who created all ˹things in˺ pairs, and made for you ships and animals to ride
en.43:13: so that you may sit firmly on their backs, and remember your Lord’s blessings once you are settled on them, saying, “Glory be to the One Who has subjected these for us, for we could have never done so ˹on our own˺
en.43:14: And surely to our Lord we will ˹all˺ return.”
en.43:15: Still the pagans have made some of His creation out to be a part of Him. Indeed, humankind is clearly ungrateful
en.43:16: Has He taken ˹angels as His˺ daughters from what He created, and favoured you ˹O pagans˺ with sons
en.43:17: Whenever one of them is given the good news of what they attribute to the Most Compassionate, his face grows gloomy, as he suppresses his rage
en.43:18: ˹Do they attribute to Him˺ those who are brought up in fineries and are not commanding in disputes
en.43:19: Still they have labelled the angels, who are servants of the Most Compassionate, as female. Did they witness their creation? Their statement will be recorded, and they will be questioned
en.43:20: And they argue, “Had the Most Compassionate willed, we would have never worshipped them.” They have no knowledge ˹in support˺ of this ˹claim˺. They do nothing but lie
en.43:21: Or have We given them a Book ˹for proof˺, before this ˹Quran˺, to which they are holding firm
en.43:22: In fact, they say, “We found our forefathers following a ˹particular˺ way, and we are following in their footsteps.”
en.43:23: Similarly, whenever We sent a warner to a society before you ˹O Prophet˺, its ˹spoiled˺ elite would say, “We found our forefathers following a ˹particular˺ way, and we are walking in their footsteps.”
en.43:24: Each ˹warner˺ asked, “Even if what I brought you is better guidance than what you found your forefathers practicing?” They replied, “We totally reject whatever you have been sent with.”
en.43:25: So We inflicted punishment upon them. See then what was the fate of the deniers
en.43:26: ˹Remember, O Prophet˺ when Abraham declared to his father and his people, “I am totally free of whatever ˹gods˺ you worship
en.43:27: except the One Who originated me, and He will surely guide me.”
en.43:28: And he left this enduring declaration among his descendants, so they may ˹always˺ turn back ˹to Allah˺
en.43:29: In fact, I had allowed enjoyment for these ˹Meccans˺ and their forefathers, until the truth came to them along with a messenger making things clear
en.43:30: ˹But˺ when the truth came to them, they said, “This is magic, and we totally reject it.”
en.43:31: And they exclaimed, “If only this Quran was revealed to a great man from ˹one of˺ the two cities!”
en.43:32: Is it they who distribute your Lord’s mercy? We ˹alone˺ have distributed their ˹very˺ livelihood among them in this worldly life and raised some of them in rank above others so that some may employ others in service. ˹But˺ your Lord’s mercy is far better than whatever ˹wealth˺ they amass
en.43:33: Were it not that people might ˹be tempted to˺ become one community ˹of disbelievers˺, We would have supplied the homes of ˹only˺ those who disbelieve in the Most Compassionate with silver roofs and ˹silver˺ stairways to ascend
en.43:34: as well as ˹silver˺ gates and thrones to recline on
en.43:35: and ornaments ˹of gold˺. Yet all this is no more than a ˹fleeting˺ enjoyment in this worldly life. ˹But˺ the Hereafter with your Lord is ˹only˺ for those mindful ˹of Him˺
en.43:36: And whoever turns a blind eye to the Reminder of the Most Compassionate, We place at the disposal of each a devilish one as their close associate
en.43:37: who will certainly hinder them from the ˹Right˺ Way while they think they are ˹rightly˺ guided
en.43:38: But when such a person comes to Us, one will say ˹to their associate˺, “I wish you were as distant from me as the east is from the west! What an evil associate ˹you were˺!”
en.43:39: ˹It will be said to both,˺ “Since you all did wrong, sharing in the punishment will be of no benefit to you this Day.”
en.43:40: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind or those clearly astray
en.43:41: Even if We take you away ˹from this world˺, We will surely inflict punishment upon them
en.43:42: Or if We show you what We threaten them with, We certainly have full power over them
en.43:43: So hold firmly to what has been revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺. You are truly on the Straight Path
en.43:44: Surely this ˹Quran˺ is a glory for you and your people. And you will ˹all˺ be questioned ˹about it˺
en.43:45: Ask ˹the followers of˺ the messengers that We already sent before you if We ˹ever˺ appointed ˹other˺ gods to be worshipped besides the Most Compassionate
en.43:46: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, and he said: “I am a messenger of the Lord of all worlds.”
en.43:47: But as soon as he came to them with Our signs, they laughed at them
en.43:48: although every sign We showed them was greater than the one before. Ultimately, We seized them with torments so that they might return ˹to the Right Path˺
en.43:49: ˹Then˺ they pleaded, “O ˹mighty˺ magician! Pray to your Lord on our behalf, by virtue of the covenant He made with you. We will certainly accept guidance.”
en.43:50: But as soon as We removed the torments from them, they broke their promise
en.43:51: And Pharaoh called out to his people, boasting, “O my people! Am I not sovereign over Egypt as well as ˹all˺ these streams flowing at my feet? Can you not see
en.43:52: Am I not better than this nobody who can hardly express himself
en.43:53: Why then have no golden bracelets ˹of kingship˺ been granted to him or angels come with him as escorts!”
en.43:54: And so he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. They were truly a rebellious people
en.43:55: So when they enraged Us, We inflicted punishment upon them, drowning them all
en.43:56: And We made them an example and a lesson for those after them
en.43:57: When the son of Mary was cited as an example ˹in argument˺, your people ˹O Prophet˺ broke into ˹joyful˺ applause
en.43:58: They exclaimed, “Which is better: our gods or Jesus?” They cite him only to argue. In fact, they are a people prone to dispute
en.43:59: He was only a servant We showed favour to, and made as an example for the Children of Israel
en.43:60: Had We willed, We could have easily replaced you ˹all˺ with angels, succeeding one another on earth
en.43:61: And his ˹second˺ coming is truly a sign for the Hour. So have no doubt about it, and follow me. This is the Straight Path
en.43:62: And do not let Satan hinder you, ˹for˺ he is certainly your sworn enemy
en.43:63: When Jesus came with clear proofs, he declared, “I have come to you with wisdom, and to clarify to you some of what you differ about. So fear Allah, and obey me
en.43:64: Surely Allah ˹alone˺ is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ˹alone˺. This is the Straight Path.”
en.43:65: Yet their ˹various˺ groups have differed among themselves ˹about him˺, so woe to the wrongdoers when they face the torment of a painful Day
en.43:66: Are they waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise when they least expect ˹it˺
en.43:67: Close friends will be enemies to one another on that Day, except the righteous
en.43:68: ˹who will be told,˺ “O My servants! There is no fear for you Today, nor will you grieve—
en.43:69: ˹those˺ who believed in Our signs and ˹fully˺ submitted ˹to Us˺
en.43:70: Enter Paradise, you and your spouses, rejoicing.”
en.43:71: Golden trays and cups will be passed around to them. There will be whatever the souls desire and the eyes delight in. And you will be there forever
en.43:72: That is the Paradise which you will be awarded for what you used to do
en.43:73: There you will have abundant fruit to eat from
en.43:74: Indeed, the wicked will be in the torment of Hell forever
en.43:75: It will never be lightened for them, and there they will be overwhelmed with despair
en.43:76: We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers
en.43:77: They will cry, “O Mâlik! Let your Lord finish us off.” He will answer, “You are definitely here to stay.”
en.43:78: We certainly brought the truth to you, but most of you were resentful of the truth
en.43:79: Or have they mastered some ˹evil˺ plan? Then We ˹too˺ are surely planning
en.43:80: Or do they think that We do not hear their ˹evil˺ thoughts and secret talks? Yes ˹We do˺! And Our messenger-angels are in their presence, recording ˹it all˺
en.43:81: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “If the Most Compassionate ˹really˺ had offspring, I would be the first worshipper.”
en.43:82: Glorified is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, far above what they claim
en.43:83: So let them indulge ˹in falsehood˺ and amuse ˹themselves˺ until they face their Day, which they have been warned of
en.43:84: It is He Who is ˹the only˺ God in the heavens and ˹the only˺ God on the earth. For He is the All-Wise, All-Knowing
en.43:85: And Blessed is the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between! With Him ˹alone˺ is the knowledge of the Hour. And to Him you will ˹all˺ be returned
en.43:86: ˹But˺ those ˹objects of worship˺ they invoke besides Him have no power to intercede, except those who testify to the truth knowingly
en.43:87: If you ask them ˹O Prophet˺ who created them, they will certainly say, “Allah!” How can they then be deluded ˹from the truth˺
en.43:88: ˹Allah is Aware of˺ the Prophet’s cry: “O my Lord! Indeed, these are a people who persist in disbelief.”
en.43:89: So bear with them and respond with peace. They will soon come to know
en.44:3: Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night, for We always warn ˹against evil˺
en.44:4: On that night every matter of wisdom is ordained
en.44:5: by a command from Us, for We have always sent ˹messengers˺
en.44:6: as a mercy from your Lord. He ˹alone˺ is truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing—
en.44:7: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith
en.44:8: There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. He ˹alone˺ gives life and causes death. ˹He is˺ your Lord, and the Lord of your forefathers
en.44:9: In fact, they are in doubt, amusing themselves
en.44:10: Wait then ˹O Prophet˺ for the day ˹when˺ the sky will be veiled in haze, clearly visible
en.44:11: overwhelming the people. ˹They will cry,˺ “This is a painful torment
en.44:12: Our Lord! Remove ˹this˺ torment from us, ˹and˺ we will certainly believe.”
en.44:13: How can they be reminded when a messenger has already come to them, making things clear
en.44:14: then they turned away from him, saying, “A madman, taught by others!”
en.44:15: Indeed, We will remove ˹that˺ torment for a while, and you ˹Meccans˺ will return ˹to disbelief˺
en.44:16: ˹Then˺ on the Day We will deal ˹you˺ the fiercest blow, We will surely inflict punishment
en.44:17: Indeed, before them We tested Pharaoh’s people: a noble messenger came to them
en.44:18: ˹proclaiming,˺ “Hand over the servants of Allah to me. I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you
en.44:19: And do not be arrogant with Allah. I have certainly come to you with a compelling proof
en.44:20: And indeed, I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord so you do not stone me ˹to death˺
en.44:21: ˹But˺ if you do not believe me, then let me be.”
en.44:22: Ultimately, he cried out to his Lord, “These are a wicked people!”
en.44:23: ˹Allah responded,˺ “Leave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued
en.44:24: And leave the sea parted, for they are certainly an army bound to drown.”
en.44:25: ˹Imagine˺ how many gardens and springs the tyrants left behind
en.44:26: as well as ˹various˺ crops and splendid residences
en.44:27: and luxuries which they fully enjoyed
en.44:28: So it was. And We awarded it ˹all˺ to another people
en.44:29: Neither heaven nor earth wept over them, nor was their fate delayed
en.44:30: And We certainly delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment
en.44:31: of Pharaoh. He was truly a tyrant, a transgressor
en.44:32: And indeed, We chose the Israelites knowingly above the others
en.44:33: And We showed them signs in which there was a clear test
en.44:35: “There is nothing beyond our first death, and we will never be resurrected
en.44:36: Bring ˹back˺ our forefathers, if what you say is true.”
en.44:37: Are they superior to the people of Tubba’ and those before them? We destroyed them ˹all˺, ˹for˺ they were truly wicked
en.44:38: We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything in between for sport
en.44:39: We only created them for a purpose, but most of these ˹pagans˺ do not know
en.44:40: Surely the Day of ˹Final˺ Decision is the time appointed for all—
en.44:41: the Day no kith or kin will be of benefit to another whatsoever, nor will they be helped
en.44:42: except those shown mercy by Allah. He is truly the Almighty, Most Merciful
en.44:43: Surely ˹the fruit of˺ the tree of Zaqqûm
en.44:44: will be the food of the evildoer
en.44:45: Like molten metal, it will boil in the bellies
en.44:46: like the boiling of hot water
en.44:47: ˹It will be said,˺ “Seize them and drag them into the depths of the Hellfire
en.44:48: Then pour over their heads the torment of boiling water.”
en.44:49: ˹The wicked will be told,˺ “Taste this. You mighty, noble one
en.44:50: This is truly what you ˹all˺ used to doubt.”
en.44:51: Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure place
en.44:53: dressed in fine silk and rich brocade, facing one another
en.44:54: So it will be. And We will pair them to maidens with gorgeous eyes
en.44:55: There they will call for every fruit in serenity
en.44:56: There they will never taste death, beyond the first death. And He will protect them from the punishment of the Hellfire—
en.44:57: as ˹an act of˺ grace from your Lord. That is ˹truly˺ the ultimate triumph
en.44:58: Indeed, We have made this ˹Quran˺ easy in your own language ˹O Prophet˺ so perhaps they will be mindful
en.44:59: Wait then! They too are certainly waiting
en.45:2: The revelation of this Book is from Allah—the Almighty, All-Wise
en.45:3: Surely in ˹the creation of˺ the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers
en.45:4: And in your own creation, and whatever living beings He dispersed, are signs for people of sure faith
en.45:5: And ˹in˺ the alternation of the day and the night, the provision sent down from the skies by Allah—reviving the earth after its death—and the shifting of the winds, are signs for people of understanding
en.45:6: These are Allah’s revelations which We recite to you ˹O Prophet˺ in truth. So what message will they believe in after ˹denying˺ Allah and His revelations
en.45:8: They hear Allah’s revelations recited to them, then persist ˹in denial˺ arrogantly as if they did not hear them. So give them good news of a painful punishment
en.45:9: And whenever they learn anything of Our revelations, they make a mockery of it. It is they who will suffer a humiliating punishment
en.45:10: Awaiting them is Hell. Their ˹worldly˺ gains will not be of any benefit to them whatsoever, nor will those protectors they have taken besides Allah. And they will suffer a tremendous punishment
en.45:11: This ˹Quran˺ is ˹true˺ guidance. And those who deny their Lord’s revelations will suffer the ˹worst˺ torment of agonizing pain
en.45:12: Allah is the One Who has subjected the sea for you so that ships may sail upon it by His command, and that you may seek His bounty, and that perhaps you will be grateful
en.45:13: He ˹also˺ subjected for you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth—all by His grace. Surely in this are signs for people who reflect
en.45:14: ˹O Prophet!˺ Tell the believers to forgive those who do not fear Allah’s days ˹of torment˺, so that He will reward each group for what they used to commit
en.45:15: Whoever does good, it is to their own benefit. And whoever does evil, it is to their own loss. Then to your Lord you will ˹all˺ be returned
en.45:16: Indeed, We gave the Children of Israel the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood; granted them good, lawful provisions; and favoured them above the others
en.45:17: We ˹also˺ gave them clear commandments regarding ˹their˺ faith. But it was not until knowledge came to them that they differed out of mutual envy. Surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their differences
en.45:18: Now We have set you ˹O Prophet˺ on the ˹clear˺ Way of faith. So follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know ˹the truth˺
en.45:19: They certainly can be of no benefit to you against Allah whatsoever. Surely the wrongdoers are patrons of each other, whereas Allah is the Patron of the righteous
en.45:20: This ˹Quran˺ is an insight for humanity—a guide and mercy for people of sure faith
en.45:21: Or do those who commit evil deeds ˹simply˺ think that We will make them equal—in their life and after their death—to those who believe and do good? How wrong is their judgment
en.45:22: For Allah created the heavens and the earth for a purpose, so that every soul may be paid back for what it has committed. And none will be wronged
en.45:23: Have you seen ˹O Prophet˺ those who have taken their own desires as their god? ˹And so˺ Allah left them to stray knowingly, sealed their hearing and hearts, and placed a cover on their sight. Who then can guide them after Allah? Will you ˹all˺ not then be mindful
en.45:24: And they argue, “There is nothing beyond our worldly life. We die; others are born. And nothing destroys us but ˹the passage of˺ time.” Yet they have no knowledge ˹in support˺ of this ˹claim˺. They only speculate
en.45:25: And whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, their only argument is to say: “Bring our forefathers back, if what you say is true!”
en.45:26: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “˹It is˺ Allah ˹Who˺ gives you life, then causes you to die, then will gather you ˹all˺ on the Day of Judgment, about which there is no doubt. But most people do not know.”
en.45:27: To Allah ˹alone˺ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. On the Day the Hour will be established, the people of falsehood will then be in ˹total˺ loss
en.45:28: And you will see every faith-community on its knees. Every community will be summoned to its record ˹of deeds˺. ˹They all will be told,˺ “This Day you will be rewarded for what you used to do
en.45:29: This record of Ours speaks the truth about you. Indeed, We always had your deeds recorded ˹by the angels˺.”
en.45:30: As for those who believed and did good, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is ˹truly˺ the absolute triumph
en.45:31: And as for those who disbelieved, ˹they will be told,˺ “Were My revelations not recited to you, yet you acted arrogantly and were a wicked people
en.45:32: And whenever it was said ˹to you˺, ‘Surely Allah’s promise ˹of judgment˺ is true and there is no doubt about the Hour,’ you said ˹mockingly˺, ‘We do not know what the Hour is! We think it is no more than speculation, and we are not convinced ˹that it will ever come˺.’”
en.45:33: And the evil ˹consequences˺ of their deeds will unfold before them, and they will be overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule
en.45:34: It will be said, “This Day We will neglect you as you neglected the meeting of this Day of yours! Your home will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers
en.45:35: This is because you made a mockery of Allah’s revelations, and were deluded by ˹your˺ worldly life.” So ˹from˺ that Day ˹on˺ they will not be taken out of the Fire, nor will they be allowed to appease ˹their Lord˺
en.45:36: So all praise is for Allah—Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of all worlds
en.45:37: To Him belongs ˹all˺ Majesty in the heavens and the earth. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.46:2: The revelation of this Book is from Allah—the Almighty, All-Wise
en.46:3: We only created the heavens and the earth and everything in between for a purpose and an appointed term. Yet the disbelievers are turning away from what they have been warned about
en.46:4: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “Have you considered whatever ˹idols˺ you invoke besides Allah? Show me what they have created on earth! Or do they have a share in ˹the creation of˺ the heavens? Bring me a scripture ˹revealed˺ before this ˹Quran˺ or a shred of knowledge, if what you say is true.”
en.46:5: And who could be more astray than those who call upon others besides Allah—˹others˺ that cannot respond to them until the Day of Judgment, and are ˹even˺ unaware of their calls
en.46:6: And when ˹such˺ people will be gathered together, those ˹gods˺ will be their enemies and will disown their worship
en.46:7: Whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, the disbelievers say of the truth when it has come to them, “This is pure magic.”
en.46:8: Or do they say, “He has fabricated this ˹Quran˺!”? Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “If I have done so, then there is nothing whatsoever you can do to save me from Allah. He knows best what ˹slurs˺ you indulge about it. Sufficient is He as a Witness between you and me. And He is the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”
en.46:9: Say, “I am not the first messenger ever sent, nor do I know what will happen to me or you. I only follow what is revealed to me. And I am only sent with a clear warning.”
en.46:10: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “Consider if this ˹Quran˺ is ˹truly˺ from Allah and you deny it, and a witness from the Children of Israel attests to it and then believes, whereas you act arrogantly. Surely Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.”
en.46:11: The disbelievers say of the believers, “Had it been ˹something˺ good, they would not have beaten us to it.” Now since they reject its guidance, they will say, “˹This is˺ an ancient fabrication!”
en.46:12: And before this ˹Quran˺ the Book of Moses was ˹revealed as˺ a guide and mercy. And this Book is a confirmation, in the Arabic tongue, to warn those who do wrong, and as good news to those who do good
en.46:13: Surely those who say, “Our Lord is Allah,” and then remain steadfast—there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve
en.46:14: It is they who will be the residents of Paradise, staying there forever, as a reward for what they used to do
en.46:15: We have commanded people to honour their parents. Their mothers bore them in hardship and delivered them in hardship. Their ˹period of˺ bearing and weaning is thirty months. In time, when the child reaches their prime at the age of forty, they pray, “My Lord! Inspire me to ˹always˺ be thankful for Your favours which You blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please You. And instil righteousness in my offspring. I truly repent to You, and I truly submit ˹to Your Will˺.”
en.46:16: It is from these ˹people˺ that We will accept the good they did, and overlook their misdeeds—along with the residents of Paradise, ˹in fulfilment of˺ the true promise they have been given
en.46:17: But some scold their parents, “Enough with you! Are you warning me that I will be brought forth ˹from the grave˺, while many generations had already perished before me ˹for good˺?” The parents cry to Allah for help, ˹and warn their child,˺ “Pity you. Have faith! Surely Allah’s promise is true.” But the deniers insist, “This is nothing but ancient fables.”
en.46:18: These are the ones against whom the fate of earlier communities of jinn and humans has been justified, ˹for˺ they were truly losers
en.46:19: Each ˹of the two groups˺ will be ranked according to what they have done so He may fully reward all. And none will be wronged
en.46:20: ˹Watch for˺ the Day ˹when˺ the disbelievers will be exposed to the Fire. ˹They will be told,˺ “You ˹already˺ exhausted your ˹share of˺ pleasures during your worldly life, and ˹fully˺ enjoyed them. So Today you will be rewarded with the torment of disgrace for your arrogance throughout the land with no right, and for your rebelliousness.”
en.46:21: And remember the brother of ’Ȃd, when he warned his people, who inhabited the sand-hills—there were certainly warners before and after him—˹saying,˺ “Worship none but Allah. I truly fear for you the torment of a tremendous day.”
en.46:22: They argued, “Have you come to turn us away from our gods? Bring us then whatever you threaten us with, if what you say is true.”
en.46:23: He responded, “The knowledge ˹of its time˺ is only with Allah. I only convey to you what I have been sent with. But I can see that you are a people acting ignorantly.”
en.46:24: Then when they saw the torment as a ˹dense˺ cloud approaching their valleys, they said ˹happily˺, “This is a cloud bringing us rain.” ˹But Hûd replied,˺ “No, it is what you sought to hasten: a ˹fierce˺ wind carrying a painful punishment!”
en.46:25: It destroyed everything by the command of its Lord, leaving nothing visible except their ruins. This is how We reward the wicked people
en.46:26: Indeed, We had established them in a way We have not established you ˹Meccans˺. And We gave them hearing, sight, and intellect. But neither their hearing, sight, nor intellect were of any benefit to them whatsoever, since they persisted in denying Allah’s signs. And ˹so˺ they were overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule
en.46:27: We certainly destroyed the societies around you after having varied the signs so perhaps they would return ˹to the Right Path˺
en.46:28: Why then did those ˹idols˺ they took as gods—hoping to get closer ˹to Him˺—not come to their aid? Instead, they failed them. That is ˹the result of˺ their lies and their fabrications
en.46:29: ˹Remember, O Prophet,˺ when We sent a group of jinn your way to listen to the Quran. Then, upon hearing it, they said ˹to one another˺, “Listen quietly!” Then when it was over, they returned to their fellow jinn as warners
en.46:30: They declared, “O our fellow jinn! We have truly heard a scripture revealed after Moses, confirming what came before it. It guides to the truth and the Straight Way
en.46:31: O our fellow jinn! Respond to the caller of Allah and believe in him, He will forgive your sins and protect you from a painful punishment
en.46:32: And whoever does not respond to the caller of Allah will have no escape on earth, nor will they have any protectors against Him. It is they who are clearly astray.”
en.46:33: Do they not realize that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth and did not tire in creating them, is able to give life to the dead? Yes ˹indeed˺! He is certainly Most Capable of everything
en.46:34: And on the Day the disbelievers will be exposed to the Fire, ˹they will be asked,˺ “Is this ˹Hereafter˺ not the truth?” They will cry, “Absolutely, by our Lord!” It will be said, “Then taste the punishment for your disbelief.”
en.46:35: So endure patiently, as did the Messengers of Firm Resolve. And do not ˹seek to˺ hasten ˹the torment˺ for the deniers. On the Day they see what they have been threatened with, it will be as if they had only stayed ˹in this world˺ for an hour of a day. ˹This is˺ a ˹sufficient˺ warning! Then, will anyone be destroyed except the rebellious people
en.47:1: Those who disbelieve and hinder ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah, He will render their deeds void
en.47:2: As for those who believe, do good, and have faith in what has been revealed to Muḥammad—which is the truth from their Lord—He will absolve them of their sins and improve their condition
en.47:3: This is because the disbelievers follow falsehood, while the believers follow the truth from their Lord. This is how Allah shows people their true state ˹of faith˺
en.47:4: So when you meet the disbelievers ˹in battle˺, strike ˹their˺ necks until you have thoroughly subdued them, then bind them firmly. Later ˹free them either as˺ an act of grace or by ransom until the war comes to an end. So will it be. Had Allah willed, He ˹Himself˺ could have inflicted punishment on them. But He does ˹this only to˺ test some of you by means of others. And those who are martyred in the cause of Allah, He will never render their deeds void
en.47:5: He will guide them ˹to their reward˺, improve their condition
en.47:6: and admit them into Paradise, having made it known to them
en.47:7: O believers! If you stand up for Allah, He will help you and make your steps firm
en.47:8: As for the disbelievers, may they be doomed and may He render their deeds void
en.47:9: That is because they detest what Allah has revealed, so He has rendered their deeds void
en.47:10: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those before them? Allah annihilated them, and a similar fate awaits the disbelievers
en.47:11: This is because Allah is the Patron of the believers while the disbelievers have no patron
en.47:12: Surely Allah will admit those who believe and do good into Gardens under which rivers flow. As for the disbelievers, they enjoy themselves and feed like cattle. But the Fire will be their home
en.47:13: ˹Imagine, O Prophet,˺ how many societies We destroyed that were far superior in might than your society—which drove you out—and there was none to help them
en.47:14: Can those ˹believers˺ who stand on clear proof from their Lord be like those whose evil deeds are made appealing to them and ˹only˺ follow their desires
en.47:15: The description of the Paradise promised to the righteous is that in it are rivers of fresh water, rivers of milk that never changes in taste, rivers of wine delicious to drink, and rivers of pure honey. There they will ˹also˺ have all kinds of fruit, and forgiveness from their Lord. ˹Can they be˺ like those who will stay in the Fire forever, left to drink boiling water that will tear apart their insides
en.47:16: There are some of them who listen to you ˹O Prophet˺, but when they depart from you, they say ˹mockingly˺ to those ˹believers˺ gifted with knowledge, “What did he just say?” These are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed and who ˹only˺ follow their desires
en.47:17: As for those who are ˹rightly˺ guided, He increases them in guidance and blesses them with righteousness
en.47:18: Are they only waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise? Yet ˹some of˺ its signs have already come. Once it actually befalls them, will it not be too late to be mindful
en.47:19: So, know ˹well, O Prophet,˺ that there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Allah. And seek forgiveness for your shortcomings and for ˹the sins of˺ the believing men and women. For Allah ˹fully˺ knows your movements and places of rest ˹O people˺
en.47:20: And the believers say, “If only a sûrah was revealed ˹allowing self-defence˺!” Yet when a precise sûrah is revealed, in which fighting is ˹explicitly˺ mentioned, you see those with sickness in their hearts staring at you like someone in the throes of death. It would have been better for them
en.47:21: to obey and speak rightly. Then when fighting was ordained, it surely would have been better for them if they were true to Allah
en.47:22: Now if you ˹hypocrites˺ turn away, perhaps you would then spread corruption throughout the land and sever your ˹ties of˺ kinship
en.47:23: These are the ones who Allah has condemned, deafening them and blinding their eyes
en.47:24: Do they not then reflect on the Quran? Or are there locks upon their hearts
en.47:25: Indeed, those who relapse ˹into disbelief˺ after ˹true˺ guidance has become clear to them, ˹it is˺ Satan ˹that˺ has tempted them, luring them with false hopes
en.47:26: That is because they said ˹privately˺ to those who ˹also˺ detest what Allah has revealed, “We will obey you in some matters.” But Allah ˹fully˺ knows what they are hiding
en.47:27: Then how ˹horrible˺ will it be when the angels take their souls, beating their faces and backs
en.47:28: This is because they follow whatever displeases Allah and hate whatever pleases Him, so He has rendered their deeds void
en.47:29: Or do those with sickness in their hearts think that Allah will not ˹be able to˺ expose their malice
en.47:30: Had We willed, We could have truly shown them to you ˹O Prophet˺, and you would have certainly recognized them by their appearance. But you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech. And Allah ˹fully˺ knows your doings ˹O people˺
en.47:31: We will certainly test you ˹believers˺ until We prove those of you who ˹truly˺ struggle ˹in Allah’s cause˺ and remain steadfast, and reveal how you conduct yourselves
en.47:32: Indeed, those who disbelieve, hinder ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah, and defy the Messenger after ˹true˺ guidance has become clear to them; they will not harm Allah in the least, but He will render their deeds void
en.47:33: O believers! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and do not let your deeds be in vain
en.47:34: Surely those who disbelieve, hinder ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah, and then die as disbelievers; Allah will never forgive them
en.47:35: So do not falter or cry for peace, for you will have the upper hand and Allah is with you. And He will never let your deeds go to waste
en.47:36: This worldly life is no more than play and amusement. But if you are faithful and mindful ˹of Allah˺, He will grant you your ˹full˺ reward, and will not ask you ˹to donate all˺ your wealth
en.47:37: If He were to do so and pressure you, you would withhold and He would bring out your resentment
en.47:38: Here you are, being invited to donate ˹a little˺ in the cause of Allah. Still some of you withhold. And whoever does so, it is only to their own loss. For Allah is the Self-Sufficient, whereas you stand in need ˹of Him˺. If you ˹still˺ turn away, He will replace you with another people. And they will not be like you
en.48:1: Indeed, We have granted you a clear triumph ˹O Prophet˺
en.48:2: so that Allah may forgive you for your past and future shortcomings, perfect His favour upon you, guide you along the Straight Path
en.48:3: and so that Allah will help you tremendously
en.48:4: He is the One Who sent down serenity upon the hearts of the believers so that they may increase even more in their faith. To Allah ˹alone˺ belong the forces of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.48:5: So He may admit believing men and women into Gardens under which rivers flow—to stay there forever—and absolve them of their sins. And that is a supreme achievement in the sight of Allah
en.48:6: Also ˹so that˺ He may punish hypocrite men and women and polytheistic men and women, who harbour evil thoughts of Allah. May ill-fate befall them! Allah is displeased with them. He has condemned them and prepared for them Hell. What an evil destination
en.48:7: To Allah ˹alone˺ belong the forces of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is Almighty, All-Wise
en.48:8: Indeed, ˹O Prophet,˺ We have sent you as a witness, a deliverer of good news, and a warner
en.48:9: so that you ˹believers˺ may have faith in Allah and His Messenger, support and honour him, and glorify Allah morning and evening
en.48:10: Surely those who pledge allegiance to you ˹O Prophet˺ are actually pledging allegiance to Allah. Allah’s Hand is over theirs. Whoever breaks their pledge, it will only be to their own loss. And whoever fulfils their pledge to Allah, He will grant them a great reward
en.48:11: The nomadic Arabs, who stayed behind, will say to you ˹O Prophet˺, “We were preoccupied with our wealth and families, so ask for forgiveness for us.” They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say, “Who then can stand between you and Allah in any way, if He intends harm or benefit for you? In fact, Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.48:12: The truth is: you thought that the Messenger and the believers would never return to their families again. And that was made appealing in your hearts. You harboured evil thoughts ˹about Allah˺, and ˹so˺ became a doomed people.”
en.48:13: And whoever does not believe in Allah and His Messenger, then We surely have prepared for the disbelievers a blazing Fire
en.48:14: To Allah ˹alone˺ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whoever He wills, and punishes whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.48:15: Those who stayed behind will say, when you ˹believers˺ set out to take the spoils of war, “Let us accompany you.” They wish to change Allah’s promise. Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “You will not accompany us. This is what Allah has said before.” They will then say, “In fact, you are driven by jealousy against us!” The truth is: they can hardly comprehend
en.48:16: Say to nomadic Arabs, who stayed behind, “You will be called ˹to fight˺ against a people of great might, who you will fight unless they submit. If you then obey, Allah will grant you a fine reward. But if you turn away as you did before, He will inflict upon you a painful punishment.”
en.48:17: There is no blame on the blind, or the disabled, or the sick ˹for staying behind˺. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger will be admitted by Him into Gardens under which rivers flow. But whoever turns away will be subjected by Him to a painful punishment
en.48:18: Indeed, Allah was pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you ˹O Prophet˺ under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down serenity upon them and rewarded them with a victory at hand
en.48:19: and many spoils of war they will gain. For Allah is Almighty, All-Wise
en.48:20: Allah has promised you ˹believers˺ abundant spoils, which you will gain, so He hastened this ˹truce˺ for you. And He has held people’s hands back from ˹harming˺ you, so it may be a sign for the believers, and so He may guide you along the Straight Path
en.48:21: And ˹there are˺ other gains which are beyond your reach that Allah is keeping in store ˹for you˺. For Allah is Most Capable of everything
en.48:22: If the disbelievers were to fight you, they would certainly flee. Then they would never find any protector or helper
en.48:23: ˹This is˺ Allah’s way, already long established ˹in the past˺. And you will find no change in Allah’s way
en.48:24: He is the One Who held back their hands from you and your hands from them in the valley of ˹Ḥudaibiyah, near˺ Mecca, after giving you the upper hand over ˹a group of˺ them. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do
en.48:25: They are the ones who persisted in disbelief and hindered you from the Sacred Mosque, preventing the sacrificial animals from reaching their destination. ˹We would have let you march through Mecca,˺ had there not been believing men and women, unknown to you. You might have trampled them underfoot, incurring guilt for ˹what you did to˺ them unknowingly. That was so Allah may admit into His mercy whoever He wills. Had those ˹unknown˺ believers stood apart, We would have certainly inflicted a painful punishment on the disbelievers
en.48:26: ˹Remember˺ when the disbelievers had filled their hearts with pride—the pride of ˹pre-Islamic˺ ignorance—then Allah sent down His serenity upon His Messenger and the believers, inspiring them to uphold the declaration of faith, for they were better entitled and more worthy of it. And Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
en.48:27: Indeed, Allah will fulfil His Messenger’s vision in all truth: Allah willing, you will surely enter the Sacred Mosque, in security—˹some with˺ heads shaved and ˹others with˺ hair shortened—without fear. He knew what you did not know, so He first granted you the triumph at hand
en.48:28: He is the One Who has sent His Messenger with ˹right˺ guidance and the religion of truth, making it prevail over all others. And sufficient is Allah as a Witness
en.48:29: Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allah. And those with him are firm with the disbelievers and compassionate with one another. You see them bowing and prostrating ˹in prayer˺, seeking Allah’s bounty and pleasure. The sign ˹of brightness can be seen˺ on their faces from the trace of prostrating ˹in prayer˺. This is their description in the Torah. And their parable in the Gospel is that of a seed that sprouts its ˹tiny˺ branches, making it strong. Then it becomes thick, standing firmly on its stem, to the delight of the planters—in this way Allah makes the believers a source of dismay for the disbelievers. To those of them who believe and do good, Allah has promised forgiveness and a great reward
en.49:1: O believers! Do not proceed ˹in any matter˺ before ˹a decree from˺ Allah and His Messenger. And fear Allah. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing
en.49:2: O believers! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, nor speak loudly to him as you do to one another, or your deeds will become void while you are unaware
en.49:3: Indeed, those who lower their voices in the presence of Allah’s Messenger are the ones whose hearts Allah has refined for righteousness. They will have forgiveness and a great reward
en.49:4: Indeed, most of those who call out to you ˹O Prophet˺ from outside ˹your˺ private quarters have no understanding ˹of manners˺
en.49:5: Had they been patient until you could come out to them, it would have certainly been better for them. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.49:6: O believers, if an evildoer brings you any news, verify ˹it˺ so you do not harm people unknowingly, becoming regretful for what you have done
en.49:7: And keep in mind that Allah’s Messenger is ˹still˺ in your midst. If he were to yield to you in many matters, you would surely suffer ˹the consequences˺. But Allah has endeared faith to you, making it appealing in your hearts. And He has made disbelief, rebelliousness, and disobedience detestable to you. Those are the ones rightly guided
en.49:8: ˹This is˺ a bounty and a blessing from Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.49:9: And if two groups of believers fight each other, then make peace between them. But if one of them transgresses against the other, then fight against the transgressing group until they ˹are willing to˺ submit to the rule of Allah. If they do so, then make peace between both ˹groups˺ in all fairness and act justly. Surely Allah loves those who uphold justice
en.49:10: The believers are but one brotherhood, so make peace between your brothers. And be mindful of Allah so you may be shown mercy
en.49:11: O believers! Do not let some ˹men˺ ridicule others, they may be better than them, nor let ˹some˺ women ridicule other women, they may be better than them. Do not defame one another, nor call each other by offensive nicknames. How evil it is to act rebelliously after having faith! And whoever does not repent, it is they who are the ˹true˺ wrongdoers
en.49:12: O believers! Avoid many suspicions, ˹for˺ indeed, some suspicions are sinful. And do not spy, nor backbite one another. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of their dead brother? You would despise that! And fear Allah. Surely Allah is ˹the˺ Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful
en.49:13: O humanity! Indeed, We created you from a male and a female, and made you into peoples and tribes so that you may ˹get to˺ know one another. Surely the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous among you. Allah is truly All-Knowing, All-Aware
en.49:14: ˹Some of˺ the nomadic Arabs say, “We believe.” Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “You have not believed. But say, ‘We have submitted,’ for faith has not yet entered your hearts. But if you obey Allah and His Messenger ˹wholeheartedly˺, He will not discount anything from ˹the reward of˺ your deeds. Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”
en.49:15: The ˹true˺ believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger—never doubting—and strive with their wealth and their lives in the cause of Allah. They are the ones true in faith
en.49:16: Say, “Do you inform Allah of your faith, when Allah ˹already˺ knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? And Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things.”
en.49:17: They regard their acceptance of Islam as a favour to you. Tell ˹them, O Prophet˺, “Do not regard your Islam as a favour to me. Rather, it is Allah Who has done you a favour by guiding you to the faith, if ˹indeed˺ you are faithful
en.49:18: Surely Allah knows the unseen of the heavens and earth. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.”
en.50:2: ˹All will be resurrected,˺ yet the deniers are astonished that a warner has come to them from among themselves ˹warning of resurrection˺. So the disbelievers say, “This is an astonishing thing
en.50:3: ˹Will we be returned to life,˺ when we are dead and reduced to dust? Such a return is impossible.”
en.50:4: We certainly know what the earth consumes of them ˹after their death˺, and with us is a well-preserved Record
en.50:5: In fact, they reject the truth when it has come to them, so they are in a confused state
en.50:6: Have they not then looked at the sky above them: how We built it and adorned it ˹with stars˺, leaving it flawless
en.50:7: As for the earth, We spread it out and placed upon it firm mountains, and produced in it every type of pleasant plant—
en.50:8: ˹all as˺ an insight and a reminder to every servant who turns ˹to Allah˺
en.50:9: And We send down blessed rain from the sky, bringing forth gardens and grains for harvest
en.50:10: and towering palm trees ˹loaded˺ with clustered fruit

en.50:24: ˹It will be said to both angels,˺ “Throw into Hell every stubborn disbeliever

en.50:28: Allah will respond, “Do not dispute in My presence, since I had already given you a warning

en.50:30: ˹Beware of˺ the Day We will ask Hell, “Are you full ˹yet˺?” And it will respond, “Are there any more?”

en.50:32: ˹And it will be said to them,˺ “This is what you were promised, for whoever ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺ and kept up ˹His commandments˺—

en.50:37: Surely in this is a reminder for whoever has a ˹mindful˺ heart and lends an attentive ear
en.50:38: Indeed, We created the heavens and the earth and everything in between in six Days, and We were not ˹even˺ touched with fatigue
en.50:39: So be patient ˹O Prophet˺ with what they say. And glorify the praises of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset
en.50:40: And glorify Him during part of the night and after the prayers
en.50:41: And listen! On the Day the caller will call out from a near place
en.50:42: the Day all will hear the ˹mighty˺ Blast in ˹all˺ truth, that will be the Day of emergence ˹from the graves˺
en.50:43: It is certainly We Who give life and cause death. And to Us is the final return
en.50:44: ˹Beware of˺ the Day the earth will split open, letting them rush forth. That will be an easy gathering for Us
en.50:45: We know best what they say. And you ˹O Prophet˺ are not ˹there˺ to compel them ˹to believe˺. So remind with the Quran ˹only˺ those who fear My warning
en.51:4: and ˹the angels˺ administering affairs by ˹Allah’s˺ command
en.51:5: Indeed, what you are promised is true
en.51:6: And the Judgment will certainly come to pass
en.51:7: ˹And˺ by the heavens in their marvellous design

en.51:9: Only those ˹destined to be˺ deluded are turned away from it
en.51:11: those who are ˹steeped˺ in ignorance, totally heedless

en.51:13: ˹It is˺ the Day they will be tormented over the Fire
en.51:14: ˹They will be told,˺ “Taste your torment! This is what you sought to hasten.”

en.51:16: ˹joyfully˺ receiving what their Lord will grant them. Before this ˹reward˺ they were truly good-doers ˹in the world˺

en.51:19: And in their wealth there was a rightful share ˹fulfilled˺ for the beggar and the poor

en.51:21: as there are within yourselves. Can you not see

en.51:23: Then by the Lord of heaven and earth! ˹All˺ this is certainly as true as ˹the fact that˺ you can speak
en.51:24: Has the story of Abraham’s honoured guests reached you ˹O Prophet˺
en.51:25: ˹Remember˺ when they entered his presence and greeted ˹him with˺, “Peace!” He replied, “Peace ˹be upon you˺!” ˹Then he said to himself,˺ “˹These are˺ an unfamiliar people.”
en.51:26: Then he slipped off to his family and brought a fat ˹roasted˺ calf
en.51:27: and placed it before them, asking, “Will you not eat?”
en.51:28: ˹They did not eat,˺ so he grew fearful of them. They reassured ˹him˺, “Do not be afraid,” and gave him good news of a knowledgeable son
en.51:29: Then his wife came forward with a cry, clasping her forehead ˹in astonishment˺, exclaiming, “˹A baby from˺ a barren, old woman!”
en.51:30: They replied, “Such has your Lord decreed. He is truly the All-Wise, All-Knowing.”
en.51:31: ˹Later,˺ Abraham asked, “What is your mission, O messengers?”
en.51:32: They replied, “We have actually been sent to a wicked people
en.51:33: to send upon them stones of ˹baked˺ clay
en.51:34: marked by your Lord for the transgressors.”
en.51:35: Then ˹before the torment˺ We evacuated the believers from the city
en.51:36: But We only found one family that had submitted ˹to Allah˺
en.51:37: And We have left a sign there ˹as a lesson˺ for those who fear the painful punishment
en.51:38: And in ˹the story of˺ Moses ˹was another lesson,˺ when We sent him to Pharaoh with compelling proof
en.51:39: but Pharaoh was carried away by his power, saying ˹of Moses˺, “A magician or a madman!”
en.51:40: So We seized him and his soldiers, casting them into the sea while he was blameworthy
en.51:41: And in ˹the story of˺ ’Âd ˹was another lesson,˺ when We sent against them the devastating wind
en.51:42: There was nothing it came upon that it did not reduce to ashes
en.51:43: And in ˹the story of˺ Thamûd ˹was another lesson,˺ when they were told, “Enjoy yourselves ˹only˺ for a ˹short˺ while.”
en.51:44: Still they persisted in defying the commands of their Lord, so they were overtaken by a ˹mighty˺ blast while they were looking on
en.51:45: Then they were not able to rise up, nor were they helped
en.51:46: And the people of Noah ˹had also been destroyed˺ earlier. They were truly a rebellious people
en.51:47: We built the universe with ˹great˺ might, and We are certainly expanding ˹it˺
en.51:48: As for the earth, We spread it out. How superbly did We smooth it out
en.51:49: And We created pairs of all things so perhaps you would be mindful
en.51:50: So ˹proclaim, O Prophet˺: “Flee to Allah! I am truly sent by Him with a clear warning to you
en.51:51: And do not set up another god with Allah. I am truly sent by Him with a clear warning to you.”
en.51:52: Similarly, no messenger came to those before them without being told: “A magician or a madman!”
en.51:53: Have they passed this ˹cliché˺ down to one another? In fact, they have ˹all˺ been a transgressing people
en.51:54: So ˹now˺ turn away from them ˹O Prophet˺, for you will not be blamed
en.51:55: But ˹continue to˺ remind. For certainly reminders benefit the believers
en.51:56: I did not create jinn and humans except to worship Me
en.51:57: I seek no provision from them, nor do I need them to feed Me
en.51:58: Indeed, Allah ˹alone˺ is the Supreme Provider—Lord of all Power, Ever Mighty
en.51:59: The wrongdoers will certainly have a share ˹of the torment˺ like that of their predecessors. So do not let them ask Me to hasten ˹it˺
en.51:60: Woe then to the disbelievers when they face their Day which they are warned of
en.52:3: on open pages ˹for all to read˺

en.52:10: and the mountains will be blown away entirely
en.52:11: Then woe on that Day to the deniers—
en.52:12: those who amuse themselves with falsehood
en.52:13: ˹It is˺ the Day they will be fiercely shoved into the Fire of Hell
en.52:14: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is the Fire which you used to deny
en.52:15: Is this magic, or do you not see

en.52:17: Indeed, the righteous will be in Gardens and bliss

en.52:19: ˹They will be told,˺ “Eat and drink happily for what you used to do.”

en.52:22: And We will ˹continually˺ provide them with whatever fruit or meat they desire

en.52:24: And they will be waited on by their youthful servants like spotless pearls

en.52:26: They will say, “Before ˹this reward˺ we used to be in awe ˹of Allah˺ in the midst of our people
en.52:27: So Allah has graced us and protected us from the torment of ˹Hell’s˺ scorching heat
en.52:28: Indeed, we used to call upon Him ˹alone˺ before. He is truly the Most Kind, Most Merciful.”
en.52:29: So ˹continue to˺ remind ˹all, O Prophet˺. For you, by the grace of your Lord, are not a fortune-teller or a madman
en.52:30: Or do they say, “˹He is˺ a poet, for whom we ˹eagerly˺ await an ill-fate!”

en.52:32: Or do their ˹intelligent˺ minds prompt them to this ˹paradox˺? Or are they ˹just˺ a transgressing people
en.52:33: Or do they say, “He made this ˹Quran˺ up!”? In fact, they have no faith

en.52:37: Or do they possess the treasuries of your Lord, or are they in control ˹of everything˺
en.52:38: Or do they have a stairway, by which they eavesdrop ˹on the heavens˺? Then let those who do so bring a compelling proof

en.53:1: By the stars when they fade away

He knew well what would become of you as He created you from the earth and while you were ˹still˺ fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not ˹falsely˺ elevate yourselves.
