en.55:27: Only your Lord Himself, full of Majesty and Honour, will remain ˹forever˺
en.55:28: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:29: All those in the heavens and the earth are dependent on Him. Day in and day out He has something to bring about
en.55:30: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:31: We will soon attend to you ˹for judgment˺, O two multitudes ˹of jinn and humans˺
en.55:32: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:33: O assembly of jinn and humans! If you can penetrate beyond the realms of the heavens and the earth, then do so. ˹But˺ you cannot do that without ˹Our˺ authority
en.55:34: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:35: Flames of fire and ˹molten˺ copper will be sent against you, and you will not be able to defend one another
en.55:36: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:37: ˹How horrible will it be˺ when the heavens will split apart, becoming rose-red like ˹burnt˺ oil
en.55:38: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:39: On that Day there will be no need for any human or jinn to be asked about their sins
en.55:40: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:41: The wicked will be recognized by their appearance, then will be seized by ˹their˺ forelocks and feet
en.55:42: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:43: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is the Hell which the wicked denied.”
en.55:44: They will alternate between its flames and scalding water
en.55:45: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:46: And whoever is in awe of standing before their Lord will have two Gardens
en.55:47: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:49: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:50: In each ˹Garden˺ will be two flowing springs
en.55:51: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:52: In each will be two types of every fruit
en.55:53: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:54: Those ˹believers˺ will recline on furnishings lined with rich brocade. And the fruit of both Gardens will hang within reach
en.55:55: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:56: In both ˹Gardens˺ will be maidens of modest gaze, who no human or jinn has ever touched before
en.55:57: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:58: Those ˹maidens˺ will be ˹as elegant˺ as rubies and coral
en.55:59: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:60: Is there any reward for goodness except goodness
en.55:61: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:62: And below these two ˹Gardens˺ will be two others
en.55:63: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:65: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:66: In each will be two gushing springs
en.55:67: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:68: In both will be fruit, palm trees, and pomegranates
en.55:69: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:70: In all Gardens will be noble, pleasant mates
en.55:71: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:72: ˹They will be˺ maidens with gorgeous eyes, reserved in pavilions
en.55:73: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:74: No human or jinn has ever touched these ˹maidens˺ before
en.55:75: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:76: All ˹believers˺ will be reclining on green cushions and splendid carpets
en.55:77: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny
en.55:78: Blessed is the Name of your Lord, full of Majesty and Honour
en.56:1: When the Inevitable Event takes place
en.56:2: then no one can deny it has come
en.56:4: When the earth will be violently shaken
en.56:5: and the mountains will be crushed to pieces
en.56:7: you will ˹all˺ be ˹divided into˺ three groups
en.56:8: the people of the right, how ˹blessed˺ will they be
en.56:9: the people of the left, how ˹miserable˺ will they be
en.56:10: and the foremost ˹in faith˺ will be the foremost ˹in Paradise˺
en.56:11: They are the ones nearest ˹to Allah˺
en.56:13: ˹They will be˺ a multitude from earlier generations
en.56:14: and a few from later generations
en.56:17: They will be waited on by eternal youths
en.56:18: with cups, pitchers, and a drink ˹of pure wine˺ from a flowing stream
en.56:19: that will cause them neither headache nor intoxication
en.56:20: ˹They will also be served˺ any fruit they choose
en.56:21: and meat from any bird they desire
en.56:22: And ˹they will have˺ maidens with gorgeous eyes
en.56:24: ˹all˺ as a reward for what they used to do
en.56:25: There they will never hear any idle or sinful talk—
en.56:27: And the people of the right—how ˹blessed˺ will they be
en.56:28: ˹They will be˺ amid thornless lote trees
en.56:33: never out of season nor forbidden—
en.56:35: Indeed, We will have perfectly created their mates
en.56:38: for the people of the right
en.56:39: ˹who will be˺ a multitude from earlier generations
en.56:40: and a multitude from later generations
en.56:41: And the people of the left—how ˹miserable˺ will they be
en.56:42: ˹They will be˺ in scorching heat and boiling water
en.56:43: in the shade of black smoke
en.56:45: Indeed, before this ˹torment˺ they were spoiled by luxury
en.56:46: and persisted in the worst of sin
en.56:47: They used to ask ˹mockingly˺, “When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected
en.56:49: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Most certainly, earlier and later generations
en.56:50: will surely be gathered ˹together˺ for the appointed Day
en.56:52: will certainly eat from ˹the fruit of˺ the trees of Zaqqûm
en.56:54: Then on top of that you will drink boiling water—
en.56:55: and you will drink ˹it˺ like thirsty camels do.”
en.56:56: This will be their accommodation on the Day of Judgment
en.56:57: It is We Who created you. Will you not then believe ˹in resurrection˺
en.56:58: Have you considered what you ejaculate
en.56:59: Is it you who create ˹a child out of˺ it, or is it We Who do so
en.56:60: We have ordained death for ˹all of˺ you, and We cannot be prevented
en.56:61: from transforming and recreating you in forms unknown to you
en.56:62: You already know how you were first created. Will you not then be mindful
en.56:63: Have you considered what you sow
en.56:64: Is it you who cause it to grow, or is it We Who do so
en.56:65: If We willed, We could simply reduce this ˹harvest˺ to chaff, leaving you to lament
en.56:67: In fact, we have been deprived ˹of our livelihood˺.”
en.56:68: Have you considered the water you drink
en.56:69: Is it you who bring it down from the clouds, or is it We Who do so
en.56:70: If We willed, We could make it salty. Will you not then give thanks
en.56:71: Have you considered the fire you kindle
en.56:72: Is it you who produce its trees, or is it We Who do so
en.56:73: We have made it ˹as˺ a reminder ˹of the Hellfire˺ and a provision for the travellers
en.56:74: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest
en.56:75: So I do swear by the positions of the stars—
en.56:76: and this, if only you knew, is indeed a great oath—
en.56:77: that this is truly a noble Quran
en.56:79: touched by none except the purified ˹angels˺
en.56:80: ˹It is˺ a revelation from the Lord of all worlds
en.56:81: How can you then take this message lightly
en.56:82: and repay ˹Allah for˺ your provisions with denial
en.56:83: Why then ˹are you helpless˺ when the soul ˹of a dying person˺ reaches ˹their˺ throat
en.56:85: And We are nearer to such a person than you, but you cannot see
en.56:86: Now, if you are not subject to Our Will ˹as you claim˺
en.56:87: bring that soul back, if what you say is true
en.56:88: So, if the deceased is one of those brought near ˹to Us˺
en.56:89: then ˹such a person will have˺ serenity, fragrance, and a Garden of Bliss
en.56:90: And if the deceased is one of the people of the right
en.56:91: then ˹they will be told,˺ “Greetings to you from the people of the right.”
en.56:92: But if such person is one of the misguided deniers
en.56:93: then their accommodation will be boiling water ˹to drink˺
en.56:95: Indeed, this is the absolute truth
en.56:96: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest
en.57:1: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, for He is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.57:2: To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And He is Most Capable of everything
en.57:3: He is the First and the Last, the Most High and Most Near, and He has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
en.57:4: He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days, then established Himself on the Throne. He knows whatever goes into the earth and whatever comes out of it, and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends into it. And He is with you wherever you are. For Allah is All-Seeing of what you do
en.57:5: To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allah all matters are returned
en.57:6: He merges the night into day and the day into night. And He knows best what is ˹hidden˺ in the heart
en.57:7: Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and donate from what He has entrusted you with. So those of you who believe and donate will have a mighty reward
en.57:8: Why do you not believe in Allah while the Messenger is inviting you to have faith in your Lord, although He has already taken your covenant, if you will ever believe
en.57:9: He is the One Who sends down clear revelations to His servant to bring you out of darkness and into light. For indeed Allah is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to you
en.57:10: And why should you not spend in the cause of Allah, while Allah is the ˹sole˺ inheritor of the heavens and the earth? Those of you who donated and fought before the victory ˹over Mecca˺ are unparalleled. They are far greater in rank than those who donated and fought afterwards. Yet Allah has promised each a fine reward. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.57:11: Who is it that will lend to Allah a good loan which Allah will multiply ˹many times over˺ for them, and they will have an honourable reward
en.57:12: On that Day you will see believing men and women with their light shining ahead of them and on their right. ˹They will be told,˺ “Today you have good news of Gardens, under which rivers flow, ˹for you˺ to stay in forever. This is ˹truly˺ the ultimate triumph.”
en.57:13: On that Day hypocrite men and women will beg the believers, “Wait for us so that we may have some of your light.” It will be said ˹mockingly˺, “Go back ˹to the world˺ and seek a light ˹there˺!” Then a ˹separating˺ wall with a gate will be erected between them. On the near side will be grace and on the far side will be torment
en.57:14: The tormented will cry out to those graced, “Were we not with you?” They will reply, “Yes ˹you were˺. But you chose to be tempted ˹by hypocrisy˺, ˹eagerly˺ awaited ˹our demise˺, doubted ˹the truth˺, and were deluded by false hopes until Allah’s decree ˹of your death˺ came to pass. And ˹so˺ the Chief Deceiver deceived you about Allah
en.57:15: So Today no ransom will be accepted from you ˹hypocrites˺, nor from the disbelievers. Your home is the Fire—it is the ˹only˺ fitting place for you. What an evil destination!”
en.57:16: Has the time not yet come for believers’ hearts to be humbled at the remembrance of Allah and what has been revealed of the truth, and not be like those given the Scripture before—˹those˺ who were spoiled for so long that their hearts became hardened. And many of them are ˹still˺ rebellious
en.57:17: Know that Allah revives the earth after its death. We have certainly made the signs clear for you so perhaps you will understand
en.57:18: Indeed, those men and women who give in charity and lend to Allah a good loan will have it multiplied for them, and they will have an honourable reward
en.57:19: ˹As for˺ those who believe in Allah and His messengers, it is they who are ˹truly˺ the people of truth. And the martyrs, with their Lord, will have their reward and their light. But ˹as for˺ those who disbelieve and reject Our signs, it is they who will be the residents of the Hellfire
en.57:20: Know that this worldly life is no more than play, amusement, luxury, mutual boasting, and competition in wealth and children. This is like rain that causes plants to grow, to the delight of the planters. But later the plants dry up and you see them wither, then they are reduced to chaff. And in the Hereafter there will be either severe punishment or forgiveness and pleasure of Allah, whereas the life of this world is no more than the delusion of enjoyment
en.57:21: ˹So˺ compete with one another for forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who believe in Allah and His messengers. This is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty
en.57:22: No calamity ˹or blessing˺ occurs on earth or in yourselves without being ˹written˺ in a Record before We bring it into being. This is certainly easy for Allah
en.57:23: ˹We let you know this˺ so that you neither grieve over what you have missed nor boast over what He has granted you. For Allah does not like whoever is arrogant, boastful—
en.57:24: those who are stingy and promote stinginess among people. And whoever turns away ˹should know that˺ Allah ˹alone˺ is truly the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy
en.57:25: Indeed, We sent Our messengers with clear proofs, and with them We sent down the Scripture and the balance ˹of justice˺ so that people may administer justice. And We sent down iron with its great might, benefits for humanity, and means for Allah to prove who ˹is willing to˺ stand up for Him and His messengers without seeing Him. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty
en.57:26: And indeed, We sent Noah and Abraham and reserved prophethood and revelation for their descendants. Some of them are ˹rightly˺ guided, while most are rebellious
en.57:27: Then in the footsteps of these ˹prophets˺, We sent Our messengers, and ˹after them˺ We sent Jesus, son of Mary, and granted him the Gospel, and instilled compassion and mercy into the hearts of his followers. As for monasticism, they made it up—We never ordained it for them—only seeking to please Allah, yet they did not ˹even˺ observe it strictly. So We rewarded those of them who were faithful. But most of them are rebellious
en.57:28: O people of faith! Fear Allah and believe in His Messenger. ˹And˺ He will grant you a double share of His mercy, provide you with a light to walk in ˹on Judgment Day˺, and forgive you. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.57:29: ˹This is so˺ that the People of the Book ˹who deny the Prophet˺ may know that they do not have any control over Allah’s grace, and that all grace is in Allah’s Hands. He grants it to whoever He wills. For Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty
en.58:1: Indeed, Allah has heard the argument of the woman who pleaded with you ˹O Prophet˺ concerning her husband, and appealed to Allah. Allah has heard your exchange. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing
en.58:2: Those of you who ˹sinfully˺ divorce their wives by comparing them to their mothers ˹should know that˺ their wives are in no way their mothers. None can be their mothers except those who gave birth to them. What they say is certainly detestable and false. Yet Allah is truly Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving
en.58:3: Those who divorce their wives in this manner, then ˹wish to˺ retract what they said, must free a slave before they touch each other. This ˹penalty˺ is meant to deter you. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.58:4: But if the husband cannot afford this, let him then fast two consecutive months before the couple touch each other. But if he is unable ˹to fast˺, then let him feed sixty poor people. This is to re-affirm your faith in Allah and His Messenger. These are the limits set by Allah. And the disbelievers will suffer a painful punishment
en.58:5: Indeed, those who defy Allah and His Messenger will be debased, just like those before them. We have certainly sent down clear revelations. And the disbelievers will suffer a humiliating punishment
en.58:6: On the Day Allah resurrects them all together, He will then inform them of what they have done. Allah has kept account of it all, while they have forgotten it. For Allah is a Witness over all things
en.58:7: Do you not see that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? If three converse privately, He is their fourth. If five, He is their sixth. Whether fewer or more, He is with them wherever they may be. Then, on the Day of Judgment, He will inform them of what they have done. Surely Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
en.58:8: Have you not seen those who were forbidden from secret talks, yet they ˹always˺ return to what they were forbidden from, conspiring in sin, aggression, and disobedience to the Messenger? And when they come to you ˹O Prophet˺, they greet you not as Allah greets you, and say to one another, “Why does Allah not punish us for what we say?” Hell is enough for them—they will burn in it. And what an evil destination
en.58:9: O believers! When you converse privately, let it not be for sin, aggression, or disobedience to the Messenger, but let it be for goodness and righteousness. And fear Allah, to Whom you will ˹all˺ be gathered
en.58:10: Secret talks are only inspired by Satan to grieve the believers. Yet he cannot harm them whatsoever except by Allah’s Will. So in Allah let the believers put their trust
en.58:11: O believers! When you are told to make room in gatherings, then do so. Allah will make room for you ˹in His grace˺. And if you are told to rise, then do so. Allah will elevate those of you who are faithful, and ˹raise˺ those gifted with knowledge in rank. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.58:12: O believers! When you consult the Messenger privately, give something in charity before your consultation. That is better and purer for you. But if you lack the means, then Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.58:13: Are you afraid of spending in charity before your private consultations ˹with him˺? Since you are unable to do so, and Allah has turned to you in mercy, then ˹continue to˺ establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and obey Allah and His Messenger. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.58:14: Have you not seen those ˹hypocrites˺ who ally themselves with a people with whom Allah is displeased? They are neither with you nor with them. And they swear to lies knowingly
en.58:15: Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. Evil indeed is what they do
en.58:16: They have made their ˹false˺ oaths as a shield, hindering ˹others˺ from the cause of Allah. So they will suffer a humiliating punishment
en.58:17: Neither their wealth nor children will be of any help to them against Allah whatsoever. It is they who will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever
en.58:18: On the Day Allah resurrects them all, they will ˹falsely˺ swear to Him as they swear to you, thinking they have something to stand on. Indeed, it is they who are the ˹total˺ liars
en.58:19: Satan has taken hold of them, causing them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are the party of Satan. Surely Satan’s party is bound to lose
en.58:20: ˹As for˺ those who defy Allah and His Messenger, they will definitely be among the most debased
en.58:21: Allah has decreed, “I and My messengers will certainly prevail.” Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty
en.58:22: You will never find a people who ˹truly˺ believe in Allah and the Last Day loyal to those who defy Allah and His Messenger, even if they were their parents, children, siblings, or extended family. For those ˹believers˺, Allah has instilled faith in their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Him. He will admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever. Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. They are the party of Allah. Indeed, Allah’s party is bound to succeed
en.59:1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.59:2: He is the One Who expelled the disbelievers of the People of the Book from their homes for ˹their˺ first banishment ˹ever˺. You never thought they would go. And they thought their strongholds would put them out of Allah’s reach. But ˹the decree of˺ Allah came upon them from where they never expected. And He cast horror into their hearts so they destroyed their houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers. So take a lesson ˹from this˺, O people of insight
en.59:3: Had Allah not decreed exile for them, He would have certainly punished them in this world. And in the Hereafter they will suffer the punishment of the Fire
en.59:4: This is because they defied Allah and His Messenger. And whoever defies Allah, then Allah is truly severe in punishment
en.59:5: Whatever palm trees you ˹believers˺ cut down or left standing intact, it was ˹all˺ by Allah’s Will, so that He might disgrace the rebellious
en.59:6: As for the gains Allah has turned over to His Messenger from them—you did not ˹even˺ spur on any horse or camel for such gains. But Allah gives authority to His messengers over whoever He wills. For Allah is Most Capable of everything
en.59:7: As for gains granted by Allah to His Messenger from the people of ˹other˺ lands, they are for Allah and the Messenger, his close relatives, orphans, the poor, and ˹needy˺ travellers so that wealth may not merely circulate among your rich. Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it. And whatever he forbids you from, leave it. And fear Allah. Surely Allah is severe in punishment
en.59:8: ˹Some of the gains will be˺ for poor emigrants who were driven out of their homes and wealth, seeking Allah’s bounty and pleasure, and standing up for Allah and His Messenger. They are the ones true in faith
en.59:9: As for those who had settled in the city and ˹embraced˺ the faith before ˹the arrival of˺ the emigrants, they love whoever immigrates to them, never having a desire in their hearts for whatever ˹of the gains˺ is given to the emigrants. They give ˹the emigrants˺ preference over themselves even though they may be in need. And whoever is saved from the selfishness of their own souls, it is they who are ˹truly˺ successful
en.59:10: And those who come after them will pray, “Our Lord! Forgive us and our fellow believers who preceded us in faith, and do not allow bitterness into our hearts towards those who believe. Our Lord! Indeed, You are Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.”
en.59:11: Have you ˹O Prophet˺ not seen the hypocrites who say to their fellow disbelievers from the People of the Book, “If you are expelled, we will certainly leave with you, and We will never obey anyone against you. And if you are fought against, we will surely help you.”? But Allah bears witness that they are truly liars
en.59:12: Indeed, if they are expelled, the hypocrites will never leave with them. And if they are fought against, the hypocrites will never help them. And even if the hypocrites did so, they would certainly flee, then the disbelievers would be left with no help
en.59:13: Indeed, there is more fear in their hearts for you ˹believers˺ than for Allah. That is because they are a people who do not comprehend
en.59:14: Even united, they would not ˹dare˺ fight against you except ˹from˺ within fortified strongholds or from behind walls. Their malice for each other is intense: you think they are united, yet their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people with no ˹real˺ understanding
en.59:15: They are ˹both just˺ like those who recently went down before them: they tasted the evil consequences of their doings. And they will suffer a painful punishment
en.59:16: ˹They are˺ like Satan when he lures someone to disbelieve. Then after they have done so, he will say ˹on Judgment Day˺, “I have absolutely nothing to do with you. I truly fear Allah—the Lord of all worlds.”
en.59:17: So they will both end up in the Fire, staying there forever. That is the reward of the wrongdoers
en.59:18: O believers! Be mindful of Allah and let every soul look to what ˹deeds˺ it has sent forth for tomorrow. And fear Allah, ˹for˺ certainly Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.59:19: And do not be like those who forgot Allah, so He made them forget themselves. It is they who are ˹truly˺ rebellious
en.59:20: The residents of the Fire cannot be equal to the residents of Paradise. ˹Only˺ the residents of Paradise will be successful
en.59:21: Had We sent down this Quran upon a mountain, you would have certainly seen it humbled and torn apart in awe of Allah. We set forth such comparisons for people, ˹so˺ perhaps they may reflect
en.59:22: He is Allah—there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him: Knower of the seen and unseen. He is the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful
en.59:23: He is Allah—there is no god except Him: the King, the Most Holy, the All-Perfect, the Source of Serenity, the Watcher ˹of all˺, the Almighty, the Supreme in Might, the Majestic. Glorified is Allah far above what they associate with Him ˹in worship˺
en.59:24: He is Allah: the Creator, the Inventor, the Shaper. He ˹alone˺ has the Most Beautiful Names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth ˹constantly˺ glorifies Him. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.60:1: O believers! Do not take My enemies and yours as trusted allies, showing them affection even though they deny what has come to you of the truth. They drove the Messenger and yourselves out ˹of Mecca˺, simply for your belief in Allah, your Lord. If you ˹truly˺ emigrated to struggle in My cause and seek My pleasure, ˹then do not take them as allies,˺ disclosing secrets ˹of the believers˺ to the pagans out of affection for them, when I know best whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal. And whoever of you does this has truly strayed from the Right Way
en.60:2: If they gain the upper hand over you, they would be your ˹open˺ enemies, unleashing their hands and tongues to harm you, and wishing that you would abandon faith
en.60:3: Neither your relatives nor children will benefit you on Judgment Day—He will decide between you ˹all˺. For Allah is All-Seeing of what you do
en.60:4: You already have an excellent example in Abraham and those with him, when they said to their people, “We totally dissociate ourselves from you and ˹shun˺ whatever ˹idols˺ you worship besides Allah. We reject you. The enmity and hatred that has arisen between us and you will last until you believe in Allah alone.” The only exception is when Abraham said to his father, “I will seek forgiveness for you,˹” adding, “but˺ I cannot protect you from Allah at all.” ˹The believers prayed,˺ “Our Lord! In You we trust. And to You we ˹always˺ turn. And to You is the final return
en.60:5: Our Lord! Do not subject us to the persecution of the disbelievers. Forgive us, our Lord! You ˹alone˺ are truly the Almighty, All-Wise.”
en.60:6: You certainly have an excellent example in them for whoever has hope in Allah and the Last Day. But whoever turns away, then surely Allah ˹alone˺ is the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy
en.60:7: ˹In time,˺ Allah may bring about goodwill between you and those of them you ˹now˺ hold as enemies. For Allah is Most Capable. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.60:8: Allah does not forbid you from dealing kindly and fairly with those who have neither fought nor driven you out of your homes. Surely Allah loves those who are fair
en.60:9: Allah only forbids you from befriending those who have fought you for ˹your˺ faith, driven you out of your homes, or supported ˹others˺ in doing so. And whoever takes them as friends, then it is they who are the ˹true˺ wrongdoers
en.60:10: O believers! When the believing women come to you as emigrants, test their intentions—their faith is best known to Allah—and if you find them to be believers, then do not send them back to the disbelievers. These ˹women˺ are not lawful ˹wives˺ for the disbelievers, nor are the disbelievers lawful ˹husbands˺ for them. ˹But˺ repay the disbelievers whatever ˹dowries˺ they had paid. And there is no blame on you if you marry these ˹women˺ as long as you pay them their dowries. And do not hold on to marriage with polytheistic women. ˹But˺ demand ˹repayment of˺ whatever ˹dowries˺ you had paid, and let the disbelievers do the same. That is the judgment of Allah—He judges between you. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.60:11: And if any of your wives desert you to the disbelievers, and later you take spoils from them, then pay those whose wives have gone, the equivalent of whatever ˹dowry˺ they had paid. And be mindful of Allah, in Whom you believe
en.60:12: O Prophet! When the believing women come to you, pledging to you that they will neither associate anything with Allah ˹in worship˺, nor steal, nor fornicate, nor kill their children, nor falsely attribute ˹illegitimate˺ children to their husbands, nor disobey you in what is right, then accept their pledge, and ask Allah to forgive them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.60:13: O believers! Do not ally yourselves with a people Allah is displeased with. They already have no hope for the Hereafter, just like the disbelievers lying in ˹their˺ graves
en.61:1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. For He ˹alone˺ is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.61:2: O believers! Why do you say what you do not do
en.61:3: How despicable it is in the sight of Allah that you say what you do not do
en.61:4: Surely Allah loves those who fight in His cause in ˹solid˺ ranks as if they were one concrete structure
en.61:5: ˹Remember, O Prophet,˺ when Moses said to his people, “O my people! Why do you hurt me when you already know I am Allah’s messenger to you?” So when they ˹persistently˺ deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate. For Allah does not guide the rebellious people
en.61:6: And ˹remember˺ when Jesus, son of Mary, said, “O children of Israel! I am truly Allah’s messenger to you, confirming the Torah which came before me, and giving good news of a messenger after me whose name will be Aḥmad.” Yet when the Prophet came to them with clear proofs, they said, “This is pure magic.”
en.61:7: Who does more wrong than the one who fabricates lies about Allah when invited to submit ˹to Him˺? For Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people
en.61:8: They wish to extinguish Allah’s light with their mouths, but Allah will ˹certainly˺ perfect His light, even to the dismay of the disbelievers
en.61:9: He is the One Who has sent His Messenger with ˹true˺ guidance and the religion of truth, making it prevail over all others, even to the dismay of the polytheists
en.61:10: O believers! Shall I guide you to an exchange that will save you from a painful punishment
en.61:11: ˹It is to˺ have faith in Allah and His Messenger, and strive in the cause of Allah with your wealth and your lives. That is best for you, if only you knew
en.61:12: He will forgive your sins, and admit you into Gardens under which rivers flow, and ˹house you in˺ splendid homes in the Gardens of Eternity. That is the ultimate triumph
en.61:13: ˹He will also give you˺ another favour that you long for: help from Allah and an imminent victory. ˹So˺ give good news ˹O Prophet˺ to the believers
en.61:14: O believers! Stand up for Allah, as Jesus, son of Mary, asked the disciples, “Who will stand up with me for Allah?” The disciples replied, “We will stand up for Allah.” Then a group from the Children of Israel believed while another disbelieved. We then supported the believers against their enemies, so they prevailed
en.62:1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth ˹constantly˺ glorifies Allah—the King, the Most Holy, the Almighty, the All-Wise
en.62:2: He is the One Who raised for the illiterate ˹people˺ a messenger from among themselves—reciting to them His revelations, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom, for indeed they had previously been clearly astray—
en.62:3: along with others of them who have not yet joined them ˹in faith˺. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise
en.62:4: This is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty
en.62:5: The example of those who were entrusted with ˹observing˺ the Torah but failed to do so, is that of a donkey carrying books. How evil is the example of those who reject Allah’s signs! For Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people
en.62:6: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “O Jews! If you claim to be Allah’s chosen ˹people˺ out of all humanity, then wish for death, if what you say is true.”
en.62:7: But they will never wish for that because of what their hands have done. And Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of the wrongdoers
en.62:8: Say, “The death you are running away from will inevitably come to you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the seen and unseen, and He will inform you of what you used to do.”
en.62:9: O believers! When the call to prayer is made on Friday, then proceed ˹diligently˺ to the remembrance of Allah and leave off ˹your˺ business. That is best for you, if only you knew
en.62:10: Once the prayer is over, disperse throughout the land and seek the bounty of Allah. And remember Allah often so you may be successful
en.62:11: When they saw the fanfare along with the caravan, they ˹almost all˺ flocked to it, leaving you ˹O Prophet˺ standing ˹on the pulpit˺. Say, “What is with Allah is far better than amusement and merchandise. And Allah is the Best Provider.”
en.63:1: When the hypocrites come to you ˹O Prophet˺, they say, “We bear witness that you are certainly the Messenger of Allah”—and surely Allah knows that you are His Messenger—but Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are truly liars
en.63:2: They have made their ˹false˺ oaths as a shield, hindering ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah. Evil indeed is what they do
en.63:3: This is because they believed and then abandoned faith. Therefore, their hearts have been sealed, so they do not comprehend
en.63:4: When you see them, their appearance impresses you. And when they speak, you listen to their ˹impressive˺ speech. But they are ˹just˺ like ˹worthless˺ planks of wood leaned ˹against a wall˺. They think every cry is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allah condemn them! How can they be deluded ˹from the truth˺
en.63:5: When it is said to them, “Come! The Messenger of Allah will pray for you to be forgiven,” they turn their heads ˹in disgust˺, and you see them ˹O Prophet˺ turn away in arrogance
en.63:6: It is the same whether you pray for their forgiveness or not, Allah will not forgive them. Surely Allah does not guide the rebellious people
en.63:7: They are the ones who say ˹to one another˺, “Do not spend ˹anything˺ on those ˹emigrants˺ with the Messenger of Allah so that they will break away ˹from him˺.” But to Allah ˹alone˺ belong the treasuries of the heavens and the earth, yet the hypocrites do not comprehend
en.63:8: They say, “If we return to Medina, the honourable will definitely expel the inferior.” But all honour and power belongs to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, yet the hypocrites do not know
en.63:9: O believers! Do not let your wealth or your children divert you from the remembrance of Allah. For whoever does so, it is they who are the ˹true˺ losers
en.63:10: And donate from what We have provided for you before death comes to one of you, and you cry, “My Lord! If only You delayed me for a short while, I would give in charity and be one of the righteous.”
en.63:11: But Allah never delays a soul when its appointed time comes. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.64:1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth ˹constantly˺ glorifies Allah. The kingdom is His, and all praise is for Him. For He is Most Capable of everything
en.64:2: He is the One Who created you, yet some of you are disbelievers while some are believers. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do
en.64:3: He created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. He shaped you ˹in the womb˺, perfecting your form. And to Him is the final return
en.64:4: He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And He knows whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal. For Allah knows best what is ˹hidden˺ in the heart
en.64:5: Have the stories of those who disbelieved before not reached you ˹pagans˺? They tasted the evil consequences of their doings, and they will suffer a painful punishment
en.64:6: That was because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs, but they said ˹mockingly˺, “How can humans be our guides?” So they persisted in disbelief and turned away. And Allah was not in need ˹of their faith˺. For Allah is Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy
en.64:7: The disbelievers claim they will not be resurrected. Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Yes, by my Lord, you will surely be resurrected, then you will certainly be informed of what you have done. And that is easy for Allah.”
en.64:8: So believe in Allah and His Messenger and in the Light We have revealed. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do
en.64:9: ˹Consider˺ the Day He will gather you ˹all˺ for the Day of Gathering—that will be the Day of mutual loss and gain. So whoever believes in Allah and does good, He will absolve them of their sins and admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. That is the ultimate triumph
en.64:10: As for those who disbelieve and reject Our revelations, they will be the residents of the Fire, staying there forever. What an evil destination
en.64:11: No calamity befalls ˹anyone˺ except by Allah’s Will. And whoever has faith in Allah, He will ˹rightly˺ guide their hearts ˹through adversity˺. And Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things
en.64:12: Obey Allah and obey the Messenger! But if you turn away, then Our Messenger’s duty is only to deliver ˹the message˺ clearly
en.64:13: Allah—there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. So in Allah let the believers put their trust
en.64:14: O believers! Indeed, some of your spouses and children are enemies to you, so beware of them. But if you pardon, overlook, and forgive ˹their faults˺, then Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.64:15: Your wealth and children are only a test, but Allah ˹alone˺ has a great reward
en.64:16: So be mindful of Allah to the best of your ability, hear and obey, and spend in charity—that will be best for you. And whoever is saved from the selfishness of their own souls, it is they who are ˹truly˺ successful
en.64:17: If you lend to Allah a good loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you. For Allah is Most Appreciative, Most Forbearing
en.64:18: ˹He is the˺ Knower of the seen and unseen—the Almighty, All-Wise
en.65:1: O Prophet! ˹Instruct the believers:˺ When you ˹intend to˺ divorce women, then divorce them with concern for their waiting period, and count it accurately. And fear Allah, your Lord. Do not force them out of their homes, nor should they leave—unless they commit a blatant misconduct. These are the limits set by Allah. And whoever transgresses Allah’s limits has truly wronged his own soul. You never know, perhaps Allah will bring about a change ˹of heart˺ later
en.65:2: Then when they have ˹almost˺ reached the end of their waiting period, either retain them honourably or separate from them honourably. And call two of your reliable men to witness ˹either way˺—and ˹let the witnesses˺ bear true testimony for ˹the sake of˺ Allah. This is enjoined on whoever has faith in Allah and the Last Day. And whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make a way out for them
en.65:3: and provide for them from sources they could never imagine. And whoever puts their trust in Allah, then He ˹alone˺ is sufficient for them. Certainly Allah achieves His Will. Allah has already set a destiny for everything
en.65:4: As for your women past the age of menstruation, in case you do not know, their waiting period is three months, and those who have not menstruated as well. As for those who are pregnant, their waiting period ends with delivery. And whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make their matters easy for them
en.65:5: This is the commandment of Allah, which He has revealed to you. And whoever is mindful of Allah, He will absolve them of their sins and reward them immensely
en.65:6: Let them live where you live ˹during their waiting period˺, according to your means. And do not harass them to make their stay unbearable. If they are pregnant, then maintain them until they deliver. And if they nurse your child, compensate them, and consult together courteously. But if you fail to reach an agreement, then another woman will nurse ˹the child˺ for the father
en.65:7: Let the man of wealth provide according to his means. As for the one with limited resources, let him provide according to whatever Allah has given him. Allah does not require of any soul beyond what He has given it. After hardship, Allah will bring about ease
en.65:8: ˹Imagine˺ how many societies rebelled against the commandments of their Lord and His messengers, so We called each ˹society˺ to a severe account and subjected them to a horrible punishment
en.65:9: So they tasted the evil consequences of their doings, and the outcome of their doings was ˹total˺ loss
en.65:10: Allah has ˹also˺ prepared for them a severe punishment. So fear Allah, O people of reason and faith. Allah has indeed revealed to you a Reminder
en.65:11: ˹and sent˺ a messenger reciting to you Allah’s revelations, making things clear so that He may bring those who believe and do good out of darkness and into light. And whoever believes in Allah and does good will be admitted by Him into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. Allah will have indeed granted them an excellent provision
en.65:12: Allah is the One Who created seven heavens ˹in layers˺, and likewise for the earth. The ˹divine˺ command descends between them so you may know that Allah is Most Capable of everything and that Allah certainly encompasses all things in ˹His˺ knowledge
en.66:1: O Prophet! Why do you prohibit ˹yourself˺ from what Allah has made lawful to you, seeking to please your wives? And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
en.66:2: Allah has already ordained for you ˹believers˺ the way to absolve yourselves from your oaths. For Allah is your Guardian. And He is the All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.66:3: ˹Remember˺ when the Prophet had ˹once˺ confided something to one of his wives, then when she disclosed it ˹to another wife˺ and Allah made it known to him, he presented ˹to her˺ part of what was disclosed and overlooked a part. So when he informed her of it, she exclaimed, “Who told you this?” He replied, “I was informed by the All-Knowing, All-Aware.”
en.66:4: ˹It will be better˺ if you ˹wives˺ both turn to Allah in repentance, for your hearts have certainly faltered. But if you ˹continue to˺ collaborate against him, then ˹know that˺ Allah Himself is his Guardian. And Gabriel, the righteous believers, and the angels are ˹all˺ his supporters as well
en.66:5: Perhaps, if he were to divorce you ˹all˺, his Lord would replace you with better wives who are submissive ˹to Allah˺, faithful ˹to Him˺, devout, repentant, dedicated to worship and fasting—previously married or virgins
en.66:6: O believers! Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, overseen by formidable and severe angels, who never disobey whatever Allah orders—always doing as commanded
en.66:7: ˹The deniers will then be told,˺ “O disbelievers! Make no excuses this Day! You are only rewarded for what you used to do.”
en.66:8: O believers! Turn to Allah in sincere repentance, so your Lord may absolve you of your sins and admit you into Gardens, under which rivers flow, on the Day Allah will not disgrace the Prophet or the believers with him. Their light will shine ahead of them and on their right. They will say, “Our Lord! Perfect our light for us, and forgive us. ˹For˺ You are truly Most Capable of everything.”
en.66:9: O Prophet! Struggle against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be firm with them. Hell will be their home. What an evil destination
en.66:10: Allah sets forth an example for the disbelievers: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. Each was married to one of Our righteous servants, yet betrayed them. So their husbands were of no benefit to them against Allah whatsoever. Both were told, “Enter the Fire, along with the others!”
en.66:11: And Allah sets forth an example for the believers: the wife of Pharaoh, who prayed, “My Lord! Build me a house in Paradise near You, deliver me from Pharaoh and his ˹evil˺ doing, and save me from the wrongdoing people.”
en.66:12: ˹There is˺ also ˹the example of˺ Mary, the daughter of ’Imrân, who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her ˹womb˺ through Our angel ˹Gabriel˺. She testified to the words of her Lord and His Scriptures, and was one of the ˹sincerely˺ devout
en.67:1: Blessed is the One in Whose Hands rests all authority. And He is Most Capable of everything
en.67:2: ˹He is the One˺ Who created death and life in order to test which of you is best in deeds. And He is the Almighty, All-Forgiving
en.67:3: ˹He is the One˺ Who created seven heavens, one above the other. You will never see any imperfection in the creation of the Most Compassionate. So look again: do you see any flaws
en.67:4: Then look again and again—your sight will return frustrated and weary
en.67:5: And indeed, We adorned the lowest heaven with ˹stars like˺ lamps, and made them ˹as missiles˺ for stoning ˹eavesdropping˺ devils, for whom We have also prepared the torment of the Blaze
en.67:6: Those who disbelieve in their Lord will suffer the punishment of Hell. What an evil destination
en.67:7: When they are tossed into it, they will hear its roaring as it boils over
en.67:8: almost bursting in fury. Every time a group is cast into it, its keepers will ask them, “Did a warner not come to you?”
en.67:9: They will reply, “Yes, a warner did come to us, but we denied and said, ‘Allah has revealed nothing. You are extremely astray.’”
en.67:10: And they will lament, “If only we had listened and reasoned, we would not be among the residents of the Blaze!”
en.67:11: And so they will confess their sins. So away with the residents of the Blaze
en.67:12: Indeed, those in awe of their Lord without seeing Him will have forgiveness and a mighty reward
en.67:13: Whether you speak secretly or openly—He surely knows best what is ˹hidden˺ in the heart
en.67:14: How could He not know His Own creation? For He ˹alone˺ is the Most Subtle, All-Aware
en.67:15: He is the One Who smoothed out the earth for you, so move about in its regions and eat from His provisions. And to Him is the resurrection ˹of all˺
en.67:16: Do you feel secure that the One Who is in heaven will not cause the earth to swallow you up as it quakes violently
en.67:17: Or do you feel secure that the One Who is in heaven will not unleash upon you a storm of stones. Only then would you know how ˹serious˺ My warning was
en.67:18: And certainly those before them denied ˹as well˺, then how severe was My response
en.67:19: Have they not seen the birds above them, spreading and folding their wings? None holds them up except the Most Compassionate. Indeed, He is All-Seeing of everything
en.67:20: Also, which ˹powerless˺ force will come to your help instead of the Most Compassionate? Indeed, the disbelievers are only ˹lost˺ in delusion
en.67:21: Or who is it that will provide for you if He withholds His provision? In fact, they persist in arrogance and aversion ˹to the truth˺
en.67:22: Who is ˹rightly˺ guided: the one who crawls facedown or the one who walks upright on the Straight Path
en.67:23: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “He is the One Who brought you into being and gave you hearing, sight, and intellect. ˹Yet˺ you hardly give any thanks.”
en.67:24: ˹Also˺ say, “He is the One Who has dispersed you ˹all˺ over the earth, and to Him you will ˹all˺ be gathered.”
en.67:25: ˹Still˺ they ask ˹the believers˺, “When will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?”
en.67:26: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “That knowledge is with Allah alone, and I am only sent with a clear warning.”
en.67:27: Then when they see the torment drawing near, the faces of the disbelievers will become gloomy, and it will be said ˹to them˺, “This is what you claimed would never come.”
en.67:28: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Consider this: whether Allah causes me and those with me to die or shows us mercy, who will save the disbelievers from a painful punishment?”
en.67:29: Say, “He is the Most Compassionate—in Him ˹alone˺ we believe, and in Him ˹alone˺ we trust. You will soon know who is clearly astray.”
en.67:30: Say, “Consider this: if your water were to sink ˹into the earth˺, then who ˹else˺ could bring you flowing water?”
en.68:1: Nũn. By the pen and what everyone writes
en.68:2: By the grace of your Lord, you ˹O Prophet˺ are not insane
en.68:3: You will certainly have a never-ending reward
en.68:4: And you are truly ˹a man˺ of outstanding character
en.68:5: Soon you and the pagans will see
en.68:7: Surely your Lord ˹alone˺ knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ˹rightly˺ guided
en.68:8: So do not give in to the deniers
en.68:9: They wish you would compromise so they would yield ˹to you˺
en.68:10: And do not obey the despicable, vain oath-taker
en.68:13: brute, and—on top of all that—an illegitimate child
en.68:14: Now, ˹simply˺ because he has been blessed with ˹abundant˺ wealth and children
en.68:15: whenever Our revelations are recited to him, he says, “Ancient fables!”
en.68:16: We will soon mark his snout
en.68:17: Indeed, We have tested those ˹Meccans˺ as We tested the owners of the garden—when they swore they would surely harvest ˹all˺ its fruit in the early morning
en.68:18: leaving no thought for Allah’s Will
en.68:19: Then it was struck by a torment from your Lord while they slept
en.68:20: so it was reduced to ashes
en.68:21: Then by daybreak they called out to each other
en.68:22: ˹saying,˺ “Go early to your harvest, if you want to pick ˹all˺ the fruit.”
en.68:23: So they went off, whispering to one another
en.68:24: “Do not let any poor person enter your garden today.”
en.68:25: And they proceeded early, totally fixated on their purpose
en.68:26: But when they saw it ˹devastated˺, they cried, “We must have lost ˹our˺ way
en.68:27: In fact, we have been deprived ˹of our livelihood˺.”
en.68:28: The most sensible of them said, “Did I not urge you to say, ‘Allah willing.’?”
en.68:29: They replied, “Glory be to our Lord! We have truly been wrongdoers.”
en.68:30: Then they turned on each other, throwing blame
en.68:31: They said, “Woe to us! We have certainly been transgressors
en.68:32: We trust our Lord will give us a better garden than this, ˹for˺ we are indeed turning to our Lord with hope.”
en.68:33: That is the ˹way of Our˺ punishment ˹in this world˺. But the punishment of the Hereafter is certainly far worse, if only they knew
en.68:34: Indeed, the righteous will have the Gardens of Bliss with their Lord
en.68:35: Should We then treat those who have submitted like the wicked
en.68:36: What is the matter with you? How do you judge
en.68:37: Or do you have a scripture, in which you read
en.68:38: that you will have whatever you choose
en.68:39: Or do you have oaths binding on Us until the Day of Judgment that you will have whatever you decide
en.68:40: Ask them ˹O Prophet˺ which of them can guarantee all that
en.68:41: Or do they have associate-gods ˹supporting this claim˺? Then let them bring forth their associate-gods, if what they say is true
en.68:42: ˹Beware of˺ the Day the Shin ˹of Allah˺ will be bared, and the wicked will be asked to prostrate, but they will not be able to do so
en.68:43: with eyes downcast, totally covered with disgrace. For they were ˹always˺ called to prostrate ˹in the world˺ when they were fully capable ˹but they chose not to˺
en.68:44: So leave to Me ˹O Prophet˺ those who reject this message. We will gradually draw them to destruction in ways they cannot comprehend
en.68:45: I ˹only˺ delay their end for a while, but My planning is flawless
en.68:46: Or are you asking them for a reward ˹for the message˺ so that they are overburdened by debt
en.68:47: Or do they have access to ˹the Record in˺ the unseen, so they copy it ˹for all to see˺
en.68:48: So be patient with your Lord’s decree, and do not be like ˹Jonah,˺ the Man of the Whale, who cried out ˹to Allah˺, in total distress
en.68:49: Had he not been shown grace by his Lord, he would have certainly been cast onto the open ˹shore˺, still blameworthy
en.68:50: Then his Lord chose him, making him one of the righteous
en.68:51: The disbelievers would almost cut you down with their eyes when they hear ˹you recite˺ the Reminder, and say, “He is certainly a madman.”
en.68:52: But it is simply a reminder to the whole world
en.69:3: And what will make you realize what the Inevitable Hour is
en.69:4: ˹Both˺ Thamûd and ’Ȃd denied the Striking Disaster
en.69:5: As for Thamûd, they were destroyed by an overwhelming blast
en.69:6: And as for ’Ȃd, they were destroyed by a furious, bitter wind
en.69:7: which Allah unleashed on them non-stop for seven nights and eight days, so that you would have seen its people lying dead like trunks of uprooted palm trees
en.69:8: Do you see any of them left alive
en.69:9: Also, Pharaoh and those before him, and ˹the people of˺ the overturned cities ˹of Lot˺ indulged in sin
en.69:10: each disobeying their Lord’s messenger, so He seized them with a crushing grip
en.69:11: Indeed, when the floodwater had overflowed, We carried you in the floating Ark ˹with Noah˺
en.69:12: so that We may make this a reminder to you, and that attentive ears may grasp it
en.69:13: At last, when the Trumpet will be blown with one blast
en.69:14: and the earth and mountains will be lifted up and crushed with one blow
en.69:15: on that Day the Inevitable Event will have come to pass
en.69:16: The sky will then be so torn that it will be frail
en.69:17: with the angels on its sides. On that Day eight ˹mighty angels˺ will bear the Throne of your Lord above them
en.69:18: You will then be presented ˹before Him for judgment˺, and none of your secrets will stay hidden
en.69:19: As for those given their records in their right hand, they will cry ˹happily˺, “Here ˹everyone˺! Read my record
en.69:20: I surely knew I would face my reckoning.”
en.69:21: They will be in a life of bliss
en.69:23: whose fruit will hang within reach
en.69:24: ˹They will be told,˺ “Eat and drink joyfully for what you did in the days gone by.”
en.69:25: And as for those given their record in their left hand, they will cry ˹bitterly˺, “I wish I had not been given my record
en.69:26: nor known anything of my reckoning
en.69:27: I wish death was the end
en.69:28: My wealth has not benefited me
en.69:29: My authority has been stripped from me.”
en.69:30: ˹It will be said,˺ “Seize and shackle them
en.69:32: then tie them up with chains seventy arms long
en.69:33: For they never had faith in Allah, the Greatest
en.69:34: nor encouraged the feeding of the poor
en.69:35: So this Day they will have no close friend here
en.69:37: which none will eat except the evildoers.”
en.69:38: Now, I do swear by whatever you see
en.69:40: Indeed, this ˹Quran˺ is the recitation of a noble Messenger
en.69:41: It is not the prose of a poet ˹as you claim˺, ˹yet˺ you hardly have any faith
en.69:42: Nor is it the mumbling of a fortune-teller, ˹yet˺ you are hardly mindful
en.69:43: ˹It is˺ a revelation from the Lord of all worlds
en.69:44: Had the Messenger made up something in Our Name
en.69:45: We would have certainly seized him by his right hand
en.69:47: and none of you could have shielded him ˹from Us˺
en.69:48: Indeed, this ˹Quran˺ is a reminder to those mindful ˹of Allah˺
en.69:49: And We certainly know that some of you will persist in denial
en.69:50: and it will surely be a source of regret for the disbelievers
en.69:51: And indeed, this ˹Quran˺ is the absolute truth
en.69:52: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest
en.70:1: A challenger has demanded a punishment bound to come
en.70:2: for the disbelievers—to be averted by none—
en.70:3: from Allah, Lord of pathways of ˹heavenly˺ ascent
en.70:4: ˹through which˺ the angels and the ˹holy˺ spirit will ascend to Him on a Day fifty thousand years in length
en.70:5: So endure ˹this denial, O Prophet,˺ with beautiful patience
en.70:6: They truly see this ˹Day˺ as impossible
en.70:7: but We see it as inevitable
en.70:8: On that Day the sky will be like molten brass
en.70:9: and the mountains like ˹tufts of˺ wool
en.70:10: And no close friend will ask ˹about˺ their friends
en.70:11: ˹although˺ they will be made to see each other. The wicked will wish to ransom themselves from the punishment of that Day by their children
en.70:14: and everyone on earth altogether, just to save themselves
en.70:15: But no! There will certainly be a raging Flame
en.70:17: It will summon whoever turned their backs ˹on Allah˺ and turned away ˹from the truth˺
en.70:21: and withholding when touched with good—
en.70:24: and who give the rightful share of their wealth
en.70:25: to the beggar and the poor
en.70:26: and who ˹firmly˺ believe in the Day of Judgment
en.70:27: and those who fear the punishment of their Lord—
en.70:28: ˹knowing that˺ none should feel secure from their Lord’s punishment—
en.70:29: and those who guard their chastity
en.70:30: except with their wives or those ˹bondwomen˺ in their possession, for then they are free from blame
en.70:31: but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors
en.70:32: ˹The faithful are˺ also those who are true to their trusts and covenants
en.70:33: and who are honest in their testimony
en.70:34: and who are ˹properly˺ observant of their prayers
en.70:35: These will be in Gardens, held in honour
en.70:36: So what is the matter with the disbelievers that they rush ˹head-long˺ towards you ˹O Prophet˺
en.70:37: from the right and the left, in groups ˹to mock you˺
en.70:38: Does every one of them expect to be admitted into a Garden of Bliss
en.70:39: But no! Indeed, they ˹already˺ know what We created them from
en.70:40: So, I do swear by the Lord of ˹all˺ the points of sunrise and sunset that We are truly capable
en.70:41: of replacing them with ˹others˺ better than them, and We cannot be prevented ˹from doing so˺
en.70:42: So let them indulge ˹in falsehood˺ and amuse ˹themselves˺ until they face their Day, which they have been threatened with—
en.70:43: the Day they will come forth from the graves swiftly, as if racing to an idol ˹for a blessing˺
en.70:44: with eyes downcast, utterly covered with disgrace. That is the Day they have ˹always˺ been warned of
en.71:1: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people ˹saying to him˺, “Warn your people before a painful punishment comes to them.”
en.71:2: Noah proclaimed, “O my people! I am truly sent to you with a clear warning
en.71:3: worship Allah ˹alone˺, fear Him, and obey me
en.71:4: He will forgive your sins, and delay your end until the appointed time. Indeed, when the time set by Allah comes, it cannot be delayed, if only you knew!”
en.71:5: He cried, “My Lord! I have surely called my people day and night
en.71:6: but my calls only made them run farther away
en.71:7: And whenever I invite them to be forgiven by You, they press their fingers into their ears, cover themselves with their clothes, persist ˹in denial˺, and act very arrogantly
en.71:8: Then I certainly called them openly
en.71:9: then I surely preached to them publicly and privately
en.71:10: saying, ‘Seek your Lord’s forgiveness, ˹for˺ He is truly Most Forgiving
en.71:11: He will shower you with abundant rain
en.71:12: supply you with wealth and children, and give you gardens as well as rivers
en.71:13: What is the matter with you that you are not in awe of the Majesty of Allah
en.71:14: when He truly created you in stages ˹of development˺
en.71:15: Do you not see how Allah created seven heavens, one above the other
en.71:16: placing the moon within them as a ˹reflected˺ light, and the sun as a ˹radiant˺ lamp
en.71:17: Allah ˹alone˺ caused you to grow from the earth like a plant
en.71:18: Then He will return you to it, and then simply bring you forth ˹again˺
en.71:19: And Allah ˹alone˺ spread out the earth for you
en.71:20: to walk along its spacious pathways.’”
en.71:21: ˹Eventually,˺ Noah cried, “My Lord! They have certainly persisted in disobeying me, and followed ˹instead˺ those ˹elite˺ whose ˹abundant˺ wealth and children only increase them in loss
en.71:22: and who have devised a tremendous plot
en.71:23: urging ˹their followers˺, ‘Do not abandon your idols—especially Wadd, Suwâ’, Yaghûth, Ya’ûq, and Nasr.’
en.71:24: Those ˹elite˺ have already led many astray. So ˹O Lord˺, only allow the wrongdoers to stray farther away.”
en.71:25: So because of their sins, they were drowned, then admitted into the Fire. And they found none to help them against Allah
en.71:26: Noah had prayed, “My Lord! Do not leave a single disbeliever on earth
en.71:27: For if You spare ˹any of˺ them, they will certainly mislead Your servants, and give birth only to ˹wicked˺ sinners, staunch disbelievers
en.71:28: My Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and whoever enters my house in faith, and ˹all˺ believing men and women. And increase the wrongdoers only in destruction.”
en.72:1: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “It has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened ˹to the Quran,˺ and said ˹to their fellow jinn˺: ‘Indeed, we have heard a wondrous recitation
en.72:2: It leads to Right Guidance so we believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord ˹in worship˺
en.72:3: ˹Now, we believe that˺ our Lord—Exalted is His Majesty—has neither taken a mate nor offspring
en.72:4: and that the foolish of us used to utter ˹outrageous˺ falsehoods about Allah
en.72:5: We certainly thought that humans and jinn would never speak lies about Allah
en.72:6: And some men used to seek refuge with some jinn—so they increased each other in wickedness
en.72:7: And those ˹humans˺ thought, just like you ˹jinn˺, that Allah would not resurrect anyone ˹for judgment˺
en.72:8: ˹Earlier˺ we tried to reach heaven ˹for news˺, only to find it filled with stern guards and shooting stars
en.72:9: We used to take up positions there for eavesdropping, but whoever dares eavesdrop now will find a flare lying in wait for them
en.72:10: Now, we have no clue whether evil is intended for those on earth, or their Lord intends for them what is right
en.72:11: Among us are those who are righteous and those who are less so. We have been of different factions
en.72:12: ˹Now,˺ we truly know that we cannot frustrate Allah on earth, nor can we escape from Him ˹into heaven˺
en.72:13: When we heard the guidance ˹of the Quran˺, we ˹readily˺ believed in it. For whoever believes in their Lord will have no fear of being denied ˹a reward˺ or wronged
en.72:14: And among us are those who have submitted ˹to Allah˺ and those who are deviant. So ˹as for˺ those who submitted, it is they who have attained Right Guidance
en.72:15: And as for the deviant, they will be fuel for Hell.’”
en.72:16: Had the deniers followed the Right Way, We would have certainly granted them abundant rain to drink—
en.72:17: as a test for them. And whoever turns away from the remembrance of their Lord will be admitted by Him into an overwhelming punishment
en.72:18: The places of worship are ˹only˺ for Allah, so do not invoke anyone besides Him
en.72:19: Yet when the servant of Allah stood up calling upon Him ˹alone˺, the pagans almost swarmed over him
en.72:20: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I call only upon my Lord, associating none with Him ˹in worship˺.”
en.72:21: Say, “It is not in my power to harm or benefit you.”
en.72:22: Say, “No one can protect me from Allah ˹if I were to disobey Him˺, nor can I find any refuge other than Him
en.72:23: ˹My duty is˺ only to convey ˹the truth˺ from Allah and ˹deliver˺ His messages.” And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger will certainly be in the Fire of Hell, to stay there for ever and ever
en.72:24: Only when they see what they have been threatened with will they know who is weaker in helpers and inferior in manpower
en.72:25: Say, “I do not know if what you are promised is near or my Lord has set a distant time for it
en.72:26: ˹He is the˺ Knower of the unseen, disclosing none of it to anyone
en.72:27: except messengers of His choice. Then He appoints angel-guards before and behind them
en.72:28: to ensure that the messengers fully deliver the messages of their Lord—though He ˹already˺ knows all about them, and keeps account of everything.”
en.73:2: Stand all night ˹in prayer˺ except a little—
en.73:3: ˹pray˺ half the night, or a little less
en.73:4: or a little more—and recite the Quran ˹properly˺ in a measured way
en.73:5: ˹For˺ We will soon send upon you a weighty revelation
en.73:6: Indeed, worship in the night is more impactful and suitable for recitation
en.73:7: For during the day you are over-occupied ˹with worldly duties˺
en.73:8: ˹Always˺ remember the Name of your Lord, and devote yourself to Him wholeheartedly
en.73:9: ˹He is the˺ Lord of the east and the west. There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him, so take Him ˹alone˺ as a Trustee of Affairs
en.73:10: Be patient ˹O Prophet˺ with what they say, and depart from them courteously
en.73:11: And leave to Me the deniers—the people of luxury—and bear with them for a little while
en.73:12: ˹For˺ We certainly have shackles, a ˹raging˺ Fire
en.73:13: choking food, and a painful punishment ˹in store for them˺
en.73:14: on the Day the earth and mountains will shake ˹violently˺, and mountains will be ˹reduced to˺ dunes of shifting sand
en.73:15: Indeed, We have sent to you a messenger as a witness over you, just as We sent a messenger to Pharaoh
en.73:16: But Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a stern grip
en.73:17: If you ˹pagans˺ persist in disbelief, then how will you guard yourselves against ˹the horrors of˺ a Day which will turn children’s hair grey
en.73:18: It will ˹even˺ cause the sky to split apart. His promise ˹of judgment˺ must be fulfilled
en.73:19: Surely this is a reminder. So let whoever wills take the ˹Right˺ Way to their Lord
en.73:20: Surely your Lord knows that you ˹O Prophet˺ stand ˹in prayer˺ for nearly two-thirds of the night, or ˹sometimes˺ half of it, or a third, as do some of those with you. Allah ˹alone˺ keeps a ˹precise˺ measure of the day and night. He knows that you ˹believers˺ are unable to endure this, and has turned to you in mercy. So recite ˹in prayer˺ whatever you can from the Quran. He knows that some of you will be sick, some will be travelling throughout the land seeking Allah’s bounty, and some fighting in the cause of Allah. So recite whatever you can from it. And ˹continue to˺ perform ˹regular˺ prayers, pay alms-tax, and lend to Allah a good loan. Whatever good you send forth for yourselves, you will find it with Allah far better and more rewarding. And seek Allah’s forgiveness. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful

en.75:1: I do swear by the Day of Judgment
en.75:2: And I do swear by the self-reproaching soul
en.75:3: Do people think We cannot reassemble their bones
en.75:4: Yes ˹indeed˺! We are ˹most˺ capable of restoring ˹even˺ their very fingertips
en.75:5: Still people want to deny what is yet to come
en.75:6: asking ˹mockingly˺, “When is this Day of Judgment?”
en.75:7: But when the sight is stunned
en.75:9: and the sun and the moon are brought together
en.75:10: on that Day one will cry, “Where is the escape?”
en.75:11: But no! There will be no refuge
en.75:12: On that Day all will end up before your Lord
en.75:13: All will then be informed of what they have sent forth and left behind

en.75:15: despite the excuses they come up with
en.75:16: Do not rush your tongue trying to memorize ˹a revelation of˺ the Quran

en.75:25: anticipating something devastating to befall them

en.75:31: This denier neither believed nor prayed
en.75:32: but persisted in denial and turned away
en.75:33: then went to their own people, walking boastfully
en.75:34: Woe to you, and more woe
en.75:35: Again, woe to you, and even more woe

en.76:1: Is there not a period of time when each human is nothing yet worth mentioning
en.76:2: ˹For˺ indeed, We ˹alone˺ created humans from a drop of mixed fluids, ˹in order˺ to test them, so We made them hear and see
en.76:3: We already showed them the Way, whether they ˹choose to˺ be grateful or ungrateful
en.76:4: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains, shackles, and a blazing Fire
en.76:5: Indeed, the virtuous will have a drink ˹of pure wine˺—flavoured with camphor—
en.76:6: ˹from˺ a spring where Allah’s servants will drink, flowing at their will
en.76:7: They ˹are those who˺ fulfil ˹their˺ vows and fear a Day of sweeping horror
en.76:8: and give food—despite their desire for it—to the poor, the orphan, and the captive
en.76:9: ˹saying to themselves,˺ “We feed you only for the sake of Allah, seeking neither reward nor thanks from you
en.76:10: We fear from our Lord a horribly distressful Day.”
en.76:11: So Allah will deliver them from the horror of that Day, and grant them radiance and joy
en.76:12: and reward them for their perseverance with a Garden ˹in Paradise˺ and ˹garments of˺ silk
en.76:13: There they will be reclining on ˹canopied˺ couches, never seeing scorching heat or bitter cold
en.76:14: The Garden’s shade will be right above them, and its fruit will be made very easy to reach
en.76:15: They will be waited on with silver vessels and cups of crystal—
en.76:16: crystalline silver, filled precisely as desired
en.76:17: And they will be given a drink ˹of pure wine˺ flavoured with ginger
en.76:18: from a spring there, called Salsabîl
en.76:19: They will be waited on by eternal youths. If you saw them, you would think they were scattered pearls
en.76:20: And if you looked around, you would see ˹indescribable˺ bliss and a vast kingdom
en.76:21: The virtuous will be ˹dressed˺ in garments of fine green silk and rich brocade, and adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a purifying drink
en.76:22: ˹And they will be told,˺ “All this is surely a reward for you. Your striving has been appreciated.”
en.76:23: Indeed, it is We Who have revealed the Quran to you ˹O Prophet˺ in stages
en.76:24: So be patient with your Lord’s decree, and do not yield to any evildoer or ˹staunch˺ disbeliever from among them
en.76:25: ˹Always˺ remember the Name of your Lord morning and evening
en.76:26: and prostrate before Him during part of the night, and glorify Him long at night
en.76:27: Surely those ˹pagans˺ love this fleeting world, ˹totally˺ neglecting a weighty Day ahead of them
en.76:28: It is We Who created them and perfected their ˹physical˺ form. But if We will, We can easily replace them with others
en.76:29: Surely this is a reminder. So let whoever wills take the ˹Right˺ Way to their Lord
en.76:30: But you cannot will ˹to do so˺ unless Allah wills. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise
en.76:31: He admits whoever He wills into His mercy. As for the wrongdoers, He has prepared for them a painful punishment

en.100:5: and penetrating into the heart of enemy lines
en.100:6: Surely humankind is ungrateful to their Lord—
en.100:7: and they certainly attest to this—
en.100:8: and they are truly extreme in their love of ˹worldly˺ gains
en.100:9: Do they not know that when the contents of the graves will be spilled out
en.100:10: and the secrets of the hearts will be laid bare—
en.100:11: surely their Lord is All-Aware of them on that Day
en.101:3: And what will make you realize what the Striking Disaster is
en.101:4: ˹It is˺ the Day people will be like scattered moths
en.101:5: and the mountains will be like carded wool
en.101:6: So as for those whose scale is heavy ˹with good deeds˺
en.101:7: they will be in a life of bliss
en.101:8: And as for those whose scale is light
en.101:9: their home will be the abyss
en.101:10: And what will make you realize what that is
en.102:1: Competition for more ˹gains˺ diverts you ˹from Allah˺
en.102:2: until you end up in ˹your˺ graves
en.102:3: But no! You will soon come to know
en.102:4: Again, no! You will soon come to know
en.102:5: Indeed, if you were to know ˹your fate˺ with certainty, ˹you would have acted differently˺
en.102:6: ˹But˺ you will surely see the Hellfire
en.102:7: Again, you will surely see it with the eye of certainty
en.102:8: Then, on that Day, you will definitely be questioned about ˹your worldly˺ pleasures
en.103:3: except those who have faith, do good, and urge each other to the truth, and urge each other to perseverance
en.104:2: who amasses wealth ˹greedily˺ and counts it ˹repeatedly˺
en.104:3: thinking that their wealth will make them immortal
en.104:4: Not at all! Such a person will certainly be tossed into the Crusher
en.104:5: And what will make you realize what the Crusher is
en.104:8: It will be sealed over them
en.105:1: Have you not seen ˹O Prophet˺ how your Lord dealt with the Army of the Elephant
en.105:2: Did He not frustrate their scheme
en.105:3: For He sent against them flocks of birds
en.105:4: that pelted them with stones of baked clay
en.105:5: leaving them like chewed up straw
en.106:1: ˹At least˺ for ˹the favour of˺ making Quraysh habitually secure—
en.106:2: secure in their trading caravan ˹to Yemen˺ in the winter and ˹Syria˺ in the summer—
en.106:3: let them worship the Lord of this ˹Sacred˺ House
en.106:4: Who has fed them against hunger and made them secure against fear
en.107:1: Have you seen the one who denies the ˹final˺ Judgment
en.107:2: That is the one who repulses the orphan
en.107:3: and does not encourage the feeding of the poor
en.107:4: So woe to those ˹hypocrites˺ who pray
en.107:5: yet are unmindful of their prayers
en.107:7: and refuse to give ˹even the simplest˺ aid
en.108:1: Indeed, We have granted you ˹O Prophet˺ abundant goodness
en.108:2: So pray and sacrifice to your Lord ˹alone˺
en.108:3: Only the one who hates you is truly cut off ˹from any goodness˺
en.109:2: I do not worship what you worship
en.109:3: nor do you worship what I worship
en.109:4: I will never worship what you worship
en.109:5: nor will you ever worship what I worship
en.109:6: You have your way, and I have my Way.”
en.110:1: When Allah’s ˹ultimate˺ help comes and the victory ˹over Mecca is achieved˺
en.110:2: and you ˹O Prophet˺ see the people embracing Allah’s Way in crowds
en.110:3: then glorify the praises of your Lord and seek His forgiveness, for certainly He is ever Accepting of Repentance
en.111:1: May the hands of Abu Lahab perish, and he ˹himself˺ perish
en.111:2: Neither his wealth nor ˹worldly˺ gains will benefit him
en.111:3: He will burn in a flaming Fire
en.111:4: and ˹so will˺ his wife, the carrier of ˹thorny˺ kindling
en.111:5: around her neck will be a rope of palm-fibre
en.112:3: He has never had offspring, nor was He born
en.112:4: And there is none comparable to Him.”
en.113:1: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I seek refuge in the Lord of the daybreak
en.113:2: from the evil of whatever He has created
en.113:3: and from the evil of the night when it grows dark
en.113:4: and from the evil of those ˹witches casting spells by˺ blowing onto knots
en.113:5: and from the evil of an envier when they envy.”
en.114:1: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I seek refuge in the Lord of humankind
en.114:4: from the evil of the lurking whisperer—
en.114:5: who whispers into the hearts of humankind—
